```text
  schema failures fall
  agent success improves or cost drops at same success
```

### Protocol 8: Artifact Economics Benchmark

Question:

Does a compiled artifact produce measurable economic value?

Controls:

- current raw API workflow
- gateway-cached workflow
- fine-tuned provider model
- local open model

Treatment:

- `.kolm` artifact with K-Score and runtime profile.

Metrics:

- monthly cost saved
- cloud calls avoided
- latency saved
- human review avoided
- support tickets avoided
- artifact maintenance cost
- break-even time
- gross margin per successful task

Promotion:

```text
promote if:
  artifact clears quality floor
  break-even is inside target window
  maintenance cost does not erase savings
  proof is exportable to buyer or auditor
```

## Expanded Invention Backlog

The following backlog extends beyond the initial named inventions. These are not claims of shipped functionality. They are implementation-grade invention targets.

| ID | Invention | Core breakthrough | Primary metric | First build |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| W700 | Behavior Fingerprint Engine | Task vectors drive every build decision | quality/cost | cluster captured calls and emit fingerprint JSON |
| W701 | Student Genome Search | Bayesian Pareto search over student architectures | quality/latency | offline candidate planner |
| W702 | Teacher Council | Weighted multi-teacher distillation by reliability | quality | teacher reliability table plus verifier agreement |
| W703 | Route Curriculum Miner | Escalations become targeted distillation data | cost/quality | failure cluster queue |
| W704 | Typed K-Score Cards | Score by decision context, not scalar average | proof | K_task/K_safe/K_runtime reports |
| W705 | K-Score Causal Audit | Attribute improvements to data/model/runtime/route | proof/DX | paired ablation runner |
| W706 | Proof-Carrying Runtime Profile | Runtime optimizations ship with quality evidence | latency/proof | runtime_profile proof packet |
| W707 | Behavior-Aware KolmIR | Lower behavior constraints, not just ops | portability | target legality graph |
| W708 | Distilled Quantization Map | Quantization conditioned on distilled distributions | cost/latency | protected channel map |
| W709 | Attention Pattern Contracts | Sparse attention with explicit safety/fallback scope | latency/proof | attention_contract JSON |
| W710 | Route-Aware Dynamic Depth | Early exit governed by selective risk | latency/quality | exit policy with route fallback |
| W711 | K-Score Data Economy | Teacher and label spend by expected K-Score lift | cost | value-of-information queue |
| W712 | Red-Team Compiler | OWASP/PyRIT/garak plans generated per artifact | security | artifact risk-to-test mapping |
| W713 | RAG Evidence Compiler | Source spans become artifact proof objects | proof/quality | evidence sidecar schema |
| W714 | Agent Tool Receipt | Every MCP tool call emits signed replay proof | security/DX | MCP receipt wrapper |
| W715 | Runtime Drift Sentinel | Disable profiles when live drift exceeds envelope | quality/security | online drift monitor |
| W716 | Artifact Economics Engine | ROI per artifact with score caveats | conversion | cost-per-success report |
| W717 | Privacy Rights Automaton | Data rows have machine-enforced train/eval rights | enterprise/security | row_rights state machine |
| W718 | Prompt-Retrieve-Distill Optimizer | Optimize prompt, retrieval, and student jointly | quality/cost | GEPA/TextGrad-style prompt lane plus distill lane |
| W719 | Synthetic Failure Simulator | Generate hard cases from abstracted failures | quality/security | synthetic derivative generator |
| W720 | Target Conformance Lab | Prove runtime equivalence across device targets | portability | target conformance suite |
| W721 | Governance Receipt Graph | Map every claim to audit/billing/policy evidence | enterprise/proof | evidence DAG export |
| W722 | Registry Trust Rank | Rank artifacts by task fit, proof, and safety | conversion/security | registry scoring function |
| W723 | Live Calibration Decay Model | K-Score and route confidence decay with drift | quality/proof | freshness score and block rules |

Priority order:

```text
P0:
  W700, W704, W706, W711, W717

P1:
  W701, W702, W703, W707, W708, W712, W715

P2:
  W709, W710, W713, W714, W716, W718, W719, W720, W721, W722, W723
```

The reason for this order is simple: first build measurement and rights, then build planners and optimizers, then build marketplace and advanced runtime features.

## W700-W723 Build Cards

These cards turn the backlog into implementation contracts. They are deliberately compact so they can be copied into tickets without losing the research intent.

### W700: Behavior Fingerprint Engine

Objective: turn captured traffic into a stable task vector that drives every build decision.

Inputs:

- capture events
- schemas
- outputs
- user corrections
- latency/cost telemetry
- privacy classes

Outputs:

- `kolm.behavior_fingerprint.v1`
- compile candidate score
- recommended lane
- blocked lanes with reasons

First implementation:

- cluster events by prompt/output/schema similarity
- infer task type, schema entropy, retrieval dependency, safety sensitivity, and economics
- emit deterministic JSON with hashes

Validation:

- fingerprint is stable under bootstrap resampling
- fingerprint predicts which lane wins in historical build records
- no private text is included in the fingerprint

Failure mode:

- noisy clusters lead to wrong training decisions; mitigation is human review for low-confidence fingerprints.

### W701: Student Genome Search

Objective: select the best student architecture and training recipe under quality, latency, memory, privacy, and runtime constraints.

Inputs:

- behavior fingerprint
- target runtimes
- model registry
- historical distillation outcomes
- K-Score thresholds

Outputs:

- `kolm.student_genome.v1`
- Pareto frontier
- selected genome receipt
- rejected candidate reasons

First implementation:

- static candidate library
- target legality checks
- simple predictor from parameter count, context, quantization, and target runtime
- probe distillation for top candidates

Validation:

- beats fixed-student baseline on cost per K-Score point
- selected candidate clears lower-bound K-Score
- every reject reason is machine-readable

Failure mode:

- predictor overfits past tasks; mitigation is forced exploration and out-of-domain holdout probes.

### W702: Teacher Council

Objective: use multiple teachers without averaging bad or policy-disallowed signals.

Inputs:

- allowed teacher providers
- teacher outputs
- verifier results
- human preference labels
- provider cost and policy risk

Outputs:

- `kolm.teacher_council.v1`
- teacher reliability table
- disagreement queue
- weighted distillation targets

First implementation:

- start with two teachers plus one deterministic verifier
- compute reliability by task/domain
- block high-disagreement samples unless human-labeled

Validation:

- council improves K-Score or reduces critical failures against single-teacher baseline
- unresolved disagreement does not enter train data

Failure mode:

- expensive teacher dominates despite weak domain fit; mitigation is reliability-weighted and cost-penalized weighting.

### W703: Route Curriculum Miner

Objective: convert local failures and cloud escalations into targeted curriculum.

Inputs:

- route decisions
- local outputs
- teacher outputs
- verifier results
- human corrections
- privacy rights

Outputs:

- failure clusters
- train candidates
- holdout-only candidates
- synthetic hard-case requests

First implementation:

- classify failures into schema, faithfulness, safety, retrieval, route, and tool categories
- rank by expected K-Score lift and frequency

Validation:

- targeted curriculum improves K-Score more than random sampled rows
- data rights are preserved

Failure mode:

- curriculum overfocuses on frequent easy failures; mitigation is severity and domain coverage weighting.

### W704: Typed K-Score Cards

Objective: replace scalar-only quality with decision-grade typed scorecards.

Inputs:

- eval dataset
- teacher outputs
- student outputs
- deterministic checks
- judges
- human calibration

Outputs:

- `kolm.kscore_report.v1`
- K_task, K_faith, K_schema, K_safe, K_route, K_runtime, K_port, K_audit
- confidence intervals

First implementation:

- score three task classes: extraction, RAG, and code
- require typed scores based on task class

Validation:

- typed scores correlate with human labels by stratum
- missing required typed score blocks decision-grade label

Failure mode:

- headline score hides weak dimension; mitigation is lower-bound gate for every required type.

### W705: K-Score Causal Audit

Objective: attribute score changes to data, model, runtime, route, retrieval, or interaction terms.

Inputs:

- baseline artifact
- candidate artifact
- paired eval set
- route policy
- runtime profile
- retrieval policy

Outputs:

- causal delta report
- ablation matrix
- promotion recommendation

First implementation:

- one-factor-at-a-time ablations for runtime, route, and student changes
- bootstrap intervals by task stratum

Validation:

- release notes can say what caused the improvement
- unexplainable high-risk improvements require shadow deployment

Failure mode:

- interaction effects are missed; mitigation is factorial tests for high-risk releases.

### W706: Proof-Carrying Runtime Profile

Objective: make runtime optimizations deployable only with behavior-preservation evidence.

Inputs:

- artifact
- runtime target
- calibration set
- baseline dense runtime
- optimized profile candidates

Outputs:

- `kolm.runtime_profile.v1`
- runtime proof packet
- fallback and rollback profile

First implementation:

- compare BF16 dense against one quantized profile
- report K_runtime, K_safe, latency, memory, fallback rate

Validation:

- optimization is blocked if K_runtime lower bound misses floor
- rollback path exists and is tested

Failure mode:

- speed win hides faithfulness loss; mitigation is typed K_runtime and K_faith gates.

### W707: Behavior-Aware KolmIR

Objective: lower behavior constraints into target runtimes, not just model operations.

Inputs:

- artifact graph
- schema contract
- route envelope
- runtime profile
- target runtime capabilities

Outputs:

- KolmIR node graph
- target legality report
- blocked-op reasons
- lowering plan

First implementation:

- implement nodes for model, tokenizer, schema, route, runtime profile, proof, fallback
- lower to one server runtime and one local/runtime target

Validation:

- impossible target returns blocked reason
- legal target includes proof bindings

Failure mode:

- op lowering succeeds but behavior guarantee fails; mitigation is behavior gates in legality checks.

### W708: Distilled Quantization Map

Objective: quantize by task sensitivity and distilled activation behavior.

Inputs:

- activation histograms
- teacher-student KL
- outlier channel maps
- K-Score holdout
- runtime target

Outputs:

- protected channel map
- layer precision map
- KV precision policy
- quantization proof

First implementation:

- protect persistent outlier channels
- compare against generic AWQ/GPTQ/SmoothQuant baseline

Validation:

- improves quality per byte
- no critical fail increase

Failure mode:

- quantization works in-domain but breaks out-of-domain; mitigation is route-risk and drift fallback.

### W709: Attention Pattern Contracts

Objective: make sparse attention auditable and bounded by task scope.

Inputs:

- attention maps
- task clusters
- source span labels
- K_faith holdout
- dense baseline

Outputs:

- attention contract JSON
- protected token classes
- dense fallback rules

First implementation:

- define contracts for one RAG task and one extraction task
- compare sparse vs dense logit and citation deltas

Validation:

- K_faith and citation precision do not regress
- dense fallback triggers for protected spans

Failure mode:

- sparse attention misses evidence in middle context; mitigation is protected source-span routing.

### W710: Route-Aware Dynamic Depth

Objective: use early exit only when route risk says it is safe.

Inputs:

- exit-layer logits
- entropy
- margin
- route risk
- teacher-student KL proxy

Outputs:

- exit policy
- rollback stats
- accepted-token report

First implementation:

- self-speculative path with verify-then-commit
- disable for regulated tasks until calibrated

Validation:

- p95 latency improves with no K_safe regression
- rollback rate stays inside budget

Failure mode:

- early exit commits a wrong but confident answer; mitigation is final-layer verification for medium/high risk.

### W711: K-Score Data Economy

Objective: allocate teacher calls and human labels by expected K-Score lift.

Inputs:

- uncertainty
- failure severity
- task frequency
- label cost
- teacher cost
- privacy risk

Outputs:

- value-of-information queue
- label plan
- teacher-call plan
- synthetic-case plan

First implementation:

- rank rows with a deterministic expected-value heuristic
- compare against FIFO/random labeling

Validation:

- higher K-Score lift per labeled row
- lower teacher spend per promotion

Failure mode:

- rare severe cases are underweighted; mitigation is severity and regulated-domain floors.

### W712: Red-Team Compiler

Objective: generate artifact-specific security tests from OWASP, PyRIT, and garak-style probes.

Inputs:

- artifact tool schema
- deployment mode
- data classes
- route policy
- runtime profile

Outputs:

- red-team plan
- replayable attack traces
- mitigation mapping
- security promotion gate

First implementation:

- map artifacts to OWASP LLM risks
- run prompt injection, data leakage, schema bypass, and tool misuse probes

Validation:

- high-severity failures block promotion
- every patched failure becomes a regression test

Failure mode:

- generic probes miss domain-specific risk; mitigation is customer policy and domain attack templates.

### W713: RAG Evidence Compiler

Objective: bind retrieval source spans and citations into artifact proof.

Inputs:

- documents
- retrieved chunks
- layout metadata
- citation labels
- generated answers

Outputs:

- evidence sidecar
- source span map
- K_faith report
- retrieval policy receipt

First implementation:

- support PDFs/text docs with source spans and chunk hashes
- evaluate context precision, recall, and faithfulness

Validation:

- citation precision improves or unsupported claims are blocked
- source spans survive artifact export

Failure mode:

- extracted layout corrupts source mapping; mitigation is OCR/layout confidence gates.

### W714: Agent Tool Receipt

Objective: make every agent tool call signed, scoped, and replayable.

Inputs:

- MCP tool schema
- artifact hash
- permissions
- input/output hashes
- route decision

Outputs:

- agent tool receipt
- replay log
- permission proof
- failure feedback event

First implementation:

- wrap MCP calls with hash and permission metadata
- emit a receipt for every call

Validation:

- invalid arguments and unsafe tool calls decrease
- replay log can reproduce the decision path

Failure mode:

- receipts leak sensitive payloads; mitigation is hash-only private payload references.

### W715: Runtime Drift Sentinel

Objective: disable runtime profiles when live behavior exits the calibrated envelope.

Inputs:

- live route receipts
- K-Score samples
- latency/memory telemetry
- prompt cluster drift
- error reports

Outputs:

- drift score
- profile freshness score
- automatic fallback decision

First implementation:

- monitor cluster distribution and runtime error deltas
- degrade to dense or teacher fallback when thresholds trip

Validation:

- drift cases are caught before quality breach in replay tests
- false positive fallback rate is acceptable

Failure mode:

- sentinel is too conservative and erases savings; mitigation is per-stratum thresholds.

### W716: Artifact Economics Engine

Objective: prove whether an artifact is worth maintaining.

Inputs:

- baseline spend
- artifact serving cost
- teacher deferral cost
- human review cost
- maintenance cost
- K-Score status

Outputs:

- cost per success report
- break-even estimate
- ROI with caveats
- buyer-facing economics card

First implementation:

- calculate raw API vs `.kolm` artifact economics for one workflow

Validation:

- savings claims include quality and confidence caveats
- maintenance cost is not omitted

Failure mode:

- ROI ignores quality regressions; mitigation is cost per successful task, not cost per request.

### W717: Privacy Rights Automaton

Objective: enforce row-level train/eval/export rights through the whole system.

Inputs:

- capture rows
- consent/retention settings
- policy
- PII scan
- tenant boundary

Outputs:

- row rights state
- train/eval eligibility
- governance receipt fields
- blocked operation reason

First implementation:

- implement rights states: trainable, holdout_only, tenant_local_only, synthetic_derivative_allowed, never_train

Validation:

- forbidden rows cannot enter training plans
- rights transitions are audited

Failure mode:

- derived synthetic data leaks original content; mitigation is abstraction and similarity scans.

### W718: Prompt-Retrieve-Distill Optimizer

Objective: optimize prompt, retrieval, and student training jointly.

Inputs:

- task metric
- prompts
- retrieval policy
- candidate student genomes
- teacher feedback

Outputs:

- optimized prompt/retrieval/student plan
- Pareto comparison
- no-train or train decision

First implementation:

- GEPA/TextGrad-style prompt optimization lane
- retrieval parameter search
- compare against distillation probe

Validation:

- planner chooses prompt/retrieval when they beat training
- distillation only runs when it wins the utility function

Failure mode:

- prompt optimization overfits public eval; mitigation is private holdout and drift checks.

### W719: Synthetic Failure Simulator

Objective: generate hard cases from abstracted real failures without leaking private data.

Inputs:

- failure taxonomy
- abstracted failure packets
- domain constraints
- data rights
- verifier templates

Outputs:

- synthetic hard-case dataset
- similarity/leakage report
- expected K-Score lift estimate

First implementation:

- generate variants for schema errors, retrieval misses, and policy conflicts

Validation:

- synthetic cases improve robustness on real holdout
- leakage scan passes

Failure mode:

- synthetic data teaches unrealistic patterns; mitigation is real-holdout validation.

### W720: Target Conformance Lab

Objective: prove behavior equivalence across target runtimes and devices.

Inputs:

- artifact
- target runtimes
- device profiles
- K-Score holdout
- runtime profile

Outputs:

- target conformance matrix
- K_port report
- blocked target reasons

First implementation:

- compare server baseline vs browser or local target
- record numerical and behavioral deltas

Validation:

- target claims require K_port lower-bound pass
- unsupported target is blocked with reason

Failure mode:

- target passes smoke but fails edge cases; mitigation is task-stratified conformance.

### W721: Governance Receipt Graph

Objective: make claim, audit, billing, and policy evidence navigable.

Inputs:

- artifact receipts
- governance receipts
- audit logs
- billing events
- policy decisions

Outputs:

- evidence DAG
- enterprise export
- claim-to-proof lookup

First implementation:

- build local graph from hashes and receipt IDs
- expose export JSON for one tenant

Validation:

- every public/account/CLI claim resolves to proof or blocked reason
- private evidence stays hash-referenced unless tenant-authorized

Failure mode:

- graph becomes incomplete after deletion/retention; mitigation is tombstone receipts.

### W722: Registry Trust Rank

Objective: rank artifacts by fit, proof, safety, portability, and economics.

Inputs:

- artifact cards
- K-Score reports
- runtime conformance
- security results
- usage outcomes

Outputs:

- trust rank
- task fit score
- proof completeness score
- publication block reasons

First implementation:

- score registry candidates with weighted proof completeness and K-Score

Validation:

- artifacts without proof cannot outrank proven artifacts
- task mismatch is visible

Failure mode:

- rank becomes popularity contest; mitigation is proof-first ranking and task filters.

### W723: Live Calibration Decay Model

Objective: degrade confidence when data, providers, retrieval corpora, or traffic drift.

Inputs:

- calibration date
- traffic drift
- teacher version drift
- retrieval corpus drift
- runtime drift
- human feedback

Outputs:

- freshness score
- recalibration required flag
- route fallback decision

First implementation:

- simple freshness model with hard expirations and drift thresholds

Validation:

- stale K-Score or route envelope blocks promotion
- recalibration restores decision-grade status

Failure mode:

- decay is ignored by product copy; mitigation is claim-scope gate tied to freshness score.

## W800-W831 Second-Wave Breakthrough Map

The W700 backlog makes Kolm buildable. The W800 wave makes Kolm hard to catch. These inventions use interpretability, preference optimization, constitutional policy, private learning, and speculative serving as raw material, but translate them into Kolm-specific artifact primitives.

| ID | Invention | Breakthrough | First artifact hook | Proof gate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| W800 | Feature Receipt Distillation | bind sparse-autoencoder feature evidence to student behavior | `.kolm/features/receipt.json` | feature intervention changes predicted failure slice |
| W801 | Mechanistic Regression Lock | detect when a student loses critical internal features | `kolm features diff` | red-flag feature loss blocks promotion |
| W802 | Preference Delta Compiler | convert preference pairs into typed K-Score deltas | `preference_delta.json` | DPO-style update improves target axis without safety regression |
| W803 | Constitutional Artifact Policy | compile enterprise principles into route and refusal policy | `constitution.json` | policy conflict creates review/block, not silent answer |
| W804 | Interpretability-Guided Quantization | preserve feature-sensitive channels at higher precision | `feature_precision_map.json` | fragile features survive quantization |
| W805 | Feature-Level Teacher Council | choose teachers by latent feature failure, not only task | `teacher_feature_map.json` | council resolves feature-specific failure cluster |
| W806 | Counterfactual K-Score | score whether output changes for the right causal reason | `counterfactual_scorecard.json` | spurious shortcut detected on intervention set |
| W807 | Preference-Aware Route Envelope | route decisions include calibrated human preference loss | `route_preference_budget.json` | local route rejected when preference loss exceeds budget |
| W808 | Proof-Carrying Prompt Evolution | every textual optimization carries before/after proof | `prompt_evolution_receipt.json` | prompt change rejected without K-Score lift |
| W809 | Feature-Gated Early Exit | early exit allowed only when protected features have stabilized | `feature_exit_policy.json` | high-risk exits disabled when features unstable |
| W810 | Feature Drift Sentinel | live activation features detect distribution shift before failures spike | `feature_drift_report.json` | drift predicts holdout degradation |
| W811 | Rights-Aware Preference Learning | preference pairs inherit rights and deletion constraints | `preference_rights_receipt.json` | deleted preference invalidates derived update |
| W812 | Private Teacher Aggregator | PATE-like teacher voting for sensitive tenant tasks | `private_teacher_vote.json` | privacy budget and utility reported together |
| W813 | Secure Aggregate Failure Atlas | cross-tenant failure signatures learned without raw failures | `aggregate_failure_atlas.json` | aggregate signal passes re-identification probe |
| W814 | Federated Device Calibration | device fleets calibrate runtime profiles without raw data upload | `device_calibration_round.json` | secure aggregate improves target profile |
| W815 | Semantic KV Compression | compress KV entries by feature and evidence value | `semantic_kv_tiers.json` | evidence-critical KV preserved |
| W816 | Speculative Feature Verifier | verify draft tokens by task features before teacher pass | `feature_verifier_policy.json` | speculative accept rate rises without K-Score loss |
| W817 | Multi-Token Student Heads | train task-specific future-token heads for repetitive workflows | `multi_token_head_receipt.json` | p95 latency improves at equal score |
| W818 | Human-Review Load Shaper | route ambiguous cases to humans based on marginal risk reduction | `review_value_queue.json` | review hours reduce critical failures more than FIFO |
| W819 | Compliance Crosswalk Compiler | map artifact evidence to NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act, HIPAA, SOC2 scopes | `compliance_crosswalk.json` | unsupported compliance claim blocked |
| W820 | Claim Budget Ledger | every public claim spends proof budget from evidence | `claim_budget.json` | stale proof removes claim automatically |
| W821 | Adversarial Feature Red-Team | attacks target latent features and route boundaries | `feature_redteam_pack.json` | attack expands failure taxonomy |
| W822 | Negative Transfer Firewall | block cross-domain transfer when gradients or features conflict | `transfer_firewall_report.json` | harmful source domain rejected |
| W823 | Artifact Memory Distillation | compile stable retrieval memories into artifact sidecars | `memory_sidecar.json` | memory improves K_faith without hallucination lift |
| W824 | Model-Family Substitution Proof | prove when one backbone can replace another for a task | `backbone_substitution_report.json` | replacement passes typed equivalence |
| W825 | Cost-Of-Delay Optimizer | prioritize research/build work by lost savings and ARR unlock | `work_priority_model.json` | roadmap predicts realized metric movement |
| W826 | Live Benchmark Freshness Oracle | detect when public benchmark claims have expired | `benchmark_freshness.json` | expired benchmark blocks marketing claim |
| W827 | Target-Specific Safety Envelope | safety thresholds adapt to browser, phone, edge, VPC, air-gap | `target_safety_envelope.json` | wrong target cannot run with stale envelope |
| W828 | Tool-Use Causal Receipt | agent tool calls prove which tool output caused final answer | `tool_causal_receipt.json` | unsupported tool claim rejected |
| W829 | Artifact Rollback Simulator | simulate rollback impact before revoking an artifact | `rollback_simulation.json` | rollback plan covers dependent routes |
| W830 | Distillation Incident Replay | incidents become deterministic replay packs | `incident_replay.kolmtest` | incident cannot close without replay passing |
| W831 | Standardization Pack Generator | create RFC, model card, datasheet, scorecard, and conformance pack | `standards_packet.zip` | external reviewer can rerun core claims |

### W800-W831 Build Priorities

Build the wave in four groups:

Group A: feature proof.

- W800 Feature Receipt Distillation
- W801 Mechanistic Regression Lock
- W804 Interpretability-Guided Quantization
- W809 Feature-Gated Early Exit
- W810 Feature Drift Sentinel

Why first:

- These inventions make runtime and distillation decisions inspectable.
- They create a proof layer competitors cannot copy without the same activation and failure telemetry.
- They directly improve quality, safety, quantization, and drift metrics.

Group B: preference and policy.

- W802 Preference Delta Compiler
- W803 Constitutional Artifact Policy
- W806 Counterfactual K-Score
- W807 Preference-Aware Route Envelope
- W808 Proof-Carrying Prompt Evolution

Why second:

- These inventions convert human and policy feedback into typed artifact changes.
- They keep K-Score from becoming an average by forcing causal and preference-level evidence.
- They make prompt/rubric/route changes auditable.

Group C: privacy and fleet learning.

- W811 Rights-Aware Preference Learning
- W812 Private Teacher Aggregator
- W813 Secure Aggregate Failure Atlas
- W814 Federated Device Calibration
- W822 Negative Transfer Firewall

Why third:

- These inventions let Kolm learn across deployments without taking on unbounded data risk.
- They turn privacy into a product moat rather than a constraint.
- They directly support enterprise, regulated, and device-fleet surfaces.

Group D: runtime, governance, and standardization.

- W815 Semantic KV Compression
- W816 Speculative Feature Verifier
- W817 Multi-Token Student Heads
- W818 Human-Review Load Shaper
- W819 Compliance Crosswalk Compiler
- W820 Claim Budget Ledger
- W821 Adversarial Feature Red-Team
- W823 Artifact Memory Distillation
- W824 Model-Family Substitution Proof
- W825 Cost-Of-Delay Optimizer
- W826 Live Benchmark Freshness Oracle
- W827 Target-Specific Safety Envelope
- W828 Tool-Use Causal Receipt
- W829 Artifact Rollback Simulator
- W830 Distillation Incident Replay
- W831 Standardization Pack Generator

Why fourth:

- These make Kolm operable as a regulated production system rather than a model-building tool.
- They connect runtime speed, compliance, incidents, and standards into one artifact lifecycle.

### W800 Feature Proof Contracts

W800 Feature Receipt Distillation:

```text
inputs:
  teacher activations where available
  student activations
  failure clusters
  SAE or feature dictionary
  K-Score slices

outputs:
  feature_receipt
  protected_feature_set
  feature_loss_report
  intervention_tests

promotion:
  feature-level intervention predicts at least one high-value failure slice
  protected feature retention improves K_safe or K_faith
```

Build note:

- Start with open local models where activations are available.
- For closed teachers, use behavioral probes and student-side feature maps.
- Never claim closed-model internal interpretability unless activations are actually available.

W801 Mechanistic Regression Lock:

```text
inputs:
  previous artifact feature_receipt
  candidate artifact feature_receipt
  protected_feature_set
  holdout failures

decision:
  if protected feature lost and K-Score slice regresses:
    block promotion
  else if feature lost but no slice regression:
    require human review
```

Build note:

- This becomes `kolm features diff old.kolm new.kolm`.
- The account UI should show "feature proof changed" only as scoped evidence, not as a universal explanation.

W804 Interpretability-Guided Quantization:

```text
precision_assignment:
  high precision:
    protected feature channels
    evidence-bearing attention heads
    schema-critical MLP blocks
  lower precision:
    style-only or low-elasticity components

promotion:
  lower memory or latency at equal typed K-Score
  no protected-feature intervention regression
```

Build note:

- This is the bridge from mechanistic interpretability to runtime economics.
- It should compete directly against AWQ, GPTQ, SmoothQuant, FP8, and generic KV quantization.

W809 Feature-Gated Early Exit:

```text
exit_allowed when:
  route_risk <= budget
  protected_features_stable == true
  early_exit_score >= floor
  high_risk_class_absent_or_verified == true

exit_forbidden when:
  evidence feature unstable
  safety feature unstable
  schema feature unstable
  calibration stale
```

Build note:

- This makes LayerSkip-style early exit safe for enterprise tasks.
- The win condition is p95 latency at identical critical-failure rate.

W810 Feature Drift Sentinel:

```text
monitor:
  feature distribution shift
  route cluster shift
  retrieval source shift
  teacher version shift
  score residual shift

actions:
  warn
  degrade_to_cloud_verify
  require_recalibration
  block
```

Build note:

- Drift should trigger before visible business failure where possible.
- The sentinel must emit proof fields that account, CLI, and audit exports can display.

### W800 Preference And Policy Contracts

W802 Preference Delta Compiler:

```text
input:
  pairwise preference
  failure taxonomy
  K-Score axis
  rights receipt

output:
  preference_delta:
    axis
    magnitude
    confidence
    allowed_use
    update_target

promotion:
  improves target preference slice
  does not regress safety, faithfulness, or schema validity
```

Build note:

- DPO-style optimization is useful, but Kolm must preserve typed K-Score scope.
- Preference pairs cannot become generic reward if their rights scope is tenant-only.

W803 Constitutional Artifact Policy:

```text
constitution:
  principles
  conflicts
  precedence
  examples
  blocked actions
  review-required zones

compiled_to:
  route policy
  refusal policy
  human review policy
  K-Score safety fixtures
```

Build note:

- Constitutional AI becomes enterprise-configurable artifact policy.
- Policy conflicts must create review/block states, not arbitrary model behavior.

W806 Counterfactual K-Score:

```text
counterfactual_set:
  minimal input changes
  protected attributes
  evidence substitutions
  policy toggles
  schema perturbations

score:
  right_change_when_cause_changes
  no_change_when_irrelevant_changes
```

Build note:

- This prevents artifacts from passing evals through shortcuts.
- It should become a required gate for high-risk and regulated deployments.

W807 Preference-Aware Route Envelope:

```text
route_risk = f(
  critical_failure_probability,
  preference_loss_probability,
  policy_violation_probability,
  cloud_exposure_budget,
  human_review_budget
)
```

Build note:

- Not every local answer is wrong, but some are lower preference than the teacher.
- Kolm should spend cloud calls where preference loss affects retention or revenue.

W808 Proof-Carrying Prompt Evolution:

```text
prompt_patch:
  previous_prompt_hash
  new_prompt_hash
  textual_gradient
  failure_cluster
  expected_axis_movement
  actual_axis_movement
  rejected_shortcuts
```

Build note:

- This turns GEPA/TextGrad-style optimization into audited artifact evolution.
- A prompt change without before/after K-Score proof cannot be promoted.

### W800 Privacy And Fleet Contracts

W811 Rights-Aware Preference Learning:

- each preference pair inherits source event rights
- every optimized prompt, route policy, or student update records preference dependency
- deletion invalidates derived artifacts or requires proof that the preference no longer contributes

W812 Private Teacher Aggregator:

- teachers vote on sensitive examples using noisy or private aggregation
- student sees aggregate label or critique, not raw private teacher data
- report privacy budget next to utility loss

W813 Secure Aggregate Failure Atlas:

- tenants contribute encrypted or aggregate failure vectors
- server learns only stable failure modes that pass minimum cohort and privacy thresholds
- atlas powers curriculum suggestions without raw customer data

W814 Federated Device Calibration:

- device fleets report aggregate runtime/profile residuals
- server updates target-specific profile priors
- raw prompts, outputs, and local files never leave device

W822 Negative Transfer Firewall:

- compare source and target feature gradients
- block transfer when source improves average score but harms protected target slice
- record rejected source domains in artifact provenance

### W800 Runtime And Governance Contracts

W815 Semantic KV Compression:

- classify KV cache entries by evidence value, recency, route risk, and feature importance
- protect evidence-critical KV at higher precision
- compress or evict style-only entries first

W816 Speculative Feature Verifier:

- check draft tokens against protected features before expensive verifier pass
- reject speculative branches that violate schema, safety, or evidence features
- combine SpecInfer-style trees with Kolm's route risk budget

W817 Multi-Token Student Heads:

- train extra heads for repetitive output formats and schemas
- use only where typed score proves stability
- disable for open-ended high-risk generation

W818 Human-Review Load Shaper:

- route human review by expected risk reduction per minute
- prioritize boundary cases that unlock artifact promotion
- keep review queues bounded by escalating low-value cases to teacher or block

W819 Compliance Crosswalk Compiler:

- map artifact proof to external governance frameworks
- include what is implemented, what is externally certified, and what is not claimable
- produce auditor export without overstating formal certification

W820 Claim Budget Ledger:

- every claim consumes evidence from benchmark, K-Score, certification, source, or runtime proof
- stale or revoked proof removes claim
- marketing copy can query allowed/forbidden claims

W821 Adversarial Feature Red-Team:

- attack the feature and route boundaries, not only prompt text
- search for examples where protected features activate incorrectly or fail to activate
- convert successful attacks into fixtures

W823 Artifact Memory Distillation:

- compile stable retrieval memories into signed sidecars
- keep provenance and freshness separate from model weights
- invalidate memory when source rights or corpus freshness changes

W824 Model-Family Substitution Proof:

- prove when Gemma, Qwen, Llama, Mistral, Phi, or a customer model can replace another for one task
- report equivalence only within task, target, and K-Score scope
- block broad "model X replaces model Y" claims

W825 Cost-Of-Delay Optimizer:

- calculate value lost by not shipping each invention or route optimization
- prioritize work by K-Score movement, cloud savings, ARR unlock, and risk reduction
- compare predicted and realized movement each quarter

W826 Live Benchmark Freshness Oracle:

- track benchmark data age, model version age, provider drift, and confidence interval
- degrade public claims when freshness expires
- emit "needs retest" tasks

W827 Target-Specific Safety Envelope:

- each target runtime gets its own safety and performance envelope
- an artifact valid on GPU is not automatically valid on browser or phone
- account UI and CLI show target-specific validity

W828 Tool-Use Causal Receipt:

- bind tool input/output hashes to final answer claims
- identify which tool result caused each cited field
- reject unsupported agent claims

W829 Artifact Rollback Simulator:

- before revocation, simulate dependent routes, tenants, registry records, and fallback capacity
- emit rollback blast radius and staged downgrade plan
- verify cloud or human fallback capacity exists

W830 Distillation Incident Replay:

- every incident becomes deterministic replay with input, policy, route, runtime, and proof state
- incident cannot close until replay passes
- replay joins regression suite

W831 Standardization Pack Generator:

- produce RFC draft, model card, dataset datasheet, K-Score card, conformance report, and public benchmark packet
- make external review possible without exposing private holdouts
- move `.kolm` from product format toward ecosystem standard

## W900-W919 Implementation Epics

The W700 and W800 waves describe inventions. The W900 wave describes how to build them without losing engineering discipline. Each epic has a product surface, write scope, minimum implementation, release gate, and failure test. These are implementation epics, not research wishes.

| ID | Epic | Product surface | First shipped object | Release gate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| W900 | Unified Artifact Ledger | registry/account/CLI/API | `artifact_ledger` table and receipt API | every artifact has immutable lifecycle events |
| W901 | OpenTelemetry GenAI Bridge | gateway/capture/observability | GenAI spans with Kolm artifact attributes | capture trace reconstructs request-to-artifact path |
| W902 | SLSA/Sigstore Provenance Pack | governance/registry | signed provenance bundle | artifact verifies without private server trust |
| W903 | in-toto Distillation Layout | distill/compile/audit | signed distillation supply-chain layout | every step has authorized link metadata |
| W904 | TUF-Style Artifact Update Channel | registry/devices/fleet | signed update metadata | device rejects stale or rollback artifact update |
| W905 | K-Score Program Registry | eval/account/API | versioned score program registry | score reports pin evaluator, fixtures, and rubric |
| W906 | Rights Dependency Graph | privacy/datasets/governance | rights-to-artifact DAG | deletion request finds affected artifacts |
| W907 | Experiment Contract Registry | research/product ops | experiment contract API | metric claim cannot exist without experiment record |
| W908 | Off-Policy Route Evaluator | routing/cloud/account | DR/IPS route report | policy change ships only with risk estimate |
| W909 | Shadow Deployment Runner | runtime/routing | observe-only route/runtime execution | shadow report shows guardrails before rollout |
| W910 | Runtime Conformance Lab | compiler/devices/fleet | target conformance report | target profile blocks if score or safety differs |
| W911 | Feature Proof Store | feature proof/distill/runtime | feature receipt index | protected feature regressions block promotion |
| W912 | Incident Replay Registry | support/security/governance | `.kolmtest` replay pack | incident cannot close without deterministic replay |
| W913 | Claim Scope Service | marketing/account/docs/API | allowed/forbidden claim endpoint | stale proof removes public claim |
| W914 | Human Review Economics Queue | review/routing/account | marginal-risk review queue | review spend reduces measured critical failures |
| W915 | Dataset Value Broker | datasets/labeling/distill | value-of-information queue | label or teacher spend must show expected movement |
| W916 | Teacher Council Capability Cards | distill/models | teacher capability registry | council weights cite task-slice evidence |
| W917 | Device Fleet Calibration Loop | devices/runtime/privacy | secure aggregate device report | profile prior improves without raw data upload |
| W918 | Standard Packet Builder | docs/spec/registry | RFC/model-card/datasheet bundle | external reviewer can rerun public claims |
| W919 | Product Truth Kernel | all surfaces | generated product truth graph | account, CLI, docs, API all show same state |

### W900 Epic Details

W900 Unified Artifact Ledger:

```text
write_scope:
  src/artifact-ledger.*
  migrations/artifact_ledger.*
  public/account/artifacts*
  cli surfaces/artifacts commands

minimum:
  append-only lifecycle events
  artifact hash as primary identity
  event actor and source
  event proof references
  lifecycle state machine

release_gate:
  candidate -> scored -> compiled -> signed -> deployed -> deprecated/revoked is queryable
  ledger rejects impossible transitions
```

Failure test:

- attempt to deploy unsigned artifact and confirm state transition fails
- revoke data rights and confirm dependent artifacts are visible

W901 OpenTelemetry GenAI Bridge:

```text
span_attributes:
  gen_ai.system
  gen_ai.request.model
  gen_ai.response.model
  kolm.artifact.hash
  kolm.route.decision
  kolm.kscore.report_id
  kolm.rights.scope
  kolm.runtime.profile_id
```

Release gate:

- one captured call can be traced from provider request to behavior cluster to artifact opportunity
- sensitive payload capture respects configured redaction mode

Failure test:

- telemetry processor receives sensitive field and confirms redaction before export

W902 SLSA/Sigstore Provenance Pack:

```text
provenance_bundle:
  artifact_hash
  builder_identity
  source_dataset_digests
  kscore_report_digest
  runtime_profile_digest
  model_weight_digest
  cli_version
  build_command
  signature
  transparency_log_ref
```

Release gate:

- local verify command can validate artifact signature and provenance without trusting mutable docs

Failure test:

- mutate runtime profile after signing and confirm verification fails

W903 in-toto Distillation Layout:

```text
steps:
  capture
  redact
  rights_check
  dataset_build
  teacher_query
  train_student
  score
  compile
  sign
  publish
```

Release gate:

- each step emits signed link metadata
- unauthorized step performer blocks publish

Failure test:

- remove `rights_check` link and confirm artifact cannot publish

W904 TUF-Style Artifact Update Channel:

```text
roles:
  root
  targets
  snapshot
  timestamp

metadata:
  artifact_hash
  version
  allowed_targets
  expiry
  rollback_counter
```

Release gate:

- device rejects expired metadata, rollback version, or wrong target artifact

Failure test:

- serve older signed artifact and confirm rollback protection triggers

W905 K-Score Program Registry:

```text
record:
  program_id
  task_class
  rubric_version
  fixture_versions
  judge_versions
  deterministic_metrics
  confidence_method
  public_scope
  private_holdout_refs
```

Release gate:

- score report cannot be produced from anonymous evaluator state
- stale program flags report as non-current

Failure test:

- change judge prompt and confirm program version changes

W906 Rights Dependency Graph:

```text
nodes:
  event
  redacted_event
  dataset_row
  dataset_version
  training_run
  artifact
  benchmark_report
  public_claim

edges:
  derived_from
  licensed_for
  deleted_by
  invalidates
```

Release gate:

- deletion request returns affected datasets, artifacts, benchmarks, and claims

Failure test:

- delete source event and confirm stale public claim is removed or scoped

W907 Experiment Contract Registry:

```text
experiment:
  hypothesis
  primary_metric
  guardrails
  proof_level
  baseline
  treatment
  stop_rule
  rollback_rule
  claim_scope
```

Release gate:

- product claim links to experiment and evidence
- experiment without guardrails cannot approve high-risk feature

Failure test:

- try to promote latency improvement without K_safe guardrail and confirm rejection

W908 Off-Policy Route Evaluator:

```text
inputs:
  logged_context
  historical_action
  historical_propensity
  observed_reward_or_proxy
  candidate_policy

outputs:
  IPS_estimate
  doubly_robust_estimate
  confidence_interval
  sensitivity_warning
```

Release gate:

- route policy change has off-policy estimate before shadow or live rollout

Failure test:

- missing propensities downgrade report and block automatic promotion

W909 Shadow Deployment Runner:

```text
mode:
  live_input
  treatment_decision_observed
  control_decision_served
  no_user_visible_change
```

Release gate:

- shadow run logs counterfactual decisions and guardrails
- treatment cannot ship if shadow guardrail fails

Failure test:

- treatment chooses local answer where control escalated a high-risk case and confirm review

W910 Runtime Conformance Lab:

```text
targets:
  browser_wasm
  webgpu
  ios
  android
  cpu
  nvidia_gpu
  edge_worker
```

Release gate:

- target reports K-Score slice, latency, memory, deterministic replay, and unsupported ops

Failure test:

- target silently falls back to unsupported operator and conformance blocks profile

W911 Feature Proof Store:

```text
records:
  feature_id
  artifact_hash
  layer_or_probe
  interpretation
  evidence_examples
  intervention_result
  protected_status
```

Release gate:

- protected feature regression blocks artifact promotion or requires scoped waiver

Failure test:

- quantization removes protected feature and promotion fails

W912 Incident Replay Registry:

```text
replay_pack:
  incident_id
  artifact_hash
  input_hash
  route_policy
  runtime_profile
  proof_state
  expected_safe_behavior
```

Release gate:

- every production incident adds replay pack
- replay pack runs in release verification

Failure test:

- close incident without replay and confirm governance gate fails

W913 Claim Scope Service:

```text
GET /v1/claims/allowed?artifact=<hash>
GET /v1/claims/forbidden?artifact=<hash>
POST /v1/claims/check
```

Release gate:

- docs, account, CLI, and marketing copy can query claim state
- stale proof removes claim automatically

Failure test:

- expire benchmark freshness and confirm benchmark-backed claim disappears

W914 Human Review Economics Queue:

```text
priority =
  expected_critical_failure_reduction
  + expected_artifact_promotion_value
  - review_cost
  - delay_cost
```

Release gate:

- review queue reports marginal value and realized value

Failure test:

- FIFO queue performs worse than value queue in synthetic simulation and cannot be default

W915 Dataset Value Broker:

```text
actions:
  label
  teacher_query
  synthetic_case
  red_team_probe
  do_nothing
```

Release gate:

- broker explains why each data spend is expected to move a gate

Failure test:

- broker recommends data with no linked gate and recommendation is rejected

W916 Teacher Council Capability Cards:

```text
teacher_card:
  teacher_id
  provider
  model
  strengths_by_task_slice
  weaknesses_by_failure_cluster
  cost
  latency
  rights_scope
```

Release gate:

- teacher council weights cite capability cards and recent benchmark evidence

Failure test:

- stale teacher version invalidates capability card and council recomputes

W917 Device Fleet Calibration Loop:

```text
device_report:
  target
  profile_id
  aggregate_latency
  aggregate_memory
  aggregate_failure_proxy
  secure_aggregate_proof
```

Release gate:

- calibration improves target profile without raw prompt/output upload

Failure test:

- low cohort size blocks aggregate report

W918 Standard Packet Builder:

```text
packet:
  RFC
  model_card
  datasheet
  K-Score_card
  conformance_report
  public_benchmark_report
  provenance_bundle
```

Release gate:

- external reviewer can reproduce public claim from packet

Failure test:

- missing raw public report blocks standards packet

W919 Product Truth Kernel:

```text
truth_sources:
  product graph
  readiness ledger
  closeout ledger
  claim scope
  route inventory
  API inventory
```

Release gate:

- account, CLI, docs, API, and registry render the same product state

Failure test:

- route exists without product surface owner and truth kernel fails

### W900 Release Train

Release 1: proof spine.

- W900 Unified Artifact Ledger
- W905 K-Score Program Registry
- W906 Rights Dependency Graph
- W913 Claim Scope Service

Release 2: observable runtime.

- W901 OpenTelemetry GenAI Bridge
- W908 Off-Policy Route Evaluator
- W909 Shadow Deployment Runner
- W910 Runtime Conformance Lab

Release 3: provenance and fleet safety.

- W902 SLSA/Sigstore Provenance Pack
- W903 in-toto Distillation Layout
- W904 TUF-Style Artifact Update Channel
- W917 Device Fleet Calibration Loop

Release 4: intelligence loop.

- W911 Feature Proof Store
- W914 Human Review Economics Queue
- W915 Dataset Value Broker
- W916 Teacher Council Capability Cards

Release 5: standard and product truth.

- W912 Incident Replay Registry
- W918 Standard Packet Builder
- W919 Product Truth Kernel

### W900 Non-Negotiable Engineering Rules

1. Append-only evidence beats mutable status.
2. Every public claim must be queryable as data.
3. Every route decision must be replayable.
4. Every artifact update must be rollback-protected.
5. Every benchmark claim must have freshness.
6. Every dataset row must have rights lineage.
7. Every target runtime must have conformance.
8. Every incident must become a test.
9. Every optimization must name its guardrails.
10. Every account/CLI/API/docs surface must read from the same product truth kernel.

## Closed-Loop Optimization Layer

Prompt optimizers such as DSPy MIPROv2, GEPA, and TextGrad show that language-model systems can be optimized by treating prompts, examples, and textual components as search variables. Kolm should generalize that idea beyond prompts.

Kolm optimization variables:

```text
prompt
few-shot examples
retrieval policy
schema
teacher council
student genome
curriculum
route threshold
quantization profile
attention contract
KV policy
exit policy
fallback policy
```

Objective:

```text
maximize:
  K-Score lower bound
  - cost
  - latency
  - privacy exposure
  - proof incompleteness
```

Search modes:

- textual reflection for prompts and schemas
- Bayesian search for student genomes
- Pareto search for runtime profiles
- conformal calibration for route thresholds
- value-of-information ranking for data
- red-team adversarial search for safety failures

Ultimate breakthrough:

```text
Kolm is not only a compiler. It is a self-improving compiler whose search space includes prompts, data, models, routes, runtimes, and proof artifacts.
```

Guardrail:

```text
No optimizer may optimize against private holdout labels directly.
No optimizer may promote without typed K-Score intervals.
No optimizer may erase a blocked claim.
```

## Canonical Contract Schemas

These contracts are intentionally concrete. Implementation agents should treat them as the first schema draft for code, API, CLI, account UI, and artifact receipts.

### Contract 1: Behavior Fingerprint

Purpose:

Represent the task shape that decides whether Kolm should prompt, retrieve, distill, route, quantize, or block.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.behavior_fingerprint.v1",
  "fingerprint_id": "bf_...",
  "source": {
    "traffic_window": "2026-05-01/2026-05-31",
    "event_count": 184000,
    "tenant_scope": "tenant|public|synthetic",
    "privacy_summary": {
      "contains_pii": true,
      "redacted": true,
      "train_allowed_rows": 12000,
      "holdout_only_rows": 3000
    }
  },
  "task": {
    "primary_type": "extraction|classification|rag|code|tool_use|summarization|multimodal",
    "domain": "healthcare|legal|finance|devtools|enterprise_ops|general",
    "schema_entropy": 0.21,
    "reasoning_depth": "low|medium|high",
    "retrieval_dependency": 0.82,
    "citation_dependency": 0.91,
    "safety_sensitivity": "low|medium|high|regulated",
    "long_context_profile": "none|front_loaded|middle|needle|multi_doc",
    "tool_profile": "none|single_tool|multi_tool|agentic"
  },
  "economics": {
    "monthly_calls": 184000,
    "monthly_provider_cost": 12800,
    "p95_latency_ms": 1900,
    "human_review_cost_per_case": 2.4
  },
  "recommendation": {
    "primary_lane": "no_train|prompt|retrieval|adapter|distill|route_fallback|teacher_required",
    "candidate_quality_floor": 0.92,
    "candidate_targets": ["wasm", "iphone", "vllm"],
    "blocked_lanes": [
      {
        "lane": "local_only",
        "reason": "high_reasoning_depth_without_calibration"
      }
    ]
  },
  "hashes": {
    "input_event_set": "sha256:...",
    "fingerprint": "sha256:..."
  }
}
```

## Failure Taxonomy

The research lab needs a shared language for failure. Each failure should map to an owner, mitigation, and regression test.

| Code | Failure | Owner | Mitigation | Blocks promotion |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| F001 | K-Score lower bound misses floor | evals | more data, better student, teacher route | yes |
| F002 | Critical safety regression | safety | red-team fix, block route, retrain | yes |
| F003 | Faithfulness/citation regression | RAG/evals | evidence sidecar, retrieval fix | yes for RAG |
| F004 | Schema invalid output | runtime/DX | constrained decoding, schema verifier | yes for structured tasks |
| F005 | Route calibration stale | routing | recalibrate, conservative fallback | yes |
| F006 | Cloud exposure exceeds tenant budget | routing/privacy | threshold change, local/human path | yes |
| F007 | Runtime profile lacks proof | runtime | run profile benchmark | yes |
| F008 | Target runtime illegal op | compiler | target fallback or blocked-op report | yes |
| F009 | Quantization hidden regression | runtime/evals | protect channels, higher precision | yes |
| F010 | Sparse attention citation loss | runtime/RAG | dense fallback, contract update | yes |
| F011 | Early exit unsafe commit | runtime/routing | verify then commit, route-aware exit | yes |
| F012 | Data rights violation | privacy/legal | remove row, regenerate receipt | yes |
| F013 | Holdout contamination | evals/data | retire item, refresh split | yes |
| F014 | Teacher disagreement unresolved | distill/evals | human label, verifier, holdout | yes for training |
| F015 | Red-team high severity | security | patch, add regression, block deploy | yes |
| F016 | Missing artifact signature | compiler/security | sign and verify | yes |
| F017 | Missing SBOM/provenance for enterprise export | governance | generate CycloneDX/SLSA packet | yes for enterprise |
| F018 | Cost per success worsens | product/economics | reject artifact or route change | no unless contract says |
| F019 | Unsupported device target | devices/runtime | target fallback, block install | yes for target |
| F020 | Registry card lacks proof | registry | block publication | yes |

## Evidence DAG

Kolm should store proof as a directed acyclic graph, not as disconnected JSON files.

```text
capture_event_set
  -> behavior_fingerprint
  -> dataset_split
  -> teacher_council
  -> student_genome
  -> distillation_run
  -> kscore_report
  -> route_envelope
  -> runtime_profile
  -> artifact_receipt
  -> governance_receipt
  -> deployment_receipt
```

Graph invariants:

- Every node has a content hash.
- Every edge states why the downstream artifact depends on the upstream artifact.
- Holdout nodes are marked non-trainable.
- Private nodes can be referenced by hash without exposing contents.
- Public claims can only reference public or redacted evidence.
- Enterprise exports include enough hashes to replay the proof inside the tenant boundary.

## Implementation Wave Contracts

These waves turn the research into non-overlapping implementation work.

### Wave 1: Measurement And Rights

Build:

- K-Score report schema.
- data rights state machine.
- behavior fingerprint schema.
- failure taxonomy constants.
- artifact receipt skeleton.

Done when:

- every captured row has data rights.
- every K-Score report has typed scores and intervals.
- no artifact can promote without a receipt.

### Wave 2: Build Decision Engine

Build:

- compile opportunity detector.
- behavior fingerprint builder.
- no-train vs prompt vs retrieval vs distill planner.
- student genome candidate planner.

Done when:

- every build plan explains why training is or is not needed.
- every rejected lane has a reason.
- every selected candidate has expected metric movement.

### Wave 3: Distillation And Routing Loop

Build:

- teacher council.
- curriculum miner.
- student genome search probes.
- calibrated route envelope.

Done when:

- local/cloud decisions have selective-risk evidence.
- escalations feed the curriculum queue.
- teacher disagreement is routed to labels or holdout.

### Wave 4: Runtime Profile Compiler

Build:

- runtime profile solver.
- quantization profile.
- attention contract.
- KV policy.
- route-aware exit policy.

Done when:

- runtime optimization cannot activate without K_runtime proof.
- fallback and rollback are mandatory.
- target runtime blocked reasons are explicit.

### Wave 5: Proof, Governance, And Registry

Build:

- evidence DAG.
- governance receipt.
- SBOM/provenance export.
- registry trust rank.
- agent tool receipt.

Done when:

- every account, CLI, API, and public claim can resolve to a receipt.
- registry entries without proof cannot publish.
- enterprise exports distinguish shipped controls from external certifications.

## Algorithmic Build Appendix

This section translates the frontier layer into concrete algorithms. It is intentionally implementation-facing.

### Algorithm A: Compile Opportunity Detector

Purpose:

Find repeated AI behavior that is worth compiling.

Inputs:

```text
traffic_events
account_policy
provider_spend
latency_traces
privacy_classes
user_acceptance_events
human_corrections
```

Pseudo-code:

```text
for each route_group in cluster(traffic_events):
  repetition = estimate_repetition(route_group)
  spend = estimate_provider_spend(route_group)
  latency = estimate_latency_pain(route_group)
  privacy = estimate_privacy_pressure(route_group)
  acceptance = estimate_user_acceptance(route_group)
  schema_fit = estimate_schema_fit(route_group)
  risk = estimate_risk(route_group)

  opportunity_score =
    repetition * spend * acceptance * (1 + privacy + latency + schema_fit)
    - risk_penalty(risk)

  if opportunity_score >= threshold:
    emit compile_candidate
  else:
    emit monitor_only
```

Output:

```json
{
  "candidate_id": "cc_...",
  "task_cluster": "invoice_line_item_extraction",
  "monthly_calls": 184000,
  "estimated_monthly_spend": 12800,
  "privacy_pressure": "medium",
  "schema_fit": 0.91,
  "recommended_lane": "distill_structured_student",
  "blocked_lanes": [],
  "first_eval": "schema_validity_and_faithfulness"
}
```

### Algorithm B: Behavior Fingerprint Builder

Purpose:

Convert raw traffic into a task vector that every other system can use.

Pseudo-code:

```text
function build_behavior_fingerprint(events):
  clusters = semantic_cluster(events.prompts, events.outputs, events.schemas)
  for cluster in clusters:
    task_type = classify_task(cluster)
    schema_entropy = entropy(cluster.output_schemas)
    retrieval_dependency = estimate_retrieval_dependency(cluster)
    reasoning_depth = estimate_reasoning_depth(cluster)
    safety_sensitivity = classify_safety(cluster)
    domain_density = domain_term_density(cluster)
    context_profile = context_position_profile(cluster)
    tool_profile = tool_call_profile(cluster)
    acceptance_profile = user_acceptance_profile(cluster)
    latency_profile = latency_shape(cluster)
    privacy_profile = privacy_class_distribution(cluster)

    emit fingerprint
```

Quality checks:

- Fingerprint must be stable over resampling.
- Fingerprint must expose uncertainty, not only labels.
- Fingerprint must identify when no-train or retrieval-only is the better lane.

### Algorithm C: Student Genome Search

Purpose:

Pick the best student architecture and training recipe for a task, target, and policy.

Pseudo-code:

```text
function search_student_genome(fingerprint, targets, policy):
  candidate_genomes = generate_candidates(fingerprint, targets)
  legal = []

  for genome in candidate_genomes:
    legality = target_legalize(genome, targets)
    if legality.ok:
      legal.append(genome)
    else:
      record_blocked_genome(genome, legality.reason)

  predicted = []
  for genome in legal:
    pred = predictor.predict(
      kscore=fingerprint,
      latency=target_features(genome),
      memory=target_features(genome),
      risk=policy
    )
    predicted.append((genome, pred))

  frontier = pareto_filter(predicted, objectives=[
    "kscore_high",
    "latency_low",
    "cost_low",
    "memory_low",
    "cloud_exposure_low",
    "risk_low"
  ])

  probes = run_probe_distillations(frontier)
  finalists = filter_by_confidence(probes, policy)
  return rank(finalists)
```

Proof object:

```json
{
  "selected_genome_hash": "sha256:...",
  "candidate_count": 144,
  "legal_candidate_count": 52,
  "probe_count": 8,
  "selected_reason": "best lower-bound K-Score under iPhone memory budget",
  "rejected_reasons": {
    "memory_exceeded": 31,
    "runtime_missing_op": 14,
    "quality_lower_bound_failed": 39,
    "privacy_policy_blocked": 8
  }
}
```

### Algorithm D: Teacher Council Weighting

Purpose:

Use multiple teachers without averaging bad advice.

Pseudo-code:

```text
function teacher_council(item, teachers, policy):
  signals = []
  for teacher in teachers:
    if not policy.allows(teacher):
      continue

    output = teacher.generate_or_score(item)
    verifier = run_verifiers(item, output)
    reliability = lookup_teacher_reliability(teacher, item.domain, item.task)
    cost = normalized_cost(teacher)
    risk = provider_policy_risk(teacher, item)

    weight_logit =
      reliability.domain
      + reliability.task
      + verifier.agreement
      - cost_weight * cost
      - risk_weight * risk

    signals.append((teacher, output, weight_logit))

  weights = softmax([s.weight_logit for s in signals])
  return weighted_distillation_target(signals, weights)
```

Block rule:

```text
if teacher disagreement is high and no verifier/human label resolves it:
  do not train from averaged signal
  send to label queue or holdout
```

### Algorithm E: K-Score Typed Scorecard

Purpose:

Produce decision-grade quality evidence.

Pseudo-code:

```text
function kscore_scorecard(artifact, dataset, task_class):
  required_scores = required_score_types(task_class)
  item_results = []

  for item in dataset:
    student = artifact.run(item)
    teacher = item.teacher_output
    checks = run_deterministic_checks(item, student)
    judges = run_judges(item, student, teacher)
    verifiers = run_domain_verifiers(item, student)
    hard_gates = apply_hard_gates(checks, judges, verifiers)
    item_results.append(score_item(item, checks, judges, verifiers, hard_gates))

  typed = aggregate_by_type(item_results)
  intervals = bootstrap_intervals(typed)
  calibration = apply_quarterly_calibration(typed, intervals)

  if any required score missing:
    return not_decision_grade

  if any lower confidence bound below policy floor:
    return blocked

  return scorecard
```

Output:

```json
{
  "decision_grade": true,
  "headline_k": 88.4,
  "ci_95": [86.9, 89.8],
  "typed_scores": {
    "K_task": 91.2,
    "K_faith": 87.1,
    "K_schema": 99.1,
    "K_safe": 98.7,
    "K_runtime": 96.4,
    "K_audit": 100
  },
  "critical_fail_rate": 0.0,
  "known_non_routable_cases": ["ambiguous policy conflict", "missing source document"]
}
```

### Algorithm F: Calibrated Route Envelope Solver

Purpose:

Solve when local output is acceptable.

Pseudo-code:

```text
function solve_route_envelope(artifact, calibration_set, policy):
  features = extract_route_features(artifact, calibration_set)
  labels = material_regression_labels(calibration_set)
  model = train_risk_model(features, labels)
  calibrated = calibrate(model, method="temperature+isotonic+conformal")

  thresholds = {}
  for stratum in calibration_set.strata:
    thresholds[stratum] = smallest_threshold_that_satisfies(
      P_regression <= policy.regression_budget,
      P_critical_fail <= policy.critical_fail_budget,
      cloud_exposure <= policy.cloud_exposure_budget
    )

  return route_envelope(calibrated, thresholds)
```

Runtime decision:

```text
if risk <= threshold[stratum]:
  accept local
elif cloud_allowed:
  defer teacher
elif human_review_allowed:
  human review
else:
  refuse or block
```

### Algorithm G: Runtime Profile Solver

Purpose:

Choose quantization, attention, KV, and dynamic depth policies jointly.

Pseudo-code:

```text
function solve_runtime_profile(artifact, target, policy):
  candidates = enumerate_profiles([
    quantization_options,
    attention_options,
    kv_options,
    exit_options,
    fallback_options
  ])

  legal = target_legalize(candidates, target)
  measured = []

  for profile in legal:
    micro = run_microbench(profile)
    quality = run_quality_holdout(profile)
    risk = run_safety_holdout(profile)
    measured.append((profile, micro, quality, risk))

  viable = []
  for result in measured:
    if result.quality.lower_bound >= policy.runtime_quality_floor
       and result.risk.critical_fail_delta <= policy.critical_fail_delta_budget
       and result.profile.has_fallback:
      viable.append(result)

  selected = argmax(viable, objective=latency_memory_cost_utility)
  return proof_carrying_runtime_profile(selected)
```

Output:

```json
{
  "selected": "w4a8_attention_contract_exit_verified",
  "baseline": "bf16_dense_full_depth",
  "fallback": "bf16_dense_or_teacher",
  "quality_lower_bound": 87.8,
  "latency_delta": -0.47,
  "memory_delta": -0.58,
  "critical_fail_delta": 0.0
}
```

### Algorithm H: Promotion Gate

Purpose:

Prevent "looks better on average" from shipping regressions.

Pseudo-code:

```text
function promote_candidate(candidate, baseline, policy):
  deltas = paired_eval(candidate, baseline)

  if deltas.K_headline.lower_bound < policy.min_k_delta:
    return block("headline improvement not proven")

  for required_score in policy.required_scores:
    if deltas[required_score].lower_bound < -policy.max_score_regression:
      return block("typed score regression")

  if deltas.critical_fail_rate.upper_bound > policy.max_critical_fail_delta:
    return block("critical failure regression")

  if not candidate.proof_packet.complete:
    return block("proof incomplete")

  if not candidate.rollback_profile:
    return block("rollback missing")

  return promote_with_shadow(canonical_receipt(candidate))
```

Promotion should be staged:

```text
offline eval -> shadow traffic -> internal dogfood -> design partner -> GA
```

### Algorithm I: Privacy-Preserving Cross-Tenant Learning

Purpose:

Learn from many tenants without leaking tenant data.

Data classes:

```text
public_trainable
customer_trainable
tenant_local_only
holdout_only
synthetic_derivative_allowed
never_train
```

Process:

```text
for each failure packet:
  classify data rights
  if public_trainable or customer_trainable:
    eligible for direct training
  elif synthetic_derivative_allowed:
    generate abstracted hard case, then run PII/license scan
  elif tenant_local_only:
    update tenant-local adapter only
  elif holdout_only:
    use for eval, never train
  else:
    discard from learning loop
```

The research breakthrough is not only a privacy feature. It creates product value because enterprises can benefit from their own route failures without sacrificing data boundaries.

### Algorithm J: Ultimate Artifact Receipt

Purpose:

Make every claim traceable.

Receipt fields:

```json
{
  "artifact_hash": "sha256:...",
  "behavior_fingerprint_hash": "sha256:...",
  "student_genome_hash": "sha256:...",
  "teacher_council_hash": "sha256:...",
  "curriculum_hash": "sha256:...",
  "kscore_report_hash": "sha256:...",
  "route_envelope_hash": "sha256:...",
  "runtime_profile_hash": "sha256:...",
  "target_runtime": "vllm-h100",
  "data_rights_summary": {},
  "blocked_claims": [],
  "known_limits": [],
  "rollback": {},
  "signature": "ed25519:..."
}
```

Every website claim, account page, CLI output, and enterprise export should be able to point back to this receipt.

### Algorithm K: Feature Receipt Builder

Purpose:

Turn activation, probe, or behavioral feature evidence into a promotion gate.

Inputs:

- artifact candidate
- feature dictionary or probe set
- failure clusters
- typed K-Score slices
- intervention set
- target runtime profile

Process:

```text
extract features from baseline artifact
extract features from candidate artifact
for each protected failure cluster:
  identify feature set correlated with correct behavior
  run feature intervention or behavioral proxy test
  estimate feature importance and confidence
  mark feature as protected if intervention changes target K-Score slice
compare candidate against baseline:
  lost_protected_features = protected_baseline - candidate_features
  gained_features = candidate_features - baseline_features
  unstable_features = high_variance(candidate_features)
if lost_protected_features intersects high-risk slice:
  block promotion
else:
  emit feature_receipt
```

Outputs:

- `feature_receipt`
- protected feature list
- feature regression report
- waiver-required list

Promotion:

- protected feature retention is stable
- intervention suite predicts at least one real failure slice
- quantization or early-exit profile cannot bypass protected feature checks

Failure mode:

- feature names become storytelling without causal intervention; mitigation is to require intervention or counterfactual evidence before marking a feature protected.

### Algorithm L: Preference Delta Compiler

Purpose:

Convert pairwise preferences into typed K-Score and artifact update signals.

Inputs:

- preference pair
- source event rights
- rubric version
- K-Score axis map
- artifact candidate
- safety and faithfulness guardrails

Process:

```text
for each preference pair:
  validate rights scope
  classify preference reason:
    task_success
    faithfulness
    style
    safety
    schema
    refusal_quality
    policy_alignment
  map reason to K-Score axis
  estimate delta magnitude and confidence
  reject if pair conflicts with policy or rights
  attach delta to allowed update targets:
    prompt
    rubric
    route threshold
    student training
    holdout only
aggregate deltas by failure cluster
emit preference_delta report
```

Outputs:

- `preference_delta.json`
- allowed update target
- forbidden use list
- deletion dependency edge

Promotion:

- target preference slice improves
- K_safe and K_faith do not regress
- every preference pair has rights lineage

Failure mode:

- preference optimization teaches style shortcuts; mitigation is counterfactual K-Score plus safety and faithfulness lower bounds.

### Algorithm M: Claim Scope Resolver

Purpose:

Make public, account, docs, CLI, and enterprise claims data-driven.

Inputs:

- artifact receipt
- K-Score report
- benchmark freshness
- compliance crosswalk
- source ledger
- route and runtime conformance
- external certification state

Process:

```text
collect candidate claims from product copy and UI
for each claim:
  map claim to required evidence type
  check evidence freshness
  check evidence scope:
    task
    target
    tenant
    model family
    benchmark date
    certification status
  if evidence missing:
    mark forbidden
  elif evidence partial:
    rewrite to scoped claim
  else:
    mark allowed with evidence links
emit allowed_claims and forbidden_claims
```

Outputs:

- allowed claims
- forbidden claims
- scoped rewrites
- evidence links
- stale proof alerts

Promotion:

- no broad claim can render without evidence
- stale benchmark or calibration automatically removes claim

Failure mode:

- marketing text bypasses resolver; mitigation is claim-scope lint across public surfaces and generated docs.

### Algorithm N: Off-Policy Route Evaluator

Purpose:

Estimate whether a new route policy would have improved historical traffic before live rollout.

Inputs:

- historical route contexts
- historical actions
- action propensities
- observed reward or proxy K-Score
- candidate route policy
- risk and cloud exposure budgets

Process:

```text
for each historical event:
  compute candidate_action = policy(context)
  if candidate_action == historical_action:
    use observed reward
  else:
    estimate reward with reward_model(context, candidate_action)
  compute IPS estimate when propensity is known
  compute doubly robust estimate when reward model exists
  compute confidence interval and sensitivity bounds
check guardrails:
  false_local_accept
  critical_fail
  cloud_exposure
  human_review_load
emit route_policy_report
```

Outputs:

- IPS estimate
- doubly robust estimate
- sensitivity warning
- guardrail table
- recommendation: block, shadow, or rollout

Promotion:

- candidate policy has non-negative accepted workload value
- high-risk policies must pass shadow mode after off-policy estimate

Failure mode:

- missing propensities create false confidence; mitigation is report downgrade and forced shadow deployment.

### Algorithm O: Provenance Bundle Builder

Purpose:

Build a verifiable artifact supply chain using signed receipts and provenance metadata.

Inputs:

- artifact receipt
- source dataset digests
- training run metadata
- K-Score report digest
- runtime profile digest
- builder identity
- build command
- SBOM

Process:

```text
collect content hashes for every proof node
create in-toto style step links:
  capture
  rights_check
  dataset_build
  train
  score
  compile
  sign
create SLSA-style provenance statement
attach SBOM and runtime dependencies
sign artifact and provenance bundle
optionally log signature to transparency log
verify bundle before registry publish
```

Outputs:

- provenance bundle
- supply-chain layout
- signature
- SBOM reference
- verification report

Promotion:

- artifact verifies without trusting mutable docs
- missing required step blocks publish

Failure mode:

- signature proves only final file, not process; mitigation is step-level signed link metadata.

### Algorithm P: Runtime Conformance Matrix

Purpose:

Prove that a `.kolm` artifact remains within scope across target runtimes.

Inputs:

- artifact
- runtime profile
- target list
- K-Score program
- replay fixtures
- performance budgets

Process:

```text
for each target:
  compile or load artifact
  run deterministic replay fixtures
  run typed K-Score slices
  measure p50, p95, p99, memory, energy if available
  detect unsupported ops and fallback behavior
  compare target result against reference profile
  if score or safety outside tolerance:
    mark target invalid
emit conformance matrix
```

Outputs:

- target conformance report
- unsupported operation list
- target-specific safety envelope
- fallback policy

Promotion:

- target appears in public/account surfaces only if conformance passes
- target-specific claim includes runtime and hardware scope

Failure mode:

- runtime silently falls back to CPU or dense profile; mitigation is runtime trace and profile hash checks.

### Algorithm Q: Dataset Value Broker

Purpose:

Choose the next label, teacher query, synthetic case, or red-team probe by expected value.

Inputs:

- failure clusters
- current K-Score report
- promotion thresholds
- teacher costs
- human labeling costs
- rights constraints
- business unlock values

Process:

```text
for each candidate data action:
  estimate expected K-Score movement
  estimate critical-failure movement
  estimate cloud-savings movement
  estimate ARR or deployment unlock
  subtract label, teacher, review, and privacy costs
  reject if rights do not allow intended use
rank by expected net value and confidence
after action completes:
  record realized movement
  update broker calibration
```

Outputs:

- prioritized data queue
- expected value explanation
- realized-vs-predicted report

Promotion:

- broker beats random, uncertainty-only, and teacher-all baselines on label cost to promotion

Failure mode:

- broker chases high-uncertainty low-value data; mitigation is commercial boundary weighting and realized movement audit.

### Algorithm R: Incident Replay Closure

Purpose:

Convert every production incident into a deterministic replay and regression gate.

Inputs:

- incident report
- artifact hash
- route policy
- runtime profile
- proof state
- input and context hashes
- expected safe behavior

Process:

```text
capture incident proof state
redact or hash sensitive payloads
create replay pack with deterministic fixtures
classify failure taxonomy code
add expected safe behavior
run replay against patched artifact or route policy
block incident closure until replay passes
attach replay to release verification
```

Outputs:

- `.kolmtest` replay pack
- failure taxonomy code
- patch verification report
- regression test ID

Promotion:

- incident cannot close without replay
- future release fails if replay regresses

Failure mode:

- incident uses unrecorded external state; mitigation is hash all external dependencies and mark unreplayable state as blocker.

### Algorithm S: Product Truth Kernel Reconciler

Purpose:

Keep account, CLI, docs, API, registry, and marketing surfaces aligned with the same product state.

Inputs:

- route inventory
- API inventory
- product graph
- readiness ledger
- closeout ledger
- claim scope service
- artifact registry

Process:

```text
load all product truth sources
normalize surfaces, routes, claims, readiness items, and proofs
for each public or post-auth surface:
  check route ownership
  check readiness visibility
  check claim scope
  check stale proof warnings
  check CLI/API/account parity
emit product truth graph
fail if hidden route, hidden readiness gap, or mismatched claim exists
```

Outputs:
