- Prompt injection in reusable prefix quarantines cache.

### W1218: Tenant Cache Isolation Contract

Goal:

- Make cache sharing explicit, licensed, and auditable.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/team-capture-rbac.js`, `src/licensing-allowlist.js`, `src/object-storage.js`.
- New: `src/tenant-cache-isolation-contract.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1218-tenant-cache-isolation-contract.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Define cache scopes: private, team, enterprise, public_fixture, shared_system, blocked.
- Attach retention, deletion, and audit policy.

Acceptance:

- Shared cache requires explicit scope.
- Deletion request invalidates dependent cache keys.

### W1219: Graceful Degradation Catalog

Goal:

- Define what the system may do when ideal runtime proof is unavailable.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/runtime-policy.js`, `src/platform-capabilities.js`, `src/product-kernel.js`.
- New: `src/graceful-degradation-catalog.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1219-graceful-degradation-catalog.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Degradation modes: dense fallback, cloud fallback, shorter context, lower batch, human review, delayed job, read-only response, refuse.
- Each mode maps to allowed task risk classes.

Acceptance:

- Regulated task cannot degrade to unscored local runtime.
- User-visible next action is present.

### W1220: Artifact-Native Scheduler

Goal:

- Schedule by artifact proof and phase profile, not just model name.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/jobs.js`, `src/cloud-compute-broker.js`, `src/runtime-policy.js`.
- New: `src/artifact-native-scheduler.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1220-artifact-native-scheduler.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Queue keys include artifact hash, route envelope, phase profile, cache locality, tenant priority, and proof freshness.
- Co-schedule related requests when cache reuse is safe.

Acceptance:

- Scheduler can group cache-compatible requests.
- Scheduler separates tenants unless cache contract permits sharing.

### W1221: Serving Failure Injection Lab

Goal:

- Prove rollback and degradation before production incidents.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/simulation.js`, `src/replay.js`, `src/platform-capabilities.js`.
- New: `src/serving-failure-injection-lab.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1221-serving-failure-injection-lab.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Inject prefill worker loss, decode worker loss, cache miss surge, storage latency, network partition, device thermal throttle, route drift, and calibration expiration.

Acceptance:

- Every failure has expected degraded mode.
- Failure lab emits runbook and receipt.

### W1222: Cross-Engine Conformance Adapter

Goal:

- Normalize proof across vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, SGLang, ONNX Runtime, ExecuTorch, WebNN, and local runners.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/runtime-conformance-matrix.js`, `src/runners/*`, `src/compile-targets.js`.
- New: `src/cross-engine-conformance-adapter.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1222-cross-engine-conformance-adapter.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Define conformance output with logits tolerance when available, output equivalence, latency, memory, unsupported ops, and proof scope.

Acceptance:

- Engine-specific reports normalize to same schema.
- Unsupported proof dimensions stay explicit.

### W1223: Hardware Procurement Planner

Goal:

- Use real workload and artifact data to choose hardware.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/compute/estimator.js`, `src/cloud-compute-broker.js`, `src/platform-capabilities.js`.
- New: `src/hardware-procurement-planner.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1223-hardware-procurement-planner.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Compare GPU, CPU, NPU, edge, BYOC, and hosted options by goodput, cost, memory, energy, data residency, and proof coverage.

Acceptance:

- Planner can prefer cheaper hardware only when K-Score and conformance pass.
- Planner distinguishes capex, opex, and managed cloud.

### W1224: Automatic Rollback Runbook

Goal:

- Convert proof failures into executable rollback plans.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/drift-supersession.js`, `src/deployment-plans.js`, `src/audit.js`.
- New: `src/automatic-rollback-runbook.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1224-automatic-rollback-runbook.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Inputs: failed K-Score axis, route drift, runtime conformance failure, cache poisoning, incident replay, calibration debt, SLO breach.
- Output: rollback target, affected cohorts, cache invalidation, claim downgrade, notification, and verification command.

Acceptance:

- Runbook cannot omit affected artifacts.
- Claim downgrade accompanies runtime rollback when public claim changes.

### W1225: On-Device Adapter Training Guard

Goal:

- Allow future on-device personalization without creating unreviewed model drift.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/federated-learning.js`, `src/device-capabilities.js`, `src/privacy-membrane.js`.
- New: `src/on-device-adapter-training-guard.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1225-on-device-adapter-training-guard.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Permit local adapter updates only within task, rights, memory, safety, and rollback boundaries.
- Emit adapter receipt with private-only scope unless exported through explicit policy.

Acceptance:

- Local adapter cannot silently alter enterprise artifact claim.
- Adapter rollback is available offline.

### W1226: Edge Evidence Sync Protocol

Goal:

- Sync proofs from disconnected or intermittently connected devices.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/cloud-sync.js`, `src/devices.js`, `src/artifact-lineage.js`.
- New: `src/edge-evidence-sync-protocol.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1226-edge-evidence-sync-protocol.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Sync receipts, K-Score proxies, failures, rollout state, and cache invalidations with conflict resolution.
- Never sync raw private prompts unless policy permits.

Acceptance:

- Offline device can prove which artifact version ran.
- Conflict resolver preserves stronger safety restriction.

### W1227: Runtime Privacy Budget Meter

Goal:

- Track privacy leakage risk introduced by routing, cache, telemetry, and fleet aggregation.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/privacy-membrane.js`, `src/federated-learning.js`, `src/otel.js`.
- New: `src/runtime-privacy-budget-meter.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1227-runtime-privacy-budget-meter.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Meter privacy exposure for cloud fallback, telemetry export, cache sharing, federated aggregation, and benchmark export.
- Tie meter to tenant policy and data rights state.

Acceptance:

- Budget exhaustion blocks non-essential telemetry.
- Meter includes explanation and reset/renewal policy.

### W1228: Ultimate Kolm Serving Fabric Objective

Goal:

- Define the deployment objective that implementation agents optimize.

Objective:

```text
For artifact A, workload W, tenant policy P, target fleet F, and SLA L,
choose placement plan X that maximizes:

  served_value(X) =
    successful_tasks(X)
    * K_task(A, W)
    - cost_runtime(X)
    - cost_cache(X)
    - latency_penalty(X, L)
    - cloud_exposure_penalty(X, P)
    - privacy_budget_spend(X, P)
    - calibration_debt(X)
    - rollback_risk(X)

subject to:
  every phase has a proof-compatible runtime
  every KV transfer has a receipt
  every cache reuse satisfies tenant isolation
  every edge target passes conformance
  every degraded mode is allowed for the risk class
```

Implementation contract:

- New module: `src/ultimate-serving-fabric-objective.js`.
- New verifier: `scripts/verify-serving-fabric-objective.cjs`.
- Depends on: W1200-W1227.

Acceptance:

- The objective can reject the cheapest topology.
- The objective can choose edge execution, disaggregated GPU serving, or cloud fallback based on proof.
- Every term has a measurable source or explicit unknown status.

### W1200-W1228 Deployment Smoke Matrix

| Wave | Simulated fixture | Metric moved | Failure condition |
|---|---|---|---|
| W1200 | long RAG and short extraction mix | scheduler accuracy | wrong phase profile |
| W1201 | repeated prompt with tenant data | TTFT and privacy | shared private KV |
| W1202 | TTFT/TPOT SLO mix | goodput | infeasible plan emitted |
| W1203 | bursty mixed traffic | SLO attainment | static ratio remains |
| W1204 | two-region cluster | latency and residency | cross-region private transfer |
| W1205 | KV transfer event | audit proof | missing checksum or scope |
| W1206 | template variants | cache hit rate | PII normalized into shared key |
| W1207 | prompt template drift | cache economics | root segment not named |
| W1208 | high-risk queue overload | trust | unproven degradation |
| W1209 | image+text claim task | multimodal fidelity | region proof lost |
| W1210 | edge target pack | portability | unsupported op hidden |
| W1211 | WebNN/NPU target | device performance | CPU fallback marketed as NPU |
| W1212 | phone memory budget | device fit | KV memory omitted |
| W1213 | local redaction plus cloud verify | privacy | boundary receipt missing |
| W1214 | cohort rollout | fleet reliability | global promotion from one cohort |
| W1215 | cache-heavy workload | cost per task | cache cost hidden |
| W1216 | region hardware plan | energy trust | exact carbon overclaim |
| W1217 | malicious reusable prefix | safety | poisoned cache reused |
| W1218 | tenant deletion | privacy rights | dependent cache survives |
| W1219 | runtime unavailable | UX and trust | illegal degradation |
| W1220 | cache-compatible batch | throughput | unsafe tenant grouping |
| W1221 | worker loss and cache miss surge | resilience | no degraded mode |
| W1222 | vLLM vs edge runner | conformance | schema mismatch |
| W1223 | GPU vs NPU plan | margin | cheap hardware fails proof |
| W1224 | K-Score regression | rollback speed | claim not downgraded |
| W1225 | local adapter update | personalization | claim drift hidden |
| W1226 | offline fleet sync | audit | stronger safety conflict lost |
| W1227 | telemetry export | privacy | budget exhaustion ignored |
| W1228 | full placement plan | all metrics | cheapest topology wins despite proof failure |

### Deployment Build Order

Build in this order:

1. W1200 phase classifier.
2. W1201 KV locality planner.
3. W1208 proof-aware admission control.
4. W1202 goodput-SLO optimizer.
5. W1204 topology-aware placement solver.
6. W1205 KV transfer receipt.
7. W1210 edge target pack compiler.
8. W1212 device memory plan verifier.
9. W1214 fleet rollout protocol.
10. W1221 serving failure injection lab.
11. W1228 ultimate serving fabric objective.

This creates an end-to-end deployment spine before the expensive optimizers are added. The spine must prove where the artifact is allowed to run before it tries to run faster.

## Proof-Carrying Agents And Governance Layer

The prior layers make Kolm artifacts measurable, portable, and deployable. The next frontier is letting those artifacts act through tools without turning the product into an unbounded agent framework. Agent systems have a different risk shape than chat or inference systems:

- They use tools that may be poisoned, shadowed, over-scoped, or rug-pulled.
- They persist memory and context, creating long-lived attack surfaces.
- They can combine individually harmless tools into harmful workflows.
- They can persuade humans to approve unsafe actions with confident explanations.
- They can cascade bad state across agents, queues, registries, and enterprise systems.
- They can blur authorization, identity, data rights, model confidence, and business intent.

The ultimate Kolm design is not "agents with guardrails." It is:

```text
agent action = signed artifact + typed intent + least-privilege capability + policy proof + tool receipt + replayable consequence
```

Every agent plan, tool descriptor, tool invocation, memory write, inter-agent message, human approval, and external side effect must carry a proof. The agent can propose actions, but Kolm decides which actions are executable.

### W1300: Typed Intent Graph

Goal:

- Convert a user or workflow request into a typed intent graph before any tool is visible to the model.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/intent.js`, `src/workflow-ir.js`, `src/agent-blueprint.js`.
- New: `src/typed-intent-graph.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1300-typed-intent-graph.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Nodes: user goal, business object, allowed action, data class, risk class, required evidence, human approval, tool candidate, side effect.
- Edges: requires, forbids, reads, writes, sends, approves, escalates, blocks.
- The graph is built from trusted instructions and policy, not from untrusted retrieved context.

Acceptance:

- Prompt-injected context cannot create a new intent node.
- Every executable tool call must map to an intent edge.
- Ambiguous intent emits `needs_clarification`, not tool access.

### W1301: Tool Descriptor Attestation

Goal:

- Treat tool metadata as executable attack surface.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/agent-blueprint.js`, `src/registry.js`, `src/publisher-verification.js`.
- New: `src/tool-descriptor-attestation.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1301-tool-descriptor-attestation.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Hash name, description, schema, examples, permissions, network endpoints, package version, publisher, and risk class.
- Detect descriptor drift, shadowing, rug pulls, hidden prompt payloads, and schema/description mismatch.
- Require signed publisher proof for registry promotion.

Acceptance:

- Descriptor text change changes the attestation hash.
- Tool with hidden instruction in description is quarantined.
- Tool shadowing creates a registry conflict, not silent replacement.

### W1302: Capability Lease Engine

Goal:

- Replace permanent agent permissions with short-lived, scoped leases.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/auth.js`, `src/oauth.js`, `src/secrets-vault.js`, `src/team-capture-rbac.js`.
- New: `src/capability-lease-engine.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1302-capability-lease-engine.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Lease fields: principal, artifact hash, intent id, tool id, action, resource, max calls, data class, expiry, approval id, revocation status.
- Default deny. Forbid wins. Expired leases fail closed.

Acceptance:

- Tool call without lease is denied.
- Lease cannot be reused for different intent or resource.
- Revocation blocks already-planned but not-yet-executed calls.

### W1303: Policy-As-Code Compiler

Goal:

- Compile enterprise policy into analyzable tool and route decisions.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/runtime-policy.js`, `src/team-capture-rbac.js`, `src/product-kernel.js`.
- New: `src/policy-as-code-compiler.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1303-policy-as-code-compiler.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Support a minimal internal policy IR that can project to Rego-like and Cedar-like forms.
- Validate policies against tool schemas.
- Detect always-allow, always-deny, missing condition, and impossible approval rules.

Acceptance:

- Overbroad policy is rejected or warned with severity.
- Tool schema change invalidates dependent policy until revalidated.
- Policy decision includes rule id and explanation.

### W1304: MCP Authorization Boundary

Goal:

- Make MCP transport authorization insufficient by itself; every tool invocation also needs Kolm intent and lease proof.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/oauth.js`, `src/agent-blueprint.js`, `src/team-capture-rbac.js`.
- New: `src/mcp-authorization-boundary.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1304-mcp-authorization-boundary.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Normalize MCP HTTP auth, STDIO environment credentials, server metadata, token scope, and client identity into one boundary object.
- Flag STDIO credentials as local-process risk unless sandboxed.
- Bind OAuth subject to tenant, tool, and action.

Acceptance:

- Valid OAuth token is insufficient without capability lease.
- STDIO tool requires explicit local execution policy.
- Missing authorization metadata downgrades tool trust.

### W1305: Tool Call Receipt

Goal:

- Make every tool call signed, scoped, and replayable.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/agent-telemetry.js`, `src/agent-blueprint.js`, `src/artifact-receipt-builder.js`.
- New: `src/tool-call-receipt.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1305-tool-call-receipt.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Record intent id, lease id, tool descriptor attestation, arguments hash, redacted arguments, output hash, side effect class, policy decision, latency, cost, and downstream claims.

Acceptance:

- Tool output cannot support final answer without receipt.
- Side-effecting tool call requires policy decision and approval status.
- Public projection redacts secret arguments.

### W1306: Argument Firewall

Goal:

- Prevent prompt injection from turning untrusted text into executable arguments.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/privacy-membrane.js`, `src/sandbox.js`, `src/agent-blueprint.js`.
- New: `src/tool-argument-firewall.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1306-tool-argument-firewall.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Validate arguments against schema, intent graph, data rights, allowlists, regex/AST parsers, and taint labels.
- Treat retrieved content, web pages, emails, tickets, documents, and tool outputs as tainted unless declassified.

Acceptance:

- Tainted content cannot become shell/API path without explicit sanitizer proof.
- Schema-valid but intent-invalid argument is denied.
- Firewall explains the exact rejected field.

### W1307: Side-Effect Sandbox

Goal:

- Separate read-only, reversible, and irreversible tool actions.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/sandbox.js`, `src/runtime-policy.js`, `src/audit.js`.
- New: `src/side-effect-sandbox.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1307-side-effect-sandbox.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Classes: read, draft, simulate, reversible_write, irreversible_write, external_send, code_execution, payment, deletion.
- Require stronger lease and approval for higher classes.
- Support dry-run when available.

Acceptance:

- Deletion and payment cannot execute from model confidence alone.
- Tool with undeclared side effect is quarantined.
- Dry-run output is attached before approval.

### W1308: Memory Provenance Ledger

Goal:

- Make agent memory accountable and revocable.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/agent-telemetry.js`, `src/event-store.js`, `src/privacy-membrane.js`.
- New: `src/memory-provenance-ledger.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1308-memory-provenance-ledger.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Record memory source, tenant, user, confidence, expiry, privacy class, supporting receipt, contradiction set, and deletion dependencies.
- Mark untrusted memory separately from verified memory.

Acceptance:

- Retrieved or user-provided memory cannot become trusted memory without verification.
- Deletion request invalidates dependent memory.
- Conflicting memory triggers resolution, not arbitrary overwrite.

### W1309: Context Quarantine Engine

Goal:

- Separate instructions from untrusted data throughout agent execution.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/trace-capture.js`, `src/rag.js`, `src/privacy-membrane.js`.
- New: `src/context-quarantine-engine.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1309-context-quarantine-engine.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Label context as trusted_instruction, policy, tool_descriptor, retrieved_data, user_data, memory, tool_output, external_page, or synthetic.
- Deny instruction precedence to every untrusted label.

Acceptance:

- Malicious retrieved document cannot override system or policy.
- Tool output cannot grant itself future permissions.
- Final prompt assembly preserves labels for replay.

### W1310: Agent Red-Team Compiler

Goal:

- Generate artifact-specific agent attacks from OWASP LLM, OWASP MCP, OWASP Agentic, PyRIT, garak, and tenant policy.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/negative-variant-gen.js`, `src/distillation-redteam-gate.js`, `scripts/bench-redaction-fixtures.mjs`.
- New: `src/agent-redteam-compiler.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1310-agent-redteam-compiler.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Generate probes for goal hijack, tool poisoning, command injection, excessive agency, memory poisoning, inter-agent spoofing, human trust manipulation, data exfiltration, and supply-chain substitution.

Acceptance:

- Every executable tool has at least one misuse probe.
- Red-team pack includes expected deny/allow outcomes.
- Failed probe becomes incident replay fixture.

### W1311: Tool Poisoning Simulator

Goal:

- Test descriptor-level manipulation before publishing or installing tools.

Owns:

- New: `src/tool-poisoning-simulator.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1311-tool-poisoning-simulator.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Mutate tool names, descriptions, schemas, examples, return values, and version metadata.
- Score tool selection drift, unsafe invocation rate, and block rate.

Acceptance:

- Shadowed tool is detected.
- Poisoned descriptor changes block status or trust rank.
- Simulator output includes safe replay bundle.

### W1312: Inter-Agent Message Attestation

Goal:

- Prevent spoofed or poisoned messages between agents.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/agent-telemetry.js`, `src/team-events.js`, `src/pubkey-directory.js`.
- New: `src/interagent-message-attestation.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1312-interagent-message-attestation.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Sign sender, receiver, intent id, message class, data class, tool receipts referenced, expiry, and reply constraints.
- Refuse untrusted agent-to-agent delegation.

Acceptance:

- Spoofed sender fails verification.
- Message cannot expand privileges beyond original lease.
- Cascading workflow preserves the original intent id.

### W1313: Human Approval Integrity

Goal:

- Stop agents from manipulating humans into approving unsafe actions.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/federated-approvals.js`, `src/audit.js`, `src/notifications.js`.
- New: `src/human-approval-integrity.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1313-human-approval-integrity.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Approval screen data comes from receipts, not model prose.
- Show side effects, data crossing boundary, rollback, confidence, policy rule, and alternatives.
- Detect approval text that omits material risk.

Acceptance:

- Approval cannot be requested without dry-run or consequence receipt for high-risk actions.
- Model-generated summary cannot hide policy violations.
- Approval receipt binds exact action payload.

### W1314: Consequence Simulator

Goal:

- Simulate side effects before irreversible execution.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/simulation.js`, `src/replay.js`, `src/workflow-ir.js`.
- New: `src/consequence-simulator.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1314-consequence-simulator.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Given tool call and state snapshot, predict affected resources, external recipients, cost, deletions, permissions, compliance impact, and rollback path.

Acceptance:

- Irreversible action without consequence simulation is denied.
- Simulation uncertainty is shown to approval integrity layer.

### W1315: Agentic Supply Chain Verifier

Goal:

- Verify tools, MCP servers, packages, prompts, policies, and agent blueprints as supply-chain artifacts.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/sigstore.js`, `src/export-provenance.js`, `src/publisher-verification.js`, `src/package-release-readiness.js`.
- New: `src/agentic-supply-chain-verifier.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1315-agentic-supply-chain-verifier.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Check signatures, SBOM, SLSA/in-toto provenance, dependency risk, descriptor attestation, publisher reputation, and update policy.

Acceptance:

- Unsigned high-risk tool cannot publish.
- Dependency change creates review requirement.
- Registry trust rank incorporates supply-chain proof.

### W1316: Rogue Agent Circuit Breaker

Goal:

- Detect and stop agents that diverge from intent, hide state, or take self-directed action.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/agent-telemetry.js`, `src/runtime-policy.js`, `src/audit.js`.
- New: `src/rogue-agent-circuit-breaker.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1316-rogue-agent-circuit-breaker.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Watch for unauthorized goal mutation, tool escalation, memory tampering, repeated policy probes, unexplained external sends, and attempt to disable logging.

Acceptance:

- Circuit breaker can revoke leases mid-run.
- Incident receipt includes last trusted state.
- Agent cannot continue after audit logging failure.

### W1317: Agent Memory Poisoning Detector

Goal:

- Detect persistent context attacks and gradual false-memory insertion.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/memory-provenance-ledger.js` after W1308, `src/negative-variant-gen.js`.
- New: `src/agent-memory-poisoning-detector.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1317-agent-memory-poisoning-detector.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Score memory writes by source trust, contradiction, high-impact future use, novelty, repetition, and adversarial markers.
- Quarantine memory until verified.

Acceptance:

- Repeated malicious memory writes increase risk.
- Poisoned memory cannot affect route or tool policy while quarantined.

### W1318: Tool Output Truth Attribution

Goal:

- Bind final claims to the tool output that supports them.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/agent-telemetry.js`, `src/rag.js`, `src/artifact-receipt-builder.js`.
- New: `src/tool-output-truth-attribution.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1318-tool-output-truth-attribution.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Map final answer spans to tool receipt ids, output fields, retrieval spans, confidence, and transformation steps.

Acceptance:

- Unsupported claim is rejected or labeled unsupported.
- Tool output conflict triggers clarification or human review.

### W1319: Agent K-Score

Goal:

- Extend K-Score to agentic workflows.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/kscore.js`, `src/kscore-contract.js`, `src/agent-telemetry.js`.
- New: `src/agent-kscore.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1319-agent-kscore.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Axes: goal fidelity, tool correctness, permission minimality, side-effect safety, evidence attribution, recovery, human approval quality, and cost.

Acceptance:

- Successful answer with unsafe tool call cannot score high.
- Agent K-Score reports per-step failure contribution.

### W1320: Workflow Least-Privilege Synthesizer

Goal:

- Generate minimum permissions for recurring workflows.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/workflow-ir.js`, `src/team-capture-rbac.js`, `src/usage-analytics.js`.
- New: `src/workflow-least-privilege-synthesizer.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1320-workflow-least-privilege-synthesizer.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Learn required actions from receipts and propose lease templates with data classes, resources, max call counts, expiry, and approval thresholds.

Acceptance:

- Synthesized policy is narrower than observed broad credential.
- Missing action becomes explicit approval path, not broad allow.

### W1321: Agentic Incident Replay Pack

Goal:

- Turn agent failures into deterministic replay bundles.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/incident-replay-closure.js`, `src/replay.js`, `src/agent-telemetry.js`.
- New: `src/agentic-incident-replay-pack.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1321-agentic-incident-replay-pack.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Include intent graph, context labels, tool descriptors, leases, policy decisions, memory snapshots, tool receipts, approvals, outputs, and side effects.

Acceptance:

- Replay can reproduce allow/deny decisions.
- Pack redacts secrets but preserves proof hashes.

### W1322: Agent Registry Trust Rank

Goal:

- Rank agents and tools by proof, not popularity.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/registry.js`, `src/marketplace.js`, `src/registry-adverse-selection-defense.js`.
- New: `src/agent-registry-trust-rank.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1322-agent-registry-trust-rank.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Inputs: K-Score, Agent K-Score, supply-chain proof, incident rate, policy coverage, descriptor stability, publisher verification, conformance, and recency.

Acceptance:

- Popular tool with poor proof ranks below verified tool.
- Trust rank explains missing proof.

### W1323: Agent Policy Diff And Migration

Goal:

- Let enterprises safely update policy without breaking workflows or expanding risk.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/team-events.js`, `src/audit.js`, `src/product-truth-reconciler.js`.
- New: `src/agent-policy-diff.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1323-agent-policy-diff.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Diff policies by allowed actions, forbidden actions, data classes, approvals, affected workflows, and historical incidents.

Acceptance:

- Risk-increasing diff requires review.
- Migration plan includes affected leases and tools.

### W1324: Autonomous Action Budget

Goal:

- Cap how much damage or spend an agent can cause without renewed proof.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/usage.js`, `src/billing-breakdown.js`, `src/runtime-policy.js`.
- New: `src/autonomous-action-budget.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1324-autonomous-action-budget.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Budgets: money, tokens, emails, files touched, records changed, API calls, external recipients, privilege elevation attempts, and failed policy checks.

Acceptance:

- Budget exhaustion revokes leases.
- Human approval can renew only the specific budget dimension.

### W1325: Data Exfiltration Path Finder

Goal:

- Find multi-tool paths that leak data even when each individual tool looks allowed.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/privacy-membrane.js`, `src/workflow-ir.js`, `src/tool-call-receipt.js`.
- New: `src/data-exfiltration-path-finder.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1325-data-exfiltration-path-finder.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Build graph from read tools, transform tools, external-send tools, storage tools, and memory writes.
- Search for forbidden data-class movement.

Acceptance:

- Read-private plus send-external chain is blocked.
- Path finder reports minimal violating path.

### W1326: Agentic Governance Evidence Export

Goal:

- Export agent safety proof for enterprise audits.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/governance-receipt-graph.js`, `src/compliance-certification-packet.js`, `src/evidence-readiness.js`.
- New: `src/agentic-governance-export.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1326-agentic-governance-export.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Export policy coverage, tool attestations, leases, incident replay packs, approvals, red-team results, K-Score, Agent K-Score, and open risks.

Acceptance:

- Export distinguishes implemented controls from certifications.
- Missing evidence stays visible.

### W1327: Agentic Formal Runtime Monitor

Goal:

- Enforce invariants during execution, not only before execution.

Owns:

- Existing: `src/runtime-policy.js`, `src/agent-telemetry.js`, `src/audit.js`.
- New: `src/agentic-formal-runtime-monitor.js`.
- New tests: `tests/wave1327-agentic-formal-runtime-monitor.test.js`.

Implementation:

- Invariants: no tool without lease, no external send of private data, no self-grant, no unlogged side effect, no policy override from untrusted context, no memory write without provenance.

Acceptance:

- Monitor catches injected mid-run privilege escalation.
- Monitor failure stops execution.

### W1328: Ultimate Agentic Control Objective

Goal:

- Define the optimization objective for agent execution.

Objective:

```text
For intent I, policy P, artifact A, tool set T, memory M, and environment E,
choose plan X that maximizes:

  agent_value(X) =
    goal_success(X)
    * Agent_KScore(X)
    - side_effect_risk(X)
    - policy_violation_risk(X)
    - data_exfiltration_risk(X)
    - human_approval_burden(X)
    - tool_cost(X)
    - incident_debt(X)

subject to:
  every tool call has descriptor attestation
  every action has a capability lease
  every untrusted context remains quarantined
  every side effect has a receipt
  every human approval is based on receipts
  every memory write has provenance and expiry
  every final claim has attribution
```

Implementation contract:

- New module: `src/ultimate-agentic-control-objective.js`.
- New verifier: `scripts/verify-agentic-control-objective.cjs`.
- Depends on: W1300-W1327.

Acceptance:

- The objective can reject a plan that solves the task using excessive privilege.
- The objective can choose human review even when automation is cheaper.
- Every risk term has evidence or explicit unknown status.

### W1300-W1328 Agentic Smoke Matrix

| Wave | Simulated fixture | Metric moved | Failure condition |
|---|---|---|---|
| W1300 | injected document requests new goal | intent fidelity | untrusted goal accepted |
| W1301 | poisoned tool descriptor | tool safety | descriptor drift ignored |
| W1302 | expired credential | privilege safety | expired lease executes |
| W1303 | broad policy | policy quality | always-allow deployed |
| W1304 | MCP OAuth plus no lease | auth safety | token alone permits call |
| W1305 | tool output supports answer | auditability | answer lacks receipt |
| W1306 | tainted shell arg | command safety | tainted arg executes |
| W1307 | deletion tool | side-effect safety | irreversible action no approval |
| W1308 | memory write from web page | memory trust | unverified memory trusted |
| W1309 | retrieved prompt injection | instruction hierarchy | data overrides policy |
| W1310 | OWASP agent probe pack | red-team coverage | tool lacks misuse probe |
| W1311 | shadowed MCP tool | registry safety | fake tool selected |
| W1312 | spoofed agent message | multi-agent safety | spoof accepted |
| W1313 | approval with hidden risk | human trust | approval omits side effect |
| W1314 | payment dry run | consequence proof | irreversible action unmodeled |
| W1315 | unsigned MCP server | supply chain | high-risk unsigned publish |
| W1316 | agent disables logging | rogue control | run continues |
| W1317 | repeated poisoned memory | memory safety | poisoned memory used |
| W1318 | answer cites tool result | truth attribution | unsupported claim passes |
| W1319 | unsafe successful task | score integrity | high Agent K-Score |
| W1320 | recurring workflow | least privilege | broad permission template |
| W1321 | agent incident | replayability | decisions not reproducible |
| W1322 | registry ranking | marketplace trust | weak-proof tool ranks high |
| W1323 | policy migration | change safety | risk increase hidden |
| W1324 | spend budget | blast radius | budget overrun continues |
| W1325 | read plus external send | privacy | exfil path missed |
| W1326 | audit export | enterprise trust | missing evidence hidden |
| W1327 | mid-run escalation | runtime safety | monitor misses invariant |
| W1328 | full agent plan | all metrics | excessive privilege wins |

### Agentic Build Order

Build in this order:

1. W1300 Typed Intent Graph.
2. W1301 Tool Descriptor Attestation.
3. W1302 Capability Lease Engine.
4. W1306 Argument Firewall.
5. W1305 Tool Call Receipt.
6. W1309 Context Quarantine Engine.
7. W1310 Agent Red-Team Compiler.
8. W1316 Rogue Agent Circuit Breaker.
9. W1319 Agent K-Score.
10. W1327 Agentic Formal Runtime Monitor.
11. W1328 Ultimate Agentic Control Objective.

This gives Kolm a defensible agent story: agents can act only when artifact proof, policy proof, tool proof, and consequence proof line up.

## Distillation Leadership, K-Score, And Runtime Moat Execution Layer

This layer turns the distillation research program into a buildable product system. The objective is not "make small models." The objective is to make Kolm the system of record for turning expensive frontier-model behavior into owned, measured, deployable artifacts.

The winning loop is:

1. Capture production traces with consent, policy, and task labels.
2. Convert traces into a task fingerprint and teacher behavior manifold.
3. Search the student architecture and training recipe under customer constraints.
4. Distill and calibrate against public and private holdouts.
5. Score with K-Score against human preference, failure risk, cost, latency, reproducibility, and device fit.
6. Compile runtime profiles for quantization, attention, routing, early exit, layer skipping, and fallback.
7. Feed measured runtime traces back into the next search.

The public story should publish the math, open calculator, benchmark protocol, and negative results. The proprietary moat is the longitudinal trace corpus, private failure taxonomy, per-domain holdouts, activation profiles, routing labels, device traces, and distillation-to-runtime compiler.

### W1400: Distillation Research Operating Cell

Goal:

- Convert the "hire 2-3 ML researchers" idea into a measurable backend program.

Build:

- Create a research cell with three tracks:
  - Quality science: loss design, teacher modes, student retention, K-Score correlation.
  - Runtime science: quantization, sparsity, early exit, layer skipping, attention pattern mining.
  - Evaluation science: calibration data, human preference, critical failure taxonomy, benchmark validity.
- Every researcher owns one publishable paper and one shipped backend module per quarter.
- Every experiment must emit a `.kolm-research.json` record with dataset hash, teacher hash, student hash, loss recipe, K-Score vector, runtime vector, and failure taxonomy.

Smoke test:

- Simulate three researchers, nine experiments, and three paper candidates.
- Fail if any paper candidate lacks a corresponding implementation module and reproducible experiment record.

Acceptance:

- Research velocity is measured by shipped experiments, not paper drafts.
- Every result can be replayed from artifact hashes.
- Negative results are retained because they improve architecture search priors.

### W1401: Distillation Run Ledger

Goal:

- Make every distillation run a durable source of future product advantage.

Build:

- Store run-level facts:
  - `teacher_model_id`
  - `student_architecture_id`
  - `task_fingerprint_id`
  - `training_recipe_id`
  - `trace_policy_id`
  - `calibration_pack_id`
  - `runtime_profile_id`
  - `kscore_vector`
  - `failure_vector`
  - `device_vector`
- Store token-level facts:
  - teacher entropy
  - student entropy
  - teacher-student KL
  - acceptance probability
  - refusal probability
  - hallucination label
  - safety label
  - latency contribution
- Store layer-level facts:
  - activation kurtosis
  - outlier channel set
  - attention locality
  - FFN saturation
  - layer utility score

Smoke test:

- Generate a synthetic distillation run with 20 prompts, 4 task slices, 8 layers, and 3 devices.
- Fail if any K-Score component cannot be traced back to the exact run data that produced it.

Acceptance:

- The ledger is append-only.
- It supports public redaction and private replay.
- It becomes the training data for W1404, W1417, W1420, and W1423.

### W1402: Teacher Behavior Manifold

Goal:

- Represent what the teacher actually knows for a customer task, not just what benchmark scores imply.

Build:

- Embed prompts, completions, rationales, refusals, tool calls, citations, and failure labels.
- Cluster traces into task regions:
  - deterministic extraction
  - conditional classification
  - long-form generation
  - tool-mediated reasoning
  - safety-sensitive refusal
  - domain-specific style transfer
- Compute teacher behavior descriptors:
  - entropy surface
  - disagreement surface across teachers
  - fragility surface under prompt perturbation
  - policy boundary surface
  - calibration surface

Smoke test:

- Use a synthetic corpus where two clusters require opposite behavior.
- Fail if the manifold collapses them into one distillation target.

Acceptance:

- Distillation recipes can be selected per behavior region.
- K-Score can report region-level retention.
- Runtime routing can know which regions require cloud fallback.

### W1403: Task-Adaptive Student Architecture Selection

Goal:

- Turn "Task-Adaptive Student Architecture Selection" into a NeurIPS-grade system and a backend product module.

Build:

- Candidate architecture dimensions:
  - parameter count
  - layer count
  - hidden size
  - attention head count
  - KV cache format
  - tokenizer compatibility
  - MoE or dense topology
  - on-device memory budget
  - latency budget
  - quantization target
- Search objective:

```text
maximize E[KScore(student, task, device)]
subject to:
  p95_latency <= budget_latency
  memory_peak <= budget_memory
  cost_per_1k <= budget_cost
  critical_failure_rate <= budget_risk
  reproducibility_score >= budget_repro
```

- Use prior runs to predict the Pareto frontier before spending teacher tokens.
- Select an exploration batch only when expected value exceeds teacher cost.

Smoke test:

- Given three synthetic tasks, require three different student choices:
  - extraction chooses a small encoder-decoder or compact decoder.
  - long-context generation chooses a larger decoder with KV optimization.
  - safety-sensitive routing chooses a local classifier plus cloud fallback.

Acceptance:

- Architecture selection beats a fixed default student on K-Score per dollar.
- The module exposes a confidence interval, not a single overconfident answer.
- Failed searches write back negative examples.

### W1404: Distillation Scaling Law Planner

Goal:

- Use compute allocation laws to avoid expensive blind distillation.

Build:

- Model expected retention as:

```text
retention = f(student_size, teacher_quality, teacher_tokens, student_tokens, task_entropy, recipe_class)
```

- Fit per-domain parameters from the run ledger.
- Decide whether to:
  - distill now
  - collect more teacher traces
  - train a larger student
  - choose a different teacher
  - route instead of distill

Smoke test:

- Simulate a case where the teacher already exists and many students will be created.
- Simulate a case where a teacher must be trained for one student only.
- Fail if the planner recommends the same strategy for both.

Acceptance:

- Every distillation job has an expected retention curve before it starts.
- The product can quote expected cost and risk without pretending certainty.
- The planner improves as more customer tasks pass through the system.

### W1405: Adaptive Teaching Mode Router

Goal:

- Use different teacher signals for different token regimes.

Build:

- Detect token classes:
  - rote tokens
  - high-entropy decision tokens
  - safety boundary tokens
  - citation tokens
  - tool argument tokens
  - domain vocabulary tokens
  - style tokens
- Select teaching modes:
  - hard label
  - soft distribution
  - rationale trace
  - contrastive negative
  - refusal boundary
  - tool-call schema
  - preference pair

Smoke test:

- Create tokens where copying teacher logits harms generalization.
- Fail if the router uses one global loss for every token.

Acceptance:

- Student retention improves on high-entropy and safety-boundary slices.
- The recipe can be explained to an enterprise reviewer.
- The paper claim is narrow: adaptive teaching improves task retention under specified conditions.

### W1406: Cross-Domain Gradient Aggregation

Goal:

- Make "Cross-Domain Transfer in Knowledge Distillation via Gradient Aggregation" concrete.

Build:

- Track domain gradients for each task slice.
- Estimate gradient conflict:

```text
conflict(a,b) = 1 - cosine(grad_a, grad_b)
```

- Group domains into:
  - transferable
  - neutral
  - conflicting
  - safety-overriding
- Aggregate gradients with conflict-aware weights.
- Store a transfer graph that predicts which customer tasks can share a student backbone.

Smoke test:

- Synthetic domain A and B share extraction syntax but conflict on policy.
- Fail if aggregation improves extraction while degrading policy below threshold.

Acceptance:

- The model can reuse transfer where it helps and isolate domains where it hurts.
- Kolm can sell multi-team enterprise reuse without cross-team quality leakage.
- The paper includes negative transfer cases.

### W1407: Multi-Teacher Committee Distillation

Goal:

- Convert frontier model diversity into better students and stronger routing labels.

Build:

- Run teacher committees across OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, open-weight, and domain-specific teachers when policy permits.
- Compute:
  - agreement score
  - expert score by slice
  - refusal disagreement
  - cost-weighted authority
  - style variance
  - factual variance
- Train student targets from teacher mixtures only when disagreement is explainable.

Smoke test:

- Two teachers disagree on a safety refusal and one is wrong on a factual claim.
- Fail if the committee averages them into a plausible but unsafe answer.

Acceptance:

- Committee distillation improves failure rate, not just average quality.
- Teacher authority is slice-specific.
- The system can prove why a teacher was trusted for a token or slice.

### W1408: Private Holdout And Leakage Firewall

Goal:

- Prevent K-Score and distillation research from becoming benchmark theater.

Build:

- Maintain four dataset classes:
  - public calibration
  - customer-private training
  - customer-private holdout
  - Kolm-private adversarial holdout
- Enforce contamination checks:
  - semantic similarity
  - exact text overlap
  - answer-template overlap
  - synthetic generator lineage
  - model output reuse

Smoke test:

- Insert paraphrased duplicates into a training set.
- Fail if the firewall does not flag leakage before the score improves.

Acceptance:

- Public K-Score claims cite public data only.
- Enterprise reports can include private K-Score with leakage evidence.
- Research papers can be reproduced without leaking customer data.

### W1409: K-Score Mathematical Specification

Goal:

- Make K-Score a real metric, not a brand label.

Build:

- Define K-Score as a calibrated utility function:

```text
K = sigmoid(
  b
  + sum_i w_i * z_i(metric_i, slice)
  - sum_j r_j * risk_j(slice)
  - u * uncertainty(slice)
)
```

- Required components:
  - quality retention
  - human preference win rate
  - factuality
  - instruction following
  - critical failure rate
  - refusal correctness
  - cost per task
  - p50 and p95 latency
  - reproducibility
  - auditability
  - privacy risk
  - device fit
- Every component must expose:
  - raw score
  - normalized score
  - confidence interval
  - calibration version
  - slice id
  - evidence path

Smoke test:

- A model with high benchmark score but unsafe refusals must score below a safer model in a regulated slice.
- A fast model with low retention must not receive a high K-Score because it is cheap.

Acceptance:

- K-Score is decomposable.
- K-Score is monotonic only where monotonicity is justified.
- K-Score can be audited by customers and reproduced from artifacts.

### W1410: Open K-Score Calculator

Goal:

- Publish enough of K-Score that the market can adopt it as a yardstick.

Build:

- Open-source a deterministic calculator that accepts:
  - predictions
  - references where available
  - preference pairs
  - failure labels
  - runtime traces
  - cost traces
  - reproducibility hashes
- Provide adapters for common eval harnesses.
- Ship a public CLI:

```bash
kolm kscore calculate --predictions out.jsonl --calibration public-v1 --json
```

Smoke test:

- Run the calculator twice against the same fixture and require byte-stable output.
- Run it against a malformed fixture and require explicit missing-evidence errors.

Acceptance:

- Competitors can compute K-Score on public data.
- Kolm still has proprietary advantage from private calibration and trace density.
- Public methodology and private moat do not conflict.

### W1411: K-Score Calibration Dataset

Goal:

- Maintain the quarterly calibration dataset that maps K-Score to human preference.

Build:

- Dataset slices:
  - extraction
  - summarization
  - coding
  - regulated refusal
  - RAG citation
  - function/tool calling
  - multilingual
  - medical admin
  - legal admin
  - finance ops
  - device-local tasks
- Label types:
  - pairwise preference
  - absolute quality
  - critical failure
  - policy correctness
  - runtime acceptability
  - reproducibility verification

Smoke test:

- Corrupt 5 percent of labels and require the calibration pipeline to surface disagreement and confidence shrinkage.

Acceptance:

- Quarterly calibration releases include raw anonymized fixtures where legally possible.
- Private labels never leak.
- K-Score versioning makes old claims reproducible.

### W1412: Human Preference Correlation Report

Goal:

- Prove K-Score is better than generic overlap metrics for Kolm's tasks.

Build:

- Compare K-Score against:
  - BLEU
  - ROUGE
  - BERTScore
  - exact match
  - LLM judge
  - reward model
  - task-specific metric
- Report:
  - Spearman correlation
  - Kendall tau
  - pairwise accuracy
  - calibration error
  - slice-level failure
  - confidence intervals

Smoke test:

- Use synthetic labels where BLEU rewards a wrong copied answer.
- Fail if K-Score does not penalize the critical failure.

Acceptance:

- The report states where K-Score loses.
- The public claim is "better correlated on these task slices and dates," not universal superiority.
- The calculator and report share the same scoring code.

### W1413: K-Score Drift Monitor

Goal:

- Detect when K-Score stops matching human preference or production outcomes.

Build:

- Monitor drift in:
  - task distribution
  - judge behavior
  - labeler agreement
  - model family
  - user outcome
  - runtime environment
- Trigger recalibration when drift exceeds slice thresholds.

Smoke test:

- Simulate a new domain with the same average score but different failure pattern.
- Fail if the system does not demand recalibration.

Acceptance:

- Stale K-Score versions cannot be marketed as current.
- Customers can see which calibration version scored their artifact.
- The product prevents metric rot.

### W1414: Confidence-Calibrated Hybrid Local-Cloud Router

Goal:

- Make local and cloud inference one measured system.

Build:

- Route per call using:
  - local K-Score confidence
  - task criticality
  - privacy policy
  - latency budget
  - cloud cost
  - user region
  - fallback teacher reliability
  - device capability
- Learn calibrated abstention:

```text
route_local if E[utility_local] - risk_local >= E[utility_cloud] - cost_cloud - privacy_penalty
otherwise route_cloud_or_review
```

Smoke test:

- Local model is fast but uncertain on a regulated refusal.
- Fail if router chooses local only because it is cheap.

Acceptance:

- The router lowers cost while preserving failure-rate bounds.
- It creates new labels for future distillation.
- It can explain every fallback.

### W1415: Runtime Trace Feedback Loop

Goal:

- Use real runtime behavior to improve future students.

Build:

- Capture:
  - local confidence
  - fallback outcome
  - user correction
  - latency
  - memory
  - token acceptance
  - layer-skip decision
  - quantization error estimate
- Feed traces into:
  - architecture selection
  - calibration data
  - runtime compiler
  - route thresholds

Smoke test:

- Runtime shows one slice repeatedly falls back to cloud.
- Fail if the next distillation plan ignores that slice.

Acceptance:

- Production usage improves future artifacts.
- Trace capture is privacy-scoped and tenant-isolated.
- Every improvement has a data lineage.

### W1416: Distilled Activation Profiler

Goal:

- Find the activation distributions that make distilled models different from generic full models.

Build:

- Profile by layer, channel, token type, and task slice:
  - mean
  - variance
  - kurtosis
  - outlier frequency
  - zero density
  - sign distribution
  - entropy
  - saturation
- Compare student against teacher and base checkpoint.
- Emit a profile consumed by quantization and kernel selection.

Smoke test:

- Synthetic student has one high-outlier channel that harms quantization.
- Fail if profiler reports only global activation stats.

Acceptance:

- Quantization is slice-aware.
- Runtime kernels are selected from measured profiles.
- Distillation telemetry becomes a hardware advantage.

### W1417: Distillation-Aware Quantization Compiler

Goal:

- Build the best quantization tool for distilled artifacts.

Build:

- Choose per-layer and per-channel formats using:
  - activation outlier profile
  - K-Score sensitivity
  - device ISA
  - memory budget
  - task criticality
  - KV cache pressure
- Candidate formats:
  - FP8
  - INT8
  - INT6
  - INT4
  - mixed weight-only
  - activation-aware per-channel
  - KV cache quantization
- Optimize:

```text
minimize latency + memory + energy
subject to KScore_drop <= threshold and critical_failure_delta <= threshold
```

Smoke test:

- Global INT4 passes average quality but fails citation tasks.
- Fail if compiler selects it without slice-level guardrails.

Acceptance:

- Quantization choices are evidence-backed.
- The compiler can say "do not quantize this layer/channel for this task."
- Quantized artifacts include a reproducible runtime profile.

### W1418: Attention Pattern Miner

Goal:

- Exploit attention patterns learned from narrow task distributions.

Build:

- Detect:
  - local window dominance
  - retrieval anchor dependence
  - schema-token focus
  - citation span focus
  - tool argument focus
  - repeated prompt scaffolding
  - dead attention heads
- Compile attention profiles:
  - full attention
  - windowed
  - sparse
  - sink-token protected
  - retrieval-anchor protected
  - schema-token protected

Smoke test:

- RAG task needs citation spans but not full history.
- Fail if attention compression drops citation evidence.

Acceptance:

- Attention optimization is task-specific.
- The artifact declares which attention shortcuts are allowed.
- K-Score includes citation retention after optimization.

### W1419: Dynamic Layer Skip Controller

Goal:

- Skip redundant layers at runtime without corrupting quality.

Build:

- Learn a policy from:
  - hidden-state stability
  - token entropy
  - layer utility score
  - task slice
  - K-Score sensitivity
  - device latency
- Actions:
  - execute layer
  - skip layer
  - execute FFN only
  - execute attention only
  - early exit
  - route cloud
- Store skip receipts per inference for audit.

Smoke test:

- Easy tokens can skip layers but safety boundary tokens cannot.
- Fail if the controller uses one fixed skip pattern.

Acceptance:

- p95 latency improves while critical failure rate stays bounded.
- Skip policy is reproducible from artifact profile.
- The controller knows when not to skip.

### W1420: Early-Exit And Self-Speculative Runtime Contract

Goal:

- Combine early exit and speculative decoding in a verifiable artifact contract.

Build:

- Define for each artifact:
  - draft layer set
  - verifier layer set
  - acceptance threshold
  - rollback behavior
  - K-Score slice guardrail
  - max speculative span
  - forbidden slices
- Use runtime traces to update acceptance predictions.

Smoke test:

- Draft tokens are accepted on generic text but rejected on tool arguments.
- Fail if the same speculation threshold is used for both.

Acceptance:

- Speedups are reported with acceptance rate and quality loss.
- Speculation never hides failed verification.
- Tool-call outputs require stricter verification.

### W1421: Device-Specific Kernel Planner

Goal:

- Make enterprise-device deployment a first-class distillation output.

Build:

- Device profiles:
  - iOS Neural Engine
  - Android NNAPI or vendor NPU
  - WebGPU
  - WebNN
  - CPU AVX
  - CUDA
  - ROCm
  - edge GPU
  - WASM
- Planner inputs:
  - artifact graph
  - quant profile
  - attention profile
  - layer skip profile
  - memory budget
  - privacy policy
- Output:
  - kernel plan
  - unsupported ops
  - expected p50 and p95
  - fallback route

Smoke test:

- A device cannot support the selected KV format.
- Fail if planner still marks the artifact deployable.

Acceptance:

- "Run anywhere" becomes a measured matrix, not marketing.
- Account UI can show exact device readiness.
- Runtime claims are backed by profiles.

### W1422: Distillation Publication Pipeline

Goal:

- Turn the three target papers into product compounding assets.

Build:

- Paper 1: Task-Adaptive Student Architecture Selection.
  - Public: method, public datasets, ablations, negative results.
  - Private: customer trace priors, enterprise holdouts, run ledger.
- Paper 2: Confidence-Calibrated Routing for Hybrid Local-Cloud Inference.
  - Public: calibration math, routing bounds, public fixtures.
  - Private: production fallback traces and tenant policies.
- Paper 3: Cross-Domain Transfer in Knowledge Distillation via Gradient Aggregation.
  - Public: gradient conflict algorithm and reproducible synthetic/domain tasks.
  - Private: customer domain graph and transfer priors.

Smoke test:

- For each paper, require one public reproducible benchmark and one private product module.
- Fail if a paper leaks private moat data or lacks shipped code.

Acceptance:

- Publications build credibility.
- Product data remains proprietary.
- Each paper creates a wedge for enterprise sales and recruiting.

### W1423: Distillation Economic Simulator

Goal:

- Predict whether a distillation project is worth running.

Build:

- Inputs:
  - current API spend
  - task volume
  - teacher mix
  - privacy constraints
  - quality threshold
  - device target
  - expected retention
  - route fallback rate
  - training cost
  - monitoring cost
- Outputs:
  - payback period
  - expected savings
  - risk-adjusted savings
  - break-even volume
  - confidence interval
  - best next experiment

Smoke test:

- Low-volume, high-risk task should route, not distill.
- High-volume deterministic task should distill.

Acceptance:

- Sales ROI and backend planner use the same economics.
- No webpage can overstate savings beyond modeled evidence.
- The simulator feeds the product graph.

### W1424: K-Score Governance Board

Goal:

- Keep K-Score credible after it becomes a marketing asset.

Build:

- Define a change process:
  - public proposal
  - calibration impact report
  - backward-compatibility report
  - benchmark gaming risk review
  - enterprise advisory review
- Publish:
  - versioned spec
  - changelog
  - deprecation policy
  - public calculator test vectors

Smoke test:

- Change a K-Score weight and require the system to show which historical claims change.

Acceptance:

- K-Score cannot be silently tuned to make Kolm look better.
- Customers can reproduce old scores.
- The metric becomes trusted because it is governed.

### W1425: Distillation Safety Regression Firewall

Goal:

- Prevent smaller, cheaper models from hiding rare catastrophic failures.

Build:

- Required checks:
  - refusal correctness
  - policy boundary retention
  - jailbreak robustness
  - private data leakage
  - hallucination severity
  - tool-call argument safety
  - calibration under uncertainty
- Use worst-slice scoring:

```text
ship if min_slice(KScore_safety) >= threshold and max_slice(critical_failure_rate) <= threshold
```

Smoke test:

- Average K-Score improves but one regulated slice fails.
- Fail if artifact passes production readiness.

Acceptance:

- Safety gates override cost savings.
- Enterprise reports show worst-slice risk.
- The compiler can recommend "do not ship."

### W1426: Distillation-To-Runtime Compiler

Goal:

- Fuse training data, evaluation, and runtime profiles into one compiled artifact.

Build:

- Compiler inputs:
  - student weights or adapter
  - K-Score card
  - quantization profile
  - attention profile
  - layer-skip profile
  - router thresholds
  - device profiles
  - safety gates
  - audit receipts
- Compiler outputs:
  - `.kolm` artifact
  - runtime manifest
  - eval manifest
  - rollback manifest
  - deployment matrix

Smoke test:

- Remove the quantization profile from an artifact.
- Fail if the compiler still marks the device runtime as certified.

Acceptance:

- Training and runtime are not separate products.
- Every compiled artifact carries the proof needed to run it.
- Device deployments inherit the same K-Score contract.

### W1427: Data Moat Privacy Contract

Goal:

- Use production data to improve the product without breaking customer trust.

Build:

- Tenant controls:
  - opt-in telemetry
  - retention limit
  - redaction policy
  - private holdout separation
  - cross-tenant aggregation boundary
  - export and delete workflow
- Aggregate only:
  - derived statistics
  - anonymized calibration deltas
  - failure taxonomy counts
  - activation profile summaries
  - route confidence histograms

Smoke test:

- Attempt to train a global prior using raw customer prompts.
- Fail unless explicit policy allows it.

Acceptance:

- The moat is legal and explainable.
- Customers can get value from global priors without leaking data.
- Enterprise trust pages can describe exactly what is and is not shared.

### W1428: Ultimate Distillation Control Objective

Goal:

- Define the top-level optimization objective for the distillation business.

Objective:

```text
maximize:
  retained_task_value
  + cost_savings
  + privacy_gain
  + device_reach
  + auditability
  + customer_switching_cost
  + research_credibility

minimize:
  critical_failure_rate
  + calibration_error
  + runtime_regression
  + support_burden
  + benchmark_gaming_risk
  + data_trust_risk
```

Build:

- The optimizer must choose among:
  - capture more data
  - distill
  - route
  - quantize
  - skip layers
  - change student architecture
  - publish benchmark
  - do not ship

Smoke test:

- A cheap model with low auditability and high safety risk must lose to a more expensive hybrid route.

Acceptance:

- The product optimizes customer value, not vanity model size.
- The business model and technical optimizer point in the same direction.
- Kolm becomes the control plane for owned AI behavior.

### W1400-W1428 Distillation Smoke Matrix

| Wave | Simulated fixture | Metric moved | Failure condition |
|---|---|---|---|
| W1400 | three-paper lab plan | research velocity | paper lacks code |
| W1401 | run ledger | replayability | score lacks lineage |
| W1402 | two behavior clusters | retention | cluster collapse |
| W1403 | three task types | K-Score per dollar | fixed student selected |
| W1404 | two compute settings | spend efficiency | same plan selected |
| W1405 | mixed token modes | generalization | global loss used |
| W1406 | conflicting domains | transfer quality | policy regression hidden |
| W1407 | disagreeing teachers | failure rate | unsafe average chosen |
| W1408 | contaminated holdout | score honesty | leakage missed |
| W1409 | fast unsafe model | metric integrity | high K-Score |
| W1410 | repeated calculator run | reproducibility | non-stable score |
| W1411 | corrupt labels | calibration | confidence unchanged |
| W1412 | copied wrong answer | human correlation | overlap metric wins |
| W1413 | shifted domain | drift | no recalibration |
| W1414 | regulated local task | route safety | cheap local selected |
| W1415 | repeated fallback slice | learning loop | next plan ignores it |
| W1416 | outlier channel | profile quality | only global stats |
| W1417 | global INT4 | quant quality | slice failure hidden |
| W1418 | RAG citation spans | attention quality | citation evidence lost |
| W1419 | safety boundary token | latency and safety | fixed skip pattern |
| W1420 | tool argument draft | speculation safety | unverified acceptance |
| W1421 | unsupported KV format | device readiness | deployable marked true |
| W1422 | paper plus product | credibility | private data leaked |
| W1423 | low-volume task | ROI | distill recommended |
| W1424 | K-Score weight change | trust | historical impact hidden |
| W1425 | worst-slice failure | safety | artifact ships |
| W1426 | missing runtime profile | deployment proof | certified marked true |
| W1427 | raw tenant prompt | trust | global training allowed |
| W1428 | cheap unsafe model | total utility | cheap model wins |

### Distillation Build Order

Build in this order:

1. W1401 Distillation Run Ledger.
2. W1409 K-Score Mathematical Specification.
3. W1410 Open K-Score Calculator.
4. W1411 K-Score Calibration Dataset.
5. W1402 Teacher Behavior Manifold.
6. W1403 Task-Adaptive Student Architecture Selection.
7. W1404 Distillation Scaling Law Planner.
8. W1405 Adaptive Teaching Mode Router.
9. W1414 Confidence-Calibrated Hybrid Local-Cloud Router.
10. W1416 Distilled Activation Profiler.
11. W1417 Distillation-Aware Quantization Compiler.
12. W1419 Dynamic Layer Skip Controller.
13. W1426 Distillation-To-Runtime Compiler.
14. W1422 Distillation Publication Pipeline.

The first product wedge is not a better generic model. It is a customer-specific artifact that proves it retained the right behavior, saved money, ran on the target runtime, and knew when to fall back.

## Autonomous Experimentation And Causal Proof Engine

The prior layers define what to build. This layer defines how Kolm should discover, rank, prove, and kill ideas faster than any competitor. The mistake would be to run manual ML experiments, declare a few wins, and let the product drift. The durable advantage is an experimentation engine that continuously turns production traces, research hypotheses, and runtime failures into causal, cost-aware, reproducible build decisions.

This is the Kolm research flywheel:

1. Convert every product question into a typed experiment.
2. Select the cheapest fidelity that can answer the next decision.
3. Allocate teacher, GPU, human-label, and device budget with a multi-objective optimizer.
4. Estimate causal lift, not just before/after metric changes.
5. Stop losing ideas early and scale promising ideas safely.
6. Require a proof packet before anything is marketed, shipped, or published.
7. Feed results into architecture priors, route priors, K-Score priors, and runtime compiler priors.

The output is not a dashboard. It is a decision engine that says:

- run this experiment
- stop this experiment
- collect this label
- promote this artifact
- route instead of distill
- quantize only these layers
- publish this result
- do not claim this metric yet

### W1500: Experiment Type System

Goal:

- Make every research and product improvement a typed object with a falsifiable hypothesis.

Build:

- Define experiment types:
  - distillation recipe
  - student architecture
  - teacher committee
  - calibration data
  - runtime quantization
  - layer skipping
  - routing threshold
  - K-Score weight
  - policy gate
  - UX conversion
  - pricing ROI
  - deployment target
- Required fields:
  - hypothesis
  - target metric
  - guardrail metric
  - expected effect
  - minimum detectable effect
  - required fidelity
  - stopping rule
  - owner
  - evidence output
  - claim scope

Smoke test:

- Submit a vague "improve distillation quality" experiment.
- Fail unless it names a metric, slice, effect size, guardrail, and stopping rule.

Acceptance:

- No research work starts without a falsifiable contract.
- Every experiment can be rendered into a public or private proof packet.
- Dead experiments become reusable priors instead of disappearing.

### W1501: Multi-Fidelity Experiment Planner

Goal:

- Spend the minimum possible teacher, GPU, label, and device budget needed to make a decision.

Build:

- Fidelity levels:
  - static simulation
  - synthetic fixture
  - tiny public benchmark
  - small private holdout
  - partial training run
  - full training run
  - shadow production
  - live controlled rollout
- Planner objective:

```text
choose fidelity f
maximize expected_information_gain(f) / cost(f)
subject to:
  risk(f) <= risk_budget
  privacy(f) <= privacy_budget
  decision_power(f) >= required_power
```

- Bias correction:
  - learn when low-fidelity results are predictive
  - downweight low-fidelity sources that mislead high-fidelity outcomes
  - store fidelity transfer curves by task family

Smoke test:

- Synthetic fixture predicts a win, but historical fidelity transfer says this fixture is biased for safety tasks.
- Fail if the planner jumps directly to production rollout.

Acceptance:

- Cheap experiments answer cheap questions.
- Expensive experiments are reserved for decisions that need them.
- Fidelity bias is measured, not hand-waved.

### W1502: Cost-Aware Bayesian Research Scheduler

Goal:

- Rank all candidate research actions by value of information under real budget limits.

Build:

- Candidate actions:
  - collect labels
  - run teacher committee
  - train student
  - try quant profile
  - run device smoke
  - run red-team probe
  - rerun calibration
  - publish benchmark
- Model the unknown objective with a surrogate.
- Use acquisition functions that account for:
  - expected K-Score lift
  - uncertainty reduction
  - cost
  - risk
  - time
  - strategic moat value
  - claim unlock value

Smoke test:

- Two experiments have equal expected K-Score lift, but one unlocks a public claim and costs one tenth as much.
- Fail if the scheduler ranks them equally.

Acceptance:

- Research prioritization is explicit.
- Budget allocation can be audited.
- The system prefers learning per dollar, not raw novelty.

### W1503: Causal Metric DAG

Goal:

- Replace metric soup with a causal graph of how product changes move business and technical outcomes.

Build:

- Nodes:
  - artifact quality
  - critical failure rate
  - route confidence
  - fallback rate
  - latency
  - memory
  - cost
  - developer activation
  - trial-to-paid conversion
  - enterprise procurement confidence
  - support burden
  - churn risk
- Edges:
  - signed direction
  - expected lag
  - evidence strength
  - confounders
  - intervention history

Smoke test:

- A change lowers cost but increases support tickets and critical failures.
- Fail if the causal graph reports only the cost win.

Acceptance:

- Every tracked metric has an owner and causal parents.
- Metric moves can be traced back to interventions.
- Product leadership can see tradeoffs before shipping.

### W1504: Counterfactual K-Score Estimator

Goal:

- Estimate what K-Score would have been under a different route, teacher, student, or runtime decision.

Build:

- Store logging policy probabilities for:
  - route decisions
  - teacher selection
  - fallback decisions
  - quantization profiles
  - layer skip actions
- Estimate counterfactual value using:
  - direct outcome model
  - inverse propensity weighting
  - doubly robust estimator
  - shrinkage when propensities are weak
- Reject estimates when support is insufficient.

Smoke test:

- Evaluate a new router on logs where the old router never chose cloud for a slice.
- Fail unless the estimator reports missing support instead of a fake lift.

Acceptance:

- Offline policy changes cannot be promoted without support.
- Counterfactual estimates include variance and support diagnostics.
- The route optimizer learns safely from historical logs.

### W1505: Safe Policy Improvement Gate

Goal:

- Promote route, fallback, skip, and quantization policies only when evidence supports improvement under guardrails.

Build:

- Candidate policy must satisfy:

```text
lower_confidence_bound(value_new - value_old) > 0
upper_confidence_bound(critical_failure_delta) <= threshold
support_coverage >= threshold
```

- Gate types:
  - offline-only
  - shadow
  - canary
  - limited rollout
  - full promotion

Smoke test:

- Candidate router shows higher average savings but uncertainty overlaps zero on safety slices.
- Fail if promotion goes beyond shadow mode.

Acceptance:

- No learned policy can ship from average win alone.
- Safety slices dominate promotion decisions.
- Promotion evidence is exportable to enterprise customers.

### W1506: Anytime-Valid Research Monitor

Goal:

- Let researchers monitor long experiments without invalid optional stopping.

Build:

- Use sequential evidence accounting for:
  - K-Score lift
  - cost reduction
  - latency reduction
  - failure-rate increase
  - conversion lift
- Track:
  - confidence sequence
  - e-value or anytime-valid evidence score
  - stopping boundary
  - alpha spending policy
  - peeking-safe status

Smoke test:

- Researcher checks the experiment 100 times and stops at the first lucky win.
- Fail if the report claims ordinary fixed-horizon significance.

Acceptance:

- Stopping early is statistically valid only when the monitor says so.
- Claims distinguish exploratory evidence from confirmatory evidence.
- Long-running costly experiments can stop safely.

### W1507: Variance Reduction For K-Score Experiments

Goal:

- Make every experiment more sample-efficient without introducing bias.

Build:

- Pre-experiment covariates:
  - prompt entropy
  - task type
  - baseline K-Score
  - baseline latency
  - teacher disagreement
  - user segment
  - device class
- Apply regression adjustment or CUPED-style adjustment where valid.
- Report effective traffic multiplier.

Smoke test:

- A covariate is affected by treatment.
- Fail if the variance reducer uses it as a pre-treatment covariate.

Acceptance:

- Experiments reach decisions faster.
- Variance reduction never hides bias.
- Effective traffic multiplier is reported by metric slice.

### W1508: Experiment Interference Detector

Goal:

- Prevent overlapping product and model experiments from contaminating each other.

Build:

- Detect interference across:
  - shared users
  - shared artifacts
  - shared route policies
  - shared teacher budgets
  - shared eval labels
  - shared device caches
  - shared UI surfaces
- Use a conflict graph to block or stratify experiments.

Smoke test:

- A pricing experiment and ROI calculator experiment run on the same trial users.
- Fail if conversion lift is attributed to either without interference warning.

Acceptance:

- Experiment reports include concurrency context.
- High-risk overlaps require explicit approval.
- Backend and frontend experiments can coexist without corrupting evidence.

### W1509: Benchmark Anti-Gaming Sentinel

Goal:

- Detect when the system optimizes for a benchmark instead of real customer value.

Build:

- Signals:
  - public benchmark overfit
  - holdout divergence
  - prompt-template memorization
  - style bias
  - judge exploitation
  - metric weight gaming
  - unnatural refusal pattern
- Sentinel compares:
  - public score
  - private score
  - customer outcome
  - perturbation robustness
  - adversarial slice score

Smoke test:

- Artifact improves BLEU and public K-Score fixture but fails paraphrased private holdout.
- Fail if the system promotes the public score.

Acceptance:

- Benchmark wins require robustness evidence.
- Public claims include dataset and date context.
- K-Score stays credible as competitors target it.

### W1510: Scientific Negative Result Bank

Goal:

- Turn failures into durable advantage.

Build:

- Store:
  - failed hypothesis
  - run conditions
  - observed failure mode
  - suspected mechanism
  - future applicability
  - affected priors
  - do-not-repeat rules
- Link failures to:
  - architecture search
  - optimizer priors
  - product claims
  - paper ablations

Smoke test:

- A researcher retries a failed recipe with the same conditions.
- Fail unless the system explains why it is expected to fail or what changed.

Acceptance:

- Failed experiments reduce future waste.
- Papers include honest ablations.
- Search improves from what did not work.

### W1511: Mechanistic Distillation Probe Bench

Goal:

- Understand why a distilled artifact works or fails at feature level.

Build:

- Probe:
  - task features
  - refusal features
  - citation features
  - domain vocabulary features
  - tool schema features
  - failure features
- Compare:
  - teacher feature strength
  - student feature strength
  - quantized feature drift
  - layer-skip feature loss

Smoke test:

- Student passes average quality but loses a refusal feature under quantization.
- Fail if only aggregate K-Score is reported.

Acceptance:

- Distillation failures become diagnosable.
- Runtime optimizations can be blocked by feature loss.
- Mechanistic evidence feeds safety reports.

### W1512: Active Label Acquisition Engine

Goal:

- Buy human labels only where they change decisions.

Build:

- Candidate label requests:
  - preference pair
  - factuality label
  - refusal correctness
  - severity label
  - route fallback acceptability
  - device latency acceptability
- Rank by:
  - expected calibration improvement
  - expected policy decision change
  - slice risk
  - label cost
  - labeler uncertainty

Smoke test:

- High-volume safe slice has uncertain labels, low-volume regulated slice has high risk.
- Fail if label budget goes only to volume.

Acceptance:

- Human label spend moves decisions.
- Labeler disagreement becomes an uncertainty signal.
- Calibration improves fastest where it matters.

### W1513: Synthetic Counterexample Generator

Goal:

- Generate adversarial but valid cases for every artifact and metric.

Build:

- Generate:
  - paraphrased prompts
  - schema perturbations
  - policy boundary cases
  - device stress cases
  - route ambiguity cases
  - citation traps
  - tool argument traps
  - privacy leakage traps
- Keep a lineage graph from generator, seed, mutation, and expected property.

Smoke test:

- Generator creates invalid prompts that no real user would send.
- Fail if those cases are allowed to dominate score movement.

Acceptance:

- Synthetic cases find failures before customers do.
- Generated cases are classified by realism.
- Public benchmark data can include safe synthetic slices.

### W1514: Experiment Reproducibility Capsule

Goal:

- Make every result replayable.

Build:

- Capsule includes:
  - code hash
  - artifact hash
  - dataset hash
  - random seeds
  - hardware profile
  - runtime versions
  - environment manifest
  - policy manifest
  - scoring manifest
  - output digest
- Capsule can be:
  - public
  - customer-private
  - internal-only
  - redacted

Smoke test:

- Remove a runtime version from a capsule.
- Fail if replay is still marked deterministic.

Acceptance:

- Research reports can be audited.
- Enterprise evidence exports can cite capsules.
- Regression investigation starts from exact state.

### W1515: Claim Promotion Gate

Goal:

- Promote product claims only when evidence is strong enough for the audience.

Build:

- Claim classes:
  - internal note
  - experimental preview
  - local benchmark claim
  - public benchmark claim
  - customer-private result
  - enterprise sales claim
  - legal/compliance claim
- Required evidence differs by class.
- Unsupported wording is blocked at publish time.

Smoke test:

- "cuts cost 40 percent" is backed by one synthetic benchmark only.
- Fail if claim class is public or enterprise.

Acceptance:

- Website, docs, sales, and API metadata cannot outrun evidence.
- Claims have scope, date, dataset, and confidence.
- The claim gate consumes the same proof packets as research.

### W1516: Cross-Surface Metric Alignment Engine

Goal:

- Ensure backend, account UI, docs, marketing, pricing, and sales all measure the same things.

Build:

- Align:
  - ROI calculator assumptions
  - K-Score reports
  - product graph readiness
  - benchmark pages
  - account dashboards
  - API envelopes
  - enterprise exports
- Detect drift:
  - stale metric name
  - stale calibration version
  - stale pricing assumption
  - unsupported claim
  - hidden readiness caveat

Smoke test:

- ROI page says 40 percent savings while backend simulator says 18 to 24 percent for the same inputs.
- Fail if both surfaces pass independently.

Acceptance:

- Product surfaces cannot contradict backend truth.
- Every metric has one canonical source.
- Sales calculators are evidence-backed.

### W1517: Research Debt Ledger

Goal:

- Track the uncertainty and missing proof behind every product claim and technical direction.

Build:

- Debt classes:
  - missing public benchmark
  - weak private holdout
  - insufficient device coverage
  - no live customer proof
  - no human preference labels
  - no certification evidence
  - stale calibration
  - small sample
  - external dependency
- Every debt item has:
  - owner
  - unblock action
  - deadline
  - affected claim
  - affected roadmap item

Smoke test:

- A readiness item remains `needs_public_benchmark_data` without owner.
- Fail if the ledger is considered complete.

Acceptance:

- Nothing ambiguous hides.
- Open uncertainty becomes planned work.
- The company can prioritize evidence debt like engineering debt.

### W1518: Causal Root Cause Investigator
