- `data_sources`: application, ACORD form, telematics, inspection, loss run,
  claim note, invoice, photo, medical, property, weather, catastrophe, or third
  party source;
- `model_context`: actuarial model, predictive model, AI system, assumption,
  parameter, drift state, and validation state;
- `consumer_impact`: adverse action, rate impact, claim impact, explanation,
  review, appeal, and complaint linkage;
- `human_authority`: underwriter, adjuster, actuary, supervisor, compliance,
  legal, or regulator review state;
- `artifact_hash`: deterministic hash of the replayable packet.

The receipt should never state "covered," "denied," "approved," "fraud," or
"rate adequate" unless the authorized insurance workflow made that decision and
the packet contains the evidence and decision authority.

### W10391 InsuranceIR

Create an `InsuranceIR` with nodes:

- Applicant;
- Insured;
- Policy;
- Coverage;
- Endorsement;
- Exclusion;
- Limit;
- Deductible;
- Risk;
- Exposure;
- Location;
- Vehicle;
- Property;
- Claim;
- Loss event;
- Reserve;
- Payment;
- Vendor;
- Producer;
- Reinsurer;
- Treaty;
- Regulator;
- Model;
- Notice.

Edges:

- applied for;
- quoted by;
- bound by;
- endorsed by;
- covered candidate;
- excluded candidate;
- loss occurred at;
- adjusted by;
- reserved by;
- paid under;
- ceded to;
- audited by;
- explained to consumer;
- appealed by;
- examined by regulator.

The IR is what prevents the product from becoming a brittle policy PDF parser.
Every downstream tool should operate on structured insurance facts and evidence.

### W10392 ACORD And Carrier Data Binder

Insurance data exchange is fragmented across ACORD forms, ACORD P&C messages,
reinsurance messages, carrier APIs, broker management systems, policy admin,
claims systems, and spreadsheets. The binder should:

- parse ACORD form fields into InsuranceIR;
- preserve form version and licensing boundary;
- map AL3/XML/API fields to carrier-specific fields;
- report missing fields;
- detect stale form versions;
- bind producer and carrier identities;
- generate a field-level lineage report.

The wedge is migration and interoperability. Carriers and agencies will buy a
system that reduces rekeying, submission defects, and broken downloads before
they trust AI underwriting.

### W10393 Policy Administration And Endorsement Compiler

Policy operations require exact version control:

- policy term;
- inception and expiration;
- named insured;
- additional insured;
- locations;
- vehicles;
- covered property;
- forms;
- endorsements;
- exclusions;
- premium;
- taxes and fees;
- cancellation and reinstatement state;
- renewal state.

The compiler should generate policy-change evidence packets and compare before
and after states. It must flag changes that affect coverage, premium, or notice.

### W10394 Underwriting Appetite And Submission Triage

Underwriting triage should compile:

- appetite match;
- missing application fields;
- missing loss runs;
- exposure conflicts;
- jurisdiction limits;
- authority level;
- referral reason;
- reinsurance dependency;
- pricing model version;
- adverse consumer impact risk.

The system can recommend "refer," "decline candidate," "more information
needed," or "within appetite candidate." It should not final-bind or decline
without carrier authority.

### W10395 Claims FNOL And Evidence Intake Compiler

First notice of loss is a high-leverage surface.

Inputs:

- loss date;
- loss location;
- claimant;
- policy;
- coverage candidate;
- photos;
- invoices;
- police report;
- medical bill;
- repair estimate;
- weather or catastrophe data;
- witness statement;
- prior loss;
- fraud indicator candidate.

Outputs:

- claim completeness score;
- missing evidence list;
- coverage review packet;
- severity estimate candidate;
- reserve recommendation candidate;
- assignment recommendation;
- customer update draft.

This improves cycle time without pretending to decide coverage.

### W10396 Coverage, Exclusion, And Reservation Boundary

Coverage interpretation must be tightly bounded.

Allowed:

- cite relevant forms;
- extract limits and deductibles;
- list exclusions that may apply;
- compare loss facts to form language;
- draft reservation of rights candidate;
- draft request for information;
- prepare adjuster review.

Blocked without authorized review:

- final coverage decision;
- denial;
- bad-faith risk conclusion;
- legal interpretation;
- waiver conclusion;
- claim settlement authority.

The artifact should say "coverage issue candidate with evidence," not "not
covered."

### W10397 Actuarial Model Governance And Assumption Binder

The actuarial binder should preserve:

- model purpose;
- intended application;
- data lineage;
- assumptions;
- parameters;
- segmentation;
- credibility method;
- validation tests;
- sensitivity analysis;
- drift monitoring;
- actuarial communication;
- reliance on third-party models;
- limitation and uncertainty disclosure.

Kolm should not replace actuaries. It should make actuarial model evidence
replayable and regulator-ready.

### W10398 NAIC AI Governance Program Packet

The insurance AI packet should align with regulator expectations for AI systems
used in insurance:

- inventory of AI systems;
- consumer-impact classification;
- governance owner;
- risk tier;
- data sources;
- model validation;
- bias and unfair discrimination tests;
- third-party vendor controls;
- monitoring plan;
- audit trail;
- adverse consumer outcome handling;
- regulator examination export.

This is a flagship compliance wedge: "show your AI insurance evidence packet"
instead of answering regulator requests manually.

### W10399 Adverse Consumer Outcome Sentinel

Detect decisions or recommendations that could harm consumers:

- quote decline;
- rate increase;
- non-renewal;
- cancellation;
- claim denial;
- delayed claim handling;
- low settlement candidate;
- fraud referral;
- producer steering;
- underwriting class change;
- model drift by protected or proxy segment.

The sentinel should create a compliance review task with evidence, not hide the
risk behind aggregate metrics.

### W10400 Claims Fraud And SIU Candidate Guard

Fraud tooling is high-risk. The guard should separate:

- anomaly;
- inconsistency;
- prior-pattern match;
- document tampering candidate;
- staged-loss candidate;
- medical billing outlier;
- property estimate outlier;
- organized activity candidate;
- SIU referral;
- final fraud finding.

The claim must stay "SIU candidate" until authorized investigation completes.

### W10401 Reinsurance Treaty, Cession, And Bordereau Compiler

Reinsurance creates a powerful enterprise wedge.

Compile:

- treaty;
- layer;
- attachment;
- limit;
- retention;
- peril;
- geography;
- ceded premium;
- ceded loss;
- bordereau;
- claim notification;
- reinstatement;
- collateral;
- aggregate erosion;
- recoverable;
- audit evidence.

The product can reduce reconciliation errors between cedents, reinsurers, and
brokers by making every cession replayable.

### W10402 Catastrophe Exposure And Event Evidence Layer

The catastrophe layer should bind:

- policy exposure;
- geocode;
- peril;
- event footprint;
- hazard intensity;
- vulnerability;
- damage observation;
- claim count;
- reserve movement;
- reinsurance recovery;
- model version;
- uncertainty band.

This supports catastrophe response, capital planning, and claim surge triage
without overstating precision.

### W10403 Subrogation, Salvage, Litigation, And Vendor Packet

Claims leakage often hides in handoffs.

Track:

- liable third party;
- subrogation candidate;
- salvage asset;
- vendor assignment;
- repair estimate;
- litigation counsel;
- demand package;
- settlement authority;
- recovery amount;
- expense;
- deadline.

The compiler should generate recovery packets and deadline alerts.

### W10404 Producer, Broker, MGA, And Wholesale Workflow

The broker/MGA surface should compile:

- submission completeness;
- market selection;
- carrier appetite match;
- quote comparison;
- subjectivities;
- bind order;
- certificate/evidence request;
- commission statement;
- E&O risk candidate;
- client explanation.

This lets Kolm sell into distribution, not only carriers.

### W10405 Regulatory Filing And Market Conduct Packet

Regulators and compliance teams need evidence:

- filing form;
- rate/rule page;
- actuarial memo;
- model version;
- consumer impact analysis;
- complaint sample;
- claim handling sample;
- producer conduct sample;
- adverse decision explanations;
- remediation plan.

The packet should be exportable by jurisdiction and examination scope.

### W10406 Insurance K-Score

Invent `Insurance K-Score`:

- `submission_completeness`;
- `underwriting_consistency`;
- `claims_cycle_time`;
- `coverage_evidence_quality`;
- `reserve_stability`;
- `consumer_explanation_quality`;
- `adverse_outcome_integrity`;
- `fraud_precision`;
- `actuarial_model_replayability`;
- `regulator_export_readiness`;
- `reinsurance_reconciliation_accuracy`;
- `data_minimization`.

This score ties AI value to insurance metrics carriers already track.

### W10407 Insurance Distillation Moat

Distill small insurance assistants from carrier-specific evidence:

- appetite triage;
- claim completeness;
- coverage issue candidate extraction;
- adjuster note summarization;
- reserve rationale drafting;
- policy-change comparison;
- broker quote comparison;
- regulatory response drafting.

The moat comes from private decision traces, approved explanations, and
regulator feedback, not from public policy PDFs.

### W10408 Insurance Clean Room

The clean room should support model improvement while protecting consumers:

- de-identify claimants and insureds;
- preserve line, peril, jurisdiction, and exposure buckets;
- suppress small cells;
- bind use to actuarial or operational purpose;
- exclude unfair proxy features;
- retain audit logs;
- support carrier-controlled deletion;
- measure re-identification risk.

This is essential for multi-carrier benchmarks and reinsurance analytics.

### W10409 Consumer Notice And Explanation Compiler

The explanation compiler should produce:

- what decision was made;
- what facts were used;
- what policy or rule applied;
- what model contributed;
- what data can be corrected;
- what review or appeal exists;
- who made the decision;
- when it was made.

Explanations must be plain language, jurisdiction-aware, and reviewable.

### W10410 Insurance Benchmark Suite

Fixtures:

- incomplete commercial submission;
- homeowner water loss with coverage issue;
- auto claim with repair estimate conflict;
- cyber claim with policy exclusion candidate;
- workers compensation injury note;
- reinsurance bordereau mismatch;
- rate model drift sample;
- adverse action explanation;
- SIU false-positive trap;
- policy endorsement diff.

Metrics should include evidence completeness, human review agreement, cycle time,
consumer explanation quality, and false-positive harm.

### W10411 Insurance Marketplace Labels

Labels:

- `acord-mapped`;
- `carrier-api-bound`;
- `policy-form-versioned`;
- `coverage-review-required`;
- `siu-candidate-only`;
- `actuary-review-required`;
- `naic-ai-packet-ready`;
- `consumer-explanation-ready`;
- `reinsurance-replayable`;
- `market-conduct-exportable`;
- `no-final-adverse-action`.

Labels are computed from receipt evidence.

### W10412 Insurance Dashboard Contract

Dashboard:

- submission defects;
- quote cycle time;
- referral queue;
- claim completeness;
- coverage issue candidates;
- reserve drift;
- SIU candidates;
- adverse consumer outcome queue;
- model drift;
- regulator packet readiness;
- reinsurance mismatches;
- consumer notice backlog.

This should be an operating console for insurance evidence, not an AI novelty
dashboard.

### W10413 Insurance Implementation Sequence

Build order:

1. `InsuranceIR` and `InsuranceEvidenceReceipt`.
2. ACORD and carrier data binder.
3. Submission completeness and underwriting triage.
4. FNOL and claim evidence compiler.
5. Coverage issue candidate boundary.
6. NAIC AI governance packet.
7. Actuarial model binder.
8. Insurance K-Score.
9. Reinsurance packet.
10. Insurance dashboard.

First sellable module: submission and claim completeness with consumer-impact
scope.

### W10414 Insurance Smoke Test

Synthetic smoke:

1. Load a commercial property submission.
2. Bind ACORD-like fields, loss runs, locations, and forms.
3. Detect missing roof age and prior loss documentation.
4. Create underwriter referral packet.
5. Create a water-loss FNOL packet.
6. Extract coverage issue candidates.
7. Draft customer information request.
8. Run adverse consumer outcome sentinel.
9. Compile `InsuranceEvidenceReceipt`.
10. Confirm no final coverage or underwriting decision is emitted.

Passing state:

- missing evidence correct;
- consumer impact visible;
- coverage conclusion blocked;
- SIU language scoped;
- receipt hash stable.

### W10415 Insurance Readiness Gate

Ready only if:

- transaction type is explicit;
- policy/form version is explicit;
- jurisdiction is explicit;
- data lineage exists;
- human authority exists;
- consumer impact is tracked;
- model purpose and validation exist;
- adverse action boundary exists;
- no final regulated decision is automated without authority.

### W10416 Insurance Claim Honesty Gate

Blocked without proof:

- "AI underwriter";
- "AI adjuster";
- "coverage decided";
- "fraud detected";
- "regulator compliant";
- "unbiased pricing";
- "actuarially sound";
- "claim approved";
- "claim denied";
- "bad-faith safe."

Allowed:

- "submission evidence compiled";
- "coverage issue candidate";
- "claim completeness scored";
- "consumer explanation drafted";
- "actuarial model evidence packet";
- "regulator export prepared."

### W10417 Insurance Outcome Warranty Scope

Warrant only operational metrics at first:

- lower submission defect rate;
- faster FNOL packet creation;
- lower rekeying;
- faster regulator evidence assembly;
- fewer reinsurance reconciliation defects;
- improved explanation completeness.

Do not warranty loss ratio, reserve adequacy, or claim outcome until actuarial
study design and authority are locked.

### W10418 Ultimate Insurance Objective

The final state:

> Kolm turns insurance AI from opaque underwriting and claims suggestions into
> replayable insurance evidence artifacts that know policies, forms, losses,
> models, consumers, producers, actuaries, reinsurers, regulators, and review
> state.

This unlocks:

- underwriting triage;
- claims acceleration;
- coverage review packets;
- consumer explanations;
- actuarial governance;
- market conduct readiness;
- reinsurance reconciliation;
- broker workflow;
- MGA operations;
- insurance AI compliance.

Insurance, claims, actuarial, and reinsurance sources:

- NAIC Model Bulletin on Use of Artificial Intelligence Systems by Insurers: https://content.naic.org/sites/default/files/inline-files/2023-12-4%20Model%20Bulletin_Adopted_0.pdf
- NAIC Insurance Data Security Model Law brief: https://content.naic.org/sites/default/files/government-affairs-brief-data-security-model-law.pdf
- NAIC Model Laws, Regulations, Guidelines and Other Resources: https://content.naic.org/prod_serv_model_laws.htm
- ACORD Property and Casualty Data Standards: https://www-dev.acord.org/standards-architecture/acord-data-standards/Property_Casualty_Data_Standards
- ACORD Global Reinsurance and Large Commercial Data Standards: https://www-dev.acord.org/standards-architecture/acord-data-standards/Global_Reinsurance_Data_Standards
- ACORD Forms Portal: https://formsportal.acord.org/Home
- Actuarial Standards Board ASOP No. 56 Modeling: https://www.actuarialstandardsboard.org/asops/modeling-3/

## Utility Grid, Energy Data, Demand Response, And Reliability Evidence Compiler Layer

Energy and utility AI is not primarily a chatbot problem. It is a reliability,
meter data, customer consent, demand response, DER, outage, interconnection,
forecasting, and compliance evidence problem. Generic AI can be useful only if it
does not blur operational boundaries or critical infrastructure obligations.

The category is not "AI for utilities." The category is grid and customer energy
evidence compilation. Kolm should produce artifacts that help utilities,
aggregators, energy service providers, regulators, large loads, and customers
coordinate actions without losing telemetry lineage, consent, reliability scope,
or control authority.

### W10420 EnergyGridEvidenceReceipt Schema

Invent `EnergyGridEvidenceReceipt`.

Required fields:

- `asset_ref`: meter, feeder, substation, DER, building, load, generator,
  storage asset, EV charger, or control system;
- `customer_ref`: customer, account, premise, or pseudonymized energy data
  subject;
- `market_context`: tariff, rate, program, ISO/RTO, utility, aggregator, or
  demand response event;
- `grid_context`: topology, constraint, outage, reliability state, forecast,
  or operating interval;
- `telemetry_sources`: AMI, SCADA, DERMS, EMS, OMS, weather, market, building
  management, or customer device source;
- `control_scope`: advisory, customer-authorized, operator-authorized, automated
  control, or blocked;
- `consent_scope`: customer data authorization, retention, third-party access,
  and revocation;
- `reliability_scope`: NERC, CIP, local reliability, cybersecurity, and human
  operator review state where applicable;
- `ai_assistance`: forecast, event recommendation, outage summary, rate
  explanation, DER dispatch candidate, or anomaly candidate;
- `artifact_hash`: deterministic hash of the replayable packet.

The receipt must never hide whether an AI output is advisory or control-capable.

### W10421 EnergyGridIR

Create `EnergyGridIR` with nodes:

- Customer;
- Premise;
- Meter;
- Circuit;
- Feeder;
- Substation;
- Generator;
- DER;
- Battery;
- EV charger;
- Load;
- Demand response event;
- Tariff;
- Rate;
- Outage;
- Work order;
- Forecast;
- Market interval;
- Operator;
- Consent grant;
- Reliability requirement;
- Cyber asset.

Edges:

- serves;
- measures;
- injects;
- consumes;
- curtailed by;
- dispatched by;
- forecast by;
- affected by outage;
- restored by;
- authorized by customer;
- reviewed by operator;
- constrained by reliability rule;
- settled under tariff.

This IR lets Kolm span customer energy data and operational grid evidence
without confusing their authority boundaries.

### W10422 NERC Reliability And CIP Evidence Binder

For bulk power and critical infrastructure contexts, the binder should preserve:

- reliability standard family;
- requirement;
- asset classification;
- responsible entity;
- evidence sample;
- operator action;
- cyber asset scope;
- access control event;
- change event;
- incident candidate;
- mitigation state;
- audit package.

Kolm should not claim NERC compliance. It should compile evidence that the
responsible entity can review and use in compliance workflows.

### W10423 FERC/ERO Reliability Workflow Packet

The reliability packet should connect:

- standard development or approval context;
- FERC order or docket reference where applicable;
- NERC standard reference;
- regional entity;
- reliability risk;
- proposed mitigation;
- affected assets;
- operator training;
- evidence retained;
- exception or self-report workflow.

This creates an enterprise-grade reliability evidence surface, not just an alert
summarizer.

### W10424 SCADA, EMS, OMS, And OT Telemetry Boundary

The telemetry boundary must separate observation from control.

Allowed by default:

- summarize alarms;
- classify telemetry quality;
- detect stale readings;
- correlate outage tickets;
- identify missing tags;
- draft operator notes;
- prepare maintenance packet.

Blocked without operator-approved control scope:

- breaker operation;
- relay setting change;
- load shed;
- generation dispatch;
- DER dispatch;
- restoration switching;
- safety tag clearance.

Every artifact must carry its control scope.

### W10425 DER, VPP, And Interconnection Compiler

DER and virtual power plant operations require evidence:

- asset type;
- inverter capability;
- interconnection agreement;
- site capacity;
- telemetry availability;
- customer authorization;
- aggregator enrollment;
- export limit;
- dispatch constraint;
- settlement method;
- fallback behavior.

The compiler should make DER resources auditable and dispatch candidates
reviewable.

### W10426 OpenADR Demand Response Event Compiler

The OpenADR compiler should bind:

- event ID;
- virtual top node;
- resource;
- signal name;
- signal type;
- interval;
- target;
- opt-in/opt-out;
- baseline;
- response;
- settlement;
- customer override;
- event replay.

The output should state whether a customer, aggregator, or operator authorized
the response.

### W10427 Green Button And Customer Energy Data Binder

Customer energy data is a trust wedge.

The binder should compile:

- usage interval;
- meter;
- premise;
- reading type;
- unit;
- tariff context;
- authorization;
- third-party app;
- retention;
- revocation;
- export packet;
- data-quality flag.

Kolm can help customers and energy service providers use meter data without
turning it into uncontrolled surveillance.

### W10428 Customer Consent And Energy Data Minimization

The consent membrane should show:

- what energy data is requested;
- purpose;
- third party;
- duration;
- revocation method;
- aggregation state;
- premise sensitivity;
- occupancy inference risk;
- export log;
- deletion state.

Meter data can reveal occupancy and behavior. The product must make that risk
first-class.

### W10429 Outage, Restoration, And Crew Evidence Compiler

Outage operations need:

- outage start;
- affected meters;
- feeder;
- probable cause;
- crew assignment;
- estimated restoration;
- switching plan candidate;
- customer communications;
- critical customer flag;
- restoration timestamp;
- nested outage state;
- post-event review.

The compiler can draft summaries and customer communications, but operator and
crew safety authority stay human-controlled.

### W10430 Load Forecast, Flexibility, And Capacity Planning Binder

The planning binder should compile:

- historical load;
- weather;
- calendar;
- DER forecast;
- EV charging forecast;
- large-load interconnection;
- demand response availability;
- capacity margin;
- uncertainty band;
- forecast drift;
- scenario;
- planning decision.

AI forecasts become useful when every input and assumption is replayable.

### W10431 Rate, Tariff, And Bill Explanation Boundary

The rate/tariff explainer should:

- cite tariff schedule;
- explain interval usage;
- explain demand charge;
- explain time-of-use period;
- explain rider or surcharge;
- show estimated savings action;
- identify uncertainty;
- route disputes to utility process.

It must not give legal or regulatory advice. It prepares a customer explanation
packet.

### W10432 Energy Market Settlement And Measurement Packet

For aggregators and large loads:

- event participation;
- baseline;
- meter data;
- dispatch instruction;
- curtailment;
- performance;
- non-performance reason;
- price;
- settlement;
- dispute state.

This lets Kolm turn flexibility into auditable settlement artifacts.

### W10433 Grid Event And Disturbance Replay Capsule

The replay capsule should include:

- topology snapshot;
- telemetry window;
- alarm sequence;
- operator notes;
- weather;
- market condition;
- DER response;
- outage tickets;
- control actions;
- reliability requirements;
- post-event analysis.

The capsule supports training, audit, and incident review without relying on
scattered logs.

### W10434 Cyber-Physical Anomaly Guard

The anomaly guard should detect candidates:

- telemetry spoofing;
- stale data;
- unexpected DER behavior;
- impossible meter interval;
- command mismatch;
- outage ticket mismatch;
- load spike;
- voltage anomaly;
- baseline manipulation;
- suspicious third-party access.

It should create a review packet. It should not label an event as an attack
without authorized investigation.

### W10435 Energy K-Score

Invent `Energy K-Score`:

- `forecast_accuracy`;
- `telemetry_lineage`;
- `outage_packet_completeness`;
- `demand_response_performance`;
- `customer_consent_integrity`;
- `control_boundary_integrity`;
- `operator_review_agreement`;
- `settlement_replayability`;
- `reliability_evidence_readiness`;
- `privacy_risk_reduction`;
- `carbon_or_flexibility_claim_scope`;
- `restoration_communication_quality`.

This score ties AI to utility outcomes while keeping safety boundaries explicit.

### W10436 Energy Distillation Moat

Distill small energy assistants from utility evidence:

- outage summarizer;
- restoration communication drafter;
- tariff explainer;
- DR event response predictor;
- meter anomaly detector;
- DER dispatch candidate ranker;
- interconnection completeness checker;
- reliability evidence compiler.

The moat is telemetry plus operational review, not generic energy knowledge.

### W10437 Grid Data Clean Room

The clean room should:

- aggregate meter data where possible;
- suppress small groups;
- bind customer purpose;
- separate customer data from OT telemetry;
- prevent occupancy inference exports;
- record derived forecasts;
- support utility deletion and retention policy;
- support regulator audit.

This is necessary before multi-utility benchmarks or cross-customer learning.

### W10438 Carbon, Renewable, And Flexibility Claim Gate

Energy claims are easy to overstate. Gate:

- renewable matching;
- carbon reduction;
- avoided emissions;
- flexibility delivered;
- load shifted;
- peak reduction;
- resilience improvement;
- reliability contribution.

Each claim needs method, baseline, time window, region, meter data, and
uncertainty.

### W10439 Interconnection And Queue Evidence Packet

Interconnection workflows need:

- application;
- site;
- equipment;
- study status;
- feeder capacity;
- protection review;
- export limit;
- cost estimate;
- agreement;
- milestone;
- withdrawal or approval state.

This is a strong wedge for solar, storage, EV charging, and large-load markets.

### W10440 Utility Marketplace Labels

Labels:

- `green-button-bound`;
- `openadr-bound`;
- `der-evidence-ready`;
- `operator-review-required`;
- `control-blocked`;
- `customer-consent-bound`;
- `nerc-evidence-packet`;
- `outage-replayable`;
- `settlement-replayable`;
- `privacy-risk-scored`;
- `carbon-claim-scoped`.

Labels must be computed from receipt evidence.

### W10441 Utility Benchmark Suite

Fixtures:

- demand response event with opt-out;
- Green Button usage export;
- tariff explanation with time-of-use charge;
- outage communication packet;
- DER interconnection missing field;
- meter anomaly with benign explanation;
- settlement baseline dispute;
- cyber-physical anomaly candidate;
- carbon claim with wrong baseline;
- reliability evidence packet.

Metrics:

- forecast error;
- false anomaly rate;
- event replayability;
- customer explanation quality;
- consent integrity;
- control boundary correctness.

### W10442 Utility Dashboard Contract

Dashboard:

- load forecast drift;
- demand response events;
- customer opt-outs;
- meter data quality;
- DER dispatch candidates;
- outage packet completeness;
- restoration communication queue;
- settlement disputes;
- consent revocations;
- control-boundary violations;
- reliability evidence readiness.

The dashboard should be quiet, operational, and role-scoped.

### W10443 Utility Implementation Sequence

Build order:

1. `EnergyGridIR` and `EnergyGridEvidenceReceipt`.
2. Green Button/customer energy data binder.
3. OpenADR event compiler.
4. Outage and restoration packet.
5. DER/interconnection packet.
6. Control-boundary membrane.
7. Reliability evidence binder.
8. Energy K-Score.
9. Settlement replay capsule.
10. Utility dashboard.

First sellable module: customer energy data plus tariff explanations and demand
response event replay.

### W10444 Utility Smoke Test

Synthetic smoke:

1. Load a Green Button-like meter interval export.
2. Bind customer consent.
3. Create a time-of-use bill explanation.
4. Create an OpenADR-like demand response event.
5. Record opt-out and response interval.
6. Compile `EnergyGridEvidenceReceipt`.
7. Generate settlement candidate.
8. Run control-boundary guard.
9. Run privacy inference risk check.
10. Confirm no grid control action is emitted.

Passing state:

- customer consent visible;
- meter data lineage stable;
- DR event replayable;
- control action blocked;
- carbon or savings claim scoped.

### W10445 Utility Readiness Gate

Ready only if:

- asset or customer context is explicit;
- data lineage exists;
- consent state exists for customer data;
- operator authority exists for control-capable contexts;
- event or tariff version exists;
- reliability scope exists when relevant;
- privacy inference risk is assessed;
- no operational control is automated without authority.

### W10446 Utility Claim Honesty Gate

Blocked without proof:

- "grid optimized";
- "NERC compliant";
- "carbon neutral";
- "guaranteed savings";
- "automatic dispatch";
- "prevents outages";
- "detects attacks";
- "settlement correct";
- "customer consent guaranteed."

Allowed:

- "meter data compiled";
- "demand response event replayable";
- "tariff explanation drafted";
- "outage packet prepared";
- "DER dispatch candidate";
- "reliability evidence packet prepared."

### W10447 Utility Outcome Warranty Scope

Warrant only bounded operational improvements first:

- fewer missing meter intervals;
- faster tariff explanation generation;
- faster outage summary creation;
- lower demand response reconciliation defects;
- lower interconnection application defects;
- better consent audit completeness.

Do not warranty outage prevention, emissions reductions, or market revenue
without locked baselines and independent measurement.

### W10448 Ultimate Utility Objective

The final state:

> Kolm turns utility AI from unsafe recommendations into grid and customer
> energy evidence artifacts that know meters, tariffs, DERs, demand response,
> outages, operators, consent, reliability scope, settlement, and replay state.

This unlocks:

- customer energy data products;
- demand response proof;
- DER/VPP operations;
- outage communications;
- tariff explanation;
- interconnection workflows;
- reliability evidence;
- settlement reconciliation;
- carbon and flexibility claim governance.

Utility grid, energy data, reliability, and demand response sources:

- NERC Reliability Standards: https://www.nerc.com/standards/reliability-standards
- FERC Office of Electric Reliability: https://ferc.gov/office-electric-reliability-oer
- OpenADR Specification: https://www.openadr.org/specification
- OpenADR FAQ: https://www.openadr.org/faq
- Green Button Alliance Technical FAQ: https://www.greenbuttonalliance.org/faqs-technical
- Department of Energy Orange Button Solar Data Standard: https://www.energy.gov/cmei/systems/orange-buttonr-solar-data-standard
- NIST Smart Grid Program: https://www.nist.gov/programs-projects/smart-grid

## Telecom, Network API, OSS/BSS, Edge, And Service Assurance Compiler Layer

Telecom is a natural Kolm expansion because operators are turning networks into
programmable platforms while still running massive OSS/BSS stacks, regulated
customer systems, field operations, service assurance workflows, and partner
interconnect processes. Generic AI agents are dangerous here because they can
confuse customer care, billing, network configuration, service-impact analysis,
and control-plane authority.

The category is not "autonomous network AI." The category is telecom service
evidence compilation. Kolm should produce signed artifacts that know service
inventory, network topology, customer products, incidents, SLAs, API calls,
network configuration, edge placement, and operator approval state.

### W10450 TelecomServiceEvidenceReceipt Schema

Invent `TelecomServiceEvidenceReceipt`.

Required fields:

- `customer_ref`: account, enterprise, subscriber, device, SIM, or pseudonym;
- `service_ref`: product, service, slice, circuit, API subscription, edge
  workload, or partner service;
- `network_ref`: access network, transport, core, cloud, edge, region, cell,
  node, interface, or logical topology;
- `oss_bss_context`: order, inventory, trouble ticket, billing, assurance,
  provisioning, and partner workflow;
- `api_context`: TM Forum Open API, CAMARA/Open Gateway API, MEF LSO API,
  NETCONF/YANG, proprietary API, or custom adapter;
- `configuration_context`: candidate config, running config, model, policy,
  validation, rollback, and approval state;
- `assurance_context`: alarm, KPI, SLA, QoS, QoE, incident, root-cause
  candidate, and customer impact;
- `control_scope`: read-only, advisory, change-candidate, operator-approved, or
  automated under policy;
- `privacy_scope`: customer, device, location, consent, retention, lawful
  request, and partner disclosure state;
- `artifact_hash`: deterministic hash of the replayable packet.

The receipt must make it impossible to mistake an AI recommendation for an
authorized network change.

### W10451 TelecomIR

Create `TelecomIR` with nodes:

- Customer;
- Subscriber;
- Device;
- SIM/eSIM;
- Product;
- Service;
- Order;
- Inventory item;
- Circuit;
- Slice;
- Cell;
- Core function;
- Edge site;
- API subscription;
- Trouble ticket;
- Alarm;
- SLA;
- Configuration;
- Operator;
- Partner;
- Consent grant.

Edges:

- ordered;
- provisioned by;
- depends on;
- impacts;
- configured by;
- assured by;
- billed under;
- exposed through API;
- authorized by subscriber;
- changed by operator;
- rolled back by;
- escalated to.

This IR is the core that lets Kolm connect OSS, BSS, network operations, and
developer-facing network APIs.

### W10452 TM Forum ODA And Open API Binder

The TM Forum binder should map:

- product catalog;
- customer;
- account;
- order;
- service inventory;
- resource inventory;
- trouble ticket;
- appointment;
- usage;
- billing;
- partner management;
- SLA;
- event notification.

The binder should preserve API version, conformance status, request/response
hash, and field-level mapping. Telecom buyers care about transformation without
breaking legacy stacks. This makes Kolm useful in modernization projects before
any autonomous networking claim.

### W10453 MEF LSO Partner Automation Binder

The MEF LSO binder should compile cross-provider service evidence:

- quote;
- product order;
- service order;
- service inventory;
- trouble ticket;
- appointment;
- performance;
- serviceability;
- partner identity;
- handoff;
- SLA;
- dispute state.

The wedge is multi-provider enterprise connectivity. Kolm can become the
evidence layer that shows which provider, partner, or system owns each step.

### W10454 GSMA Open Gateway And CAMARA API Compiler

Network APIs create a new platform surface for Kolm.

Compile:

- API family;
- API version;
- developer;
- application;
- user or device consent;
- telco operator;
- purpose;
- token context;
- request;
- response;
- error;
- rate limit;
- billing event;
- privacy classification.

For location, SIM swap, device status, carrier billing, QoD, edge discovery, or
other network APIs, the product must preserve subscriber consent and operator
policy. This is a critical developer trust wedge.

### W10455 NETCONF/YANG Configuration Evidence Binder

Configuration changes require formal evidence:

- target device;
- YANG model;
- intended config;
- candidate config;
- validation output;
- diff;
- dependency;
- maintenance window;
- operator approval;
- commit;
- rollback;
- post-change test.

Kolm should default to generating change packets, not pushing changes. Control
scope must be explicit.

### W10456 Service Assurance And Incident Compiler

Assurance packets should compile:

- alarm storm;
- topology dependency;
- customer impact;
- SLA impact;
- KPI degradation;
- correlated events;
- suspected root cause;
- remediation candidate;
- operator action;
- post-incident review.

The artifact should distinguish root-cause candidate from confirmed cause. That
distinction is essential in network operations.

### W10457 SLA, QoS, And QoE Evidence Layer

The SLA layer should track:

- service class;
- objective;
- measurement source;
- measurement interval;
- threshold;
- violation candidate;
- exception;
- customer credit candidate;
- remediation;
- notification;
- dispute state.

QoS/QoE claims must include measurement method and customer impact. Otherwise AI
will overpromise service quality.

### W10458 Edge Placement And Network-Aware Runtime Compiler

Kolm can make its own product stronger by using telecom evidence for edge
placement:

- user/device region;
- latency budget;
- bandwidth need;
- compute demand;
- data residency;
- edge site availability;
- network API availability;
- failover path;
- cost;
- privacy boundary.

This is additive to Kolm's device and cloud runtime story: the artifact can run
on device, in enterprise, in cloud, or at telecom edge with proof.

### W10459 Network Slice And Enterprise Private Network Packet

For 5G slices and private networks:

- slice/service profile;
- enterprise tenant;
- coverage;
- QoS class;
- latency target;
- reliability target;
- device enrollment;
- policy;
- lifecycle state;
- SLA;
- security boundary.

The packet should support manufacturing, logistics, healthcare, and public sector
private-network deployments without hiding operator authority.

### W10460 Subscriber Privacy And Location Consent Membrane

Telecom data can expose location, identity, communication behavior, and billing.
The membrane should preserve:

- subscriber consent;
- enterprise authority;
- legal basis;
- purpose;
- retention;
- revocation;
- partner disclosure;
- data minimization;
- location precision;
- device identifier scope;
- audit log.

No location or device API artifact should exist without this membrane.

### W10461 Lawful Request And Safety Boundary

The product must separate ordinary enterprise workflows from lawful request,
emergency, abuse, and safety workflows.

The compiler can prepare evidence packets, but it must not:

- decide legality of a request;
- disclose subscriber data;
- bypass consent;
- execute intercept;
- override safety procedures;
- suppress required audit.

The output is "review packet for authorized workflow."

### W10462 OSS/BSS Migration And Reconciliation Compiler

Telecom transformation projects fail on inventory and order reconciliation.
The compiler should compare:

- product catalog;
- service inventory;
- network resource inventory;
- billing account;
- active order;
- trouble ticket;
- partner record;
- configuration state.

It should emit mismatch packets and migration readiness scores. This is a strong
enterprise entry point.

### W10463 Field Operations And Workforce Packet

Field operations need:

- work order;
- site;
- asset;
- access requirement;
- safety note;
- parts;
- skill;
- appointment;
- outage impact;
- customer notification;
- completion evidence.

Kolm can compile field packets from tickets, inventory, and network state so
dispatchers and technicians avoid context hunting.

### W10464 Telecom Fraud, Abuse, And Revenue Assurance Guard

Guard candidates:

- SIM swap anomaly;
- subscription fraud;
- roaming abuse;
- premium-rate abuse;
- device identity anomaly;
- carrier billing dispute;
- revenue leakage;
- usage mismatch;
- partner settlement mismatch.

Claims must stay "candidate" until fraud, revenue assurance, or legal review
confirms them.

### W10465 Telecom K-Score

Invent `Telecom K-Score`:

- `order_fallout_reduction`;
- `inventory_reconciliation_accuracy`;
- `ticket_triage_quality`;
- `sla_evidence_completeness`;
- `operator_review_agreement`;
- `configuration_replayability`;
- `rollback_readiness`;
- `subscriber_privacy_integrity`;
- `network_api_consent_integrity`;
- `partner_settlement_accuracy`;
- `edge_placement_latency_gain`;
- `incident_mttx_improvement`.

This ties Kolm to operator metrics instead of vague autonomy.

### W10466 Telecom Distillation Moat

Distill small assistants from operator-reviewed traces:

- order fallout classifier;
- inventory mismatch explainer;
- trouble ticket summarizer;
- alarm correlation assistant;
- SLA violation packet builder;
- config diff reviewer;
- field work packet drafter;
- Open Gateway API policy assistant.

The moat is closed-loop operator review across millions of tickets, changes,
orders, and alarms.

### W10467 Network API Developer Experience Packet

Developer-facing network APIs need evidence:

- API capability;
- availability by market/operator;
- consent flow;
- auth profile;
- error behavior;
- sandbox result;
- production result;
- billing event;
- SLA;
- privacy notice.

Kolm can make network APIs trustworthy for AI apps by compiling API behavior into
replayable developer artifacts.

### W10468 Telecom Data Clean Room

Clean room controls:

- aggregate subscriber data;
- suppress small cell and location precision;
- separate network telemetry from subscriber identity;
- bind purpose;
- record partner exports;
- prevent unauthorized re-identification;
- support operator deletion/retention;
- support regulator audit.

This enables multi-operator benchmarks without exposing subscriber behavior.

### W10469 Autonomous Network Claim Gate

Blocked without proof:

- "self-healing network";
- "autonomous operations";
- "zero-touch change";
- "guaranteed SLA";
- "detects fraud";
- "location privacy guaranteed";
- "network optimized";
- "5G slice assured";
- "edge latency guaranteed."

Allowed:

- "incident evidence compiled";
- "config change packet prepared";
- "network API call replayable";
- "subscriber consent bound";
- "SLA violation candidate";
- "operator review required."

### W10470 Telecom Benchmark Suite

Fixtures:

- failed service order;
- mismatched inventory record;
- alarm storm with false root cause;
- SLA violation candidate;
- NETCONF/YANG config diff;
- Open Gateway location API with consent;
- carrier billing dispute;
- MEF LSO trouble ticket handoff;
- field work packet;
- edge placement scenario.

Metrics:

- root-cause candidate precision;
- order fallout classification;
- inventory reconciliation;
- privacy boundary correctness;
- config rollback readiness;
- operator agreement.

### W10471 Telecom Marketplace Labels

Labels:

- `tmf-open-api-bound`;
- `oda-mapped`;
- `mef-lso-bound`;
- `camara-api-bound`;
- `netconf-yang-bound`;
- `operator-review-required`;
- `control-blocked`;
- `subscriber-consent-bound`;
- `sla-candidate-only`;
- `edge-placement-scored`;
- `migration-reconciliation-ready`.

Labels must be evidence-derived.

### W10472 Telecom Dashboard Contract

Dashboard:

- order fallout;
- inventory mismatches;
- trouble ticket triage;
- alarm correlations;
- SLA candidates;
- config changes awaiting review;
- rollback readiness;
- network API errors;
- subscriber consent gaps;
- partner handoff delays;
- edge placement candidates;
- revenue leakage candidates.

The dashboard should be built for NOC/SOC/OSS/BSS operators, not marketing.

### W10473 Telecom Implementation Sequence

Build order:

1. `TelecomIR` and `TelecomServiceEvidenceReceipt`.
2. TM Forum Open API binder.
3. Trouble ticket and service assurance compiler.
4. Inventory reconciliation compiler.
5. NETCONF/YANG change packet.
6. CAMARA/Open Gateway API evidence packet.
7. Subscriber consent membrane.
8. Telecom K-Score.
9. Edge placement compiler.
10. Telecom dashboard.

First sellable module: order fallout plus inventory reconciliation, because it is
operationally valuable and lower risk than autonomous control.

### W10474 Telecom Smoke Test

Synthetic smoke:

1. Load customer, product, service, and resource inventory records.
2. Create a failed service order.
3. Add alarm and trouble ticket events.
4. Generate inventory mismatch packet.
5. Generate SLA candidate packet.
6. Create a NETCONF/YANG config diff candidate.
7. Bind operator approval requirement.
8. Compile `TelecomServiceEvidenceReceipt`.
9. Run subscriber privacy membrane.
10. Confirm no network change is committed.

Passing state:

- mismatch detected;
- customer impact scoped;
- SLA conclusion remains candidate;
- config commit blocked;
- receipt hash stable.

### W10475 Telecom Readiness Gate

Ready only if:

- customer or service context exists;
- inventory lineage exists;
- API version exists;
- operator authority exists for changes;
- subscriber consent exists for subscriber/device/location data;
- SLA measurement method exists;
- rollback plan exists for config changes;
- no network control action is automated without authority.

### W10476 Telecom Outcome Warranty Scope

Warrant only bounded operational improvements:

- lower order fallout;
- faster inventory reconciliation;
- faster ticket packet creation;
- fewer missing change details;
- improved SLA packet completeness;
- fewer partner handoff defects.

Do not warranty autonomous healing, SLA avoidance, or latency guarantees until
measurement, control authority, and operator acceptance are locked.

### W10477 Telecom Product Surface Integration

This layer improves Kolm's own product matrix:

- One AI API can expose network-aware routing where permitted.
- Compile/distill can train service-assurance assistants from operator review.
- Enterprise-to-device runtime can place artifacts on telecom edge, private 5G,
  or device with evidence.

It also creates a new buyer: operators and enterprise network teams that need AI
evidence before they trust automation.

### W10478 Ultimate Telecom Objective

The final state:

> Kolm turns telecom AI from unsafe autonomous-network claims into service
> evidence artifacts that know customers, services, network topology, OSS/BSS
> workflows, APIs, configurations, SLAs, subscriber consent, edge placement, and
> operator review state.

This unlocks:

- OSS/BSS modernization;
- service assurance;
- network API governance;
- edge runtime placement;
- private network operations;
- enterprise connectivity automation;
- SLA evidence;
- NOC/SOC acceleration;
- partner automation;
- network-aware AI applications.

Telecom, network API, OSS/BSS, and service assurance sources:

- TM Forum Open Digital Architecture: https://www.tmforum.org/open-digital-architecture/get-started/
- TM Forum REST/Open API integration toolkit: https://www.tmforum.org/resources/toolkit/agile-oss-rest/
- GSMA Open Gateway API descriptions: https://www.gsma.com/solutions-and-impact/gsma-open-gateway/gsma-open-gateway-api-descriptions/
- CAMARA API overview: https://camaraproject.org/api-overview/
- MEF Lifecycle Service Orchestration APIs: https://www.mef.net/service-automation/lso-apis/
- 3GPP specifications and technologies: https://www.3gpp.org/specifications-technologies
- RFC 6241 NETCONF: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6241
- RFC 7950 YANG 1.1: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7950

## Supply Chain, Procurement, Logistics, Customs, And Trade Evidence Compiler Layer

Global supply chains are artifact machines: purchase orders, supplier records,
invoices, packing lists, EPCIS events, certificates, shipping instructions,
bills of lading, customs declarations, warehouse events, quality inspections,
recalls, sanctions screenings, and payment terms. Generic AI fails here because
it summarizes documents while losing chain of custody, product identity, trade
compliance, supplier obligations, and exception ownership.

The category is not "AI procurement assistant" or "AI logistics copilot." The
category is trade and supply chain evidence compilation. Kolm should produce
signed artifacts that know product identifiers, suppliers, orders, shipments,
custody events, customs fields, certificates, exceptions, and review authority.

### W10480 SupplyChainEvidenceReceipt Schema

Invent `SupplyChainEvidenceReceipt`.

Required fields:

- `trade_party_refs`: buyer, seller, supplier, carrier, forwarder, broker,
  consignee, consignor, manufacturer, 3PL, warehouse, customs declarant, and
  financing party;
- `product_refs`: GTIN, SKU, lot, serial, batch, SSCC, EPC, HS code, country of
  origin, controlled goods flag, and certificate linkage;
- `transaction_context`: sourcing event, contract, purchase order, invoice,
  shipment, warehouse movement, customs filing, payment, return, or recall;
- `logistics_context`: container, transport document, bill of lading, waybill,
  route, port, terminal, vessel, flight, truck, rail, milestone, and exception;
- `visibility_events`: EPCIS/CBV-like what, when, where, why, how, sensor,
  custody, condition, and disposition events;
- `compliance_context`: sanctions, export controls, customs, forced labor,
  sustainability, safety, quality, and product certification boundaries;
- `human_authority`: procurement owner, trade compliance owner, logistics owner,
  broker, quality engineer, legal, finance, or regulator review;
- `artifact_hash`: deterministic hash of the replayable packet.

The receipt should compile evidence, not decide customs admissibility, sanctions
clearance, product safety, or legal compliance without authorized review.

### W10481 SupplyChainIR

Create `SupplyChainIR` with nodes:

- Party;
- Product;
- Identifier;
- Location;
- Facility;
- Order;
- Contract;
- Invoice;
- Shipment;
- Container;
- Transport document;
- Customs declaration;
- Certificate;
- Event;
- Exception;
- Sensor reading;
- Payment;
- Recall;
- Reviewer.

Edges:

- ordered from;
- manufactured at;
- packed into;
- shipped by;
- loaded on;
- crossed border;
- received at;
- inspected by;
- certified by;
- invoiced under;
- paid by;
- recalled by;
- blocked by compliance;
- reviewed by.

This IR makes supplier, logistics, finance, and compliance workflows operate on
one evidence graph instead of isolated document summaries.

### W10482 GS1 EPCIS/CBV Visibility Binder

The EPCIS binder should preserve:

- object event;
- aggregation event;
- transaction event;
- transformation event;
- association event;
- business step;
- disposition;
- read point;
- business location;
- source and destination;
- sensor data;
- certification reference.

This is a major product wedge because EPCIS already encodes supply chain
visibility. Kolm can compile AI decisions from event streams without inventing a
new tracking language.

### W10483 Product Identity, Lot, Serial, And Recall Compiler

The identity compiler should bind:

- GTIN/SKU;
- batch/lot;
- serial;
- expiration;
- manufacturing location;
- packaging hierarchy;
- quality status;
- affected shipment;
- downstream customer;
- recall class;
- notification state.

The system should produce recall evidence packets and blast-radius analysis,
never unreviewed recall decisions.

### W10484 Procurement Contract And Supplier Obligation Binder

Procurement evidence should link:

- solicitation;
- bid;
- supplier;
- award;
- contract;
- purchase order;
- delivery schedule;
- service level;
- quality clause;
- price;
- payment term;
- change order;
- obligation;
- dispute.

For public procurement, the binder should map to open contracting data where
possible. For private procurement, it should produce the same lifecycle evidence
without publishing sensitive terms.

### W10485 X12, UBL, Peppol, And EDI Translator

The translator should compile EDI and e-business documents into SupplyChainIR:

- purchase order;
- advance ship notice;
- invoice;
- remittance advice;
- warehouse shipping order;
- transportation carrier shipment status;
- customs-related messages;
- UBL order;
- UBL invoice;
- Peppol billing profile.

The product opportunity is not "replace EDI." It is make EDI explainable,
testable, replayable, and bridgeable to APIs and AI workflows.

### W10486 DCSA Bill Of Lading And Shipping Instructions Binder

The shipping binder should compile:

- shipping instructions;
- original bill of lading;
- sea waybill;
- eBL platform;
- carrier booking;
- container;
- vessel/voyage;
- port of loading;
- port of discharge;
- consignee;
- notify party;
- amendments;
- surrender;
- release;
- title-transfer state.

Bill of lading artifacts must preserve legal and operational boundaries. Kolm
can prepare evidence and detect inconsistencies, but cannot transfer title or
release cargo without authorized workflow.

### W10487 WCO Customs Data Model And Border Evidence Binder

The customs binder should map:

- declaration;
- goods item;
- HS code;
- origin;
- valuation;
- quantity;
- party;
- transport;
- license;
- permit;
- certificate;
- duty/tax;
- inspection;
- release;
- hold;
- correction.

The output is "customs packet ready for broker or agency review." It must not
claim admissibility, classification correctness, or duty certainty without
authority.

### W10488 Supplier Risk, ESG, And Forced-Labor Evidence Packet

Supplier risk packets should include:

- supplier identity;
- ownership;
- facility;
- geography;
- product;
- certificate;
- audit;
- forced-labor risk candidate;
- sanctions risk candidate;
- environmental claim;
- labor claim;
- remediation;
- reviewer.

Claims about ESG, forced labor, and sanctions require extremely tight evidence
scope. The system should compile flags and proof, not assert guilt.

### W10489 Cold Chain, Sensor, And Condition Evidence Layer

The condition layer should compile:

- temperature;
- humidity;
- shock;
- light exposure;
- door open;
- GPS;
- sensor calibration;
- logger identity;
- time window;
- threshold;
- excursion;
- affected lot;
- disposition candidate.

This is a strong vertical wedge for pharma, food, chemicals, and high-value
electronics.

### W10490 Warehouse, Yard, Port, And Terminal Event Compiler

Compile:

- receiving;
- putaway;
- pick;
- pack;
- load;
- cross-dock;
- container gate-in;
- container gate-out;
- yard location;
- demurrage/detention candidate;
- dwell time;
- exception;
- labor or equipment constraint.

The artifact should make physical operations auditable and explain why a
shipment is late or blocked.

### W10491 Trade Finance And Document Matching Packet

Trade finance evidence should link:

- purchase order;
- invoice;
- packing list;
- bill of lading;
- certificate of origin;
- inspection certificate;
- letter of credit;
- insurance certificate;
- payment instruction;
- discrepancy;
- bank review.

Kolm should detect document mismatches and generate review packets, not approve
payments or documentary credit compliance by itself.

### W10492 Exception Management And Control Tower Compiler

Exception packets should include:

- late shipment;
- missing document;
- customs hold;
- route disruption;
- quality hold;
- supplier delay;
- demand change;
- capacity constraint;
- weather disruption;
- port congestion;
- recommended action;
- owner;
- deadline.

The control tower becomes an evidence console rather than an alert flood.

### W10493 Sanctions, Export Control, And Restricted Goods Guard

Guardrails:

- party screening candidate;
- product classification candidate;
- destination risk;
- end-use risk;
- end-user risk;
- license requirement candidate;
- denied-party match confidence;
- jurisdiction;
- review owner;
- block or release authority.

The system must never silently clear restricted trade. It compiles review
evidence and blocks automation until authority exists.

### W10494 Supply Chain K-Score

Invent `Supply Chain K-Score`:

- `identifier_integrity`;
- `event_lineage`;
- `document_match_quality`;
- `customs_packet_completeness`;
- `exception_resolution_time`;
- `supplier_risk_evidence_quality`;
- `cold_chain_excursion_precision`;
- `recall_blast_radius_accuracy`;
- `EDI_translation_fidelity`;
- `human_review_agreement`;
- `trade_compliance_boundary_integrity`;
- `working_capital_cycle_gain`.

This score ties AI to measurable operations: fewer defects, faster exceptions,
cleaner customs packets, and lower rekeying.

### W10495 Supply Chain Distillation Moat

Distill assistants from reviewed operations:

- supplier onboarding reviewer;
- PO/invoice/ASN matcher;
- customs document checker;
- late-shipment triage assistant;
- recall scope extractor;
- cold-chain excursion classifier;
- EDI mapping debugger;
- trade finance discrepancy assistant.

The moat is not public trade documents. It is private exception resolution
traces, supplier history, route outcomes, broker corrections, and compliance
review feedback.

### W10496 Supply Chain Clean Room

The clean room should:

- separate supplier identity from benchmark data;
- aggregate lane performance;
- suppress sensitive volumes and prices;
- preserve product class and event type;
- bind use to operations or compliance purpose;
- record derived artifacts;
- support supplier/customer deletion constraints;
- prevent competitive leakage.

This enables multi-company benchmarking without revealing commercial secrets.

### W10497 Supply Chain Benchmark Suite

Fixtures:

- PO/invoice mismatch;
- ASN/container mismatch;
- missing certificate of origin;
- customs HS code candidate conflict;
- cold-chain temperature excursion;
- EPCIS event gap;
- bill of lading amendment;
- forced-labor risk false-positive trap;
- trade finance document discrepancy;
- recall blast-radius scenario.

Metrics:

- document match precision;
- missing evidence recall;
- false hold rate;
- exception owner accuracy;
- review agreement;
- compliance boundary correctness.

### W10498 Marketplace Labels

Labels:

- `gs1-epcis-bound`;
- `x12-bound`;
- `ubl-bound`;
- `peppol-bound`;
- `dcsa-ebl-bound`;
- `wco-customs-bound`;
- `cold-chain-evidence`;
- `sanctions-review-required`;
- `trade-compliance-review-required`;
- `recall-candidate-only`;
- `document-match-replayable`;
- `supplier-risk-candidate`.

Labels must be evidence-derived.

### W10499 Dashboard Contract

Dashboard:

- supplier onboarding defects;
- PO/invoice mismatches;
- ASN defects;
- shipment exceptions;
- customs holds;
- missing certificates;
- cold-chain excursions;
- recall blast radius;
- EDI translation failures;
- sanctions review queue;
- demurrage/detention risk;
- trade finance discrepancies.

This is a control tower for evidence, not a chat interface.

### W10500 Implementation Sequence

Build order:

1. `SupplyChainIR` and `SupplyChainEvidenceReceipt`.
2. GS1 EPCIS/CBV binder.
3. EDI/X12/UBL/Peppol translator.
4. DCSA bill-of-lading binder.
5. WCO customs binder.
6. Exception management compiler.
7. Trade compliance guard.
8. Supply Chain K-Score.
9. Recall and cold-chain packets.
10. Dashboard.

First sellable module: PO/invoice/ASN/document matching plus exception packets.

### W10501 Smoke Test

Synthetic smoke:

1. Load supplier, product, PO, ASN, invoice, shipment, and container records.
2. Add EPCIS-like shipping and receiving events.
3. Add a bill-of-lading amendment.
4. Add customs declaration fields.
5. Detect invoice quantity mismatch.
6. Detect missing certificate.
7. Compile `SupplyChainEvidenceReceipt`.
8. Run sanctions/export guard.
9. Produce exception owner and review packet.
10. Confirm no customs or sanctions clearance is emitted.

Passing state:

- document mismatch detected;
- event lineage stable;
- compliance conclusion blocked;
- exception owner present;
- receipt hash stable.

### W10502 Readiness Gate

Ready only if:

- party identity is explicit;
- product identity is explicit;
- transaction type is explicit;
- document version exists;
- custody/event lineage exists;
- compliance scope exists;
- reviewer authority exists;
- high-risk trade decisions stay blocked without review.

### W10503 Claim Honesty Gate

Blocked without proof:

- "customs compliant";
- "sanctions cleared";
- "fraud free";
- "sustainable";
- "forced-labor free";
- "recall complete";
- "invoice approved";
- "cargo released";
- "title transferred."

Allowed:

- "document evidence compiled";
- "customs packet prepared";
- "trade review required";
- "EPCIS event gap found";
- "bill-of-lading mismatch candidate";
- "cold-chain excursion candidate."

### W10504 Outcome Warranty Scope

Warrant only operational metrics first:

- fewer document mismatches;
- faster exception triage;
- lower rekeying;
- better certificate completeness;
- faster recall scope calculation;
- lower EDI mapping defect rate.

Do not warranty customs clearance, sanctions safety, supplier ethics, or trade
finance approval without external authority.

### W10505 AI-Native Supply Chain Breakthrough

The 100x invention is `Chain-of-Custody Distillation`: train students not on
documents alone, but on the sequence of physical and commercial state changes.
Each model sees event order, custody transitions, document contradictions,
sensor excursions, and human resolutions. The resulting student can detect
exceptions earlier than document matching because it learns the normal physics
and paperwork rhythm of each lane.

Build:

1. Convert event streams into temporal graphs.
2. Align documents to graph edges.
3. Mine contradiction motifs.
4. Distill exception predictors per lane, product, and supplier class.
5. Require human-reviewed holdout outcomes before automation.

### W10506 Implementation Agent Contract

Implementation deliverables:

- `SupplyChainIR` schema;
- `SupplyChainEvidenceReceipt` schema;
- EPCIS parser;
- EDI/UBL document adapters;
- bill-of-lading adapter;
- customs field adapter;
- exception scoring engine;
- compliance boundary engine;
- replay capsule;
- benchmark fixtures.

Every adapter must produce field-level lineage and unknown-field reports.

### W10507 Product Matrix Integration

This layer strengthens all three Kolm surfaces:

- One AI API: route procurement/logistics/trade tasks with strict review
  boundaries.
- Compile/distill: compile repeated exception handling into owned assistants.
- Enterprise-to-device: run warehouse, port, and field logistics assistants on
  local devices with signed evidence.

### W10508 Ultimate Supply Chain Objective

The final state:

> Kolm turns supply-chain AI from document summarization into trade evidence
> artifacts that know parties, products, identifiers, events, custody,
> documents, customs, sensors, exceptions, reviewers, and replay state.

This unlocks:

- procurement automation;
- supplier onboarding;
- logistics control towers;
- customs packet preparation;
- trade finance document matching;
- recalls;
- cold chain;
- warehouse operations;
- sanctions/export review;
- supply chain resilience.

Supply chain, procurement, logistics, customs, and trade sources:

- GS1 EPCIS and CBV: https://www.gs1.org/standards/epcis
- GS1 Standards: https://www.gs1.org/standards
- WCO Data Model: https://www.wcoomd.org/DataModel
- DCSA Bill of Lading Standard: https://dcsa.org/standards/bill-of-lading
- X12 Supply Chain: https://ecommerce.x12.org/industry/supply-chain
- X12 EDI Standard: https://ecommerce.x12.org/products/x12-edi-standard-007060
- OASIS Universal Business Language Committee: https://www.oasis-open.org/committees/tc_home.php?wg_abbrev=ubl
- OASIS UBL 2.4: https://docs.oasis-open.org/ubl/os-UBL-2.4/UBL-2.4.pdf
- Open Contracting Data Standard: https://www.open-contracting.org/data-standard/
- Peppol BIS Billing: https://docs.peppol.eu/poacc/billing/3.0/2025-Q4/bis/

## Aerospace, Defense, Space Mission, Certification, And Safety Case Compiler Layer

Aerospace, defense, and space are not generic AI markets. They are mission
assurance markets. The value is not a fluent copilot; it is a system that binds
requirements, hazards, verification, validation, configuration, telemetry,
mission operations, safety cases, certification artifacts, and human authority.

The category is mission evidence compilation. Kolm should produce artifacts that
help engineers, mission operators, safety teams, certifiers, and acquisition
owners use AI without losing traceability or authority.

### W10510 MissionAssuranceEvidenceReceipt Schema

Invent `MissionAssuranceEvidenceReceipt`.

Required fields:

- `system_ref`: aircraft, spacecraft, payload, ground system, weapon system,
  subsystem, software component, hardware component, or mission thread;
- `lifecycle_context`: concept, requirements, design, implementation, test,
  verification, validation, operations, anomaly response, sustainment, or
  retirement;
- `requirements_context`: requirement ID, source, rationale, parent, child,
  allocation, verification method, and acceptance state;
- `hazard_context`: hazard, cause, control, severity, probability, mitigation,
  residual risk, and safety authority;
- `certification_context`: standard, objective, artifact, review, compliance
  evidence, open item, and authority;
- `configuration_context`: baseline, change request, version, build, part,
  interface, and rollback;
- `telemetry_context`: test data, flight data, ground data, anomaly, command,
  state vector, and time correlation;
- `human_authority`: engineer, safety board, mission director, certifier,
  commander, flight director, or operator review state;
- `artifact_hash`: deterministic hash of the replayable packet.

The receipt should never authorize flight, weapon use, mission command, or
certification. It compiles the evidence for authorized humans and systems.

### W10511 MissionIR

Create `MissionIR` with nodes:

- Mission;
- Capability;
- Requirement;
- Interface;
- Component;
- Software item;
- Hardware item;
- Hazard;
- Control;
- Test;
- Verification result;
- Validation result;
- Anomaly;
- Telemetry packet;
- Command;
- Configuration baseline;
- Certification objective;
- Review board;
- Authority.

Edges:

- satisfies;
- allocated to;
- verified by;
- validated by;
- mitigates;
- depends on;
- interfaces with;
- commanded by;
- observed in telemetry;
- changed by;
- approved by;
- waived by;
- failed by;
- retired by.

The MissionIR is the bridge between Kolm artifacts and real mission assurance.

### W10512 Requirements Traceability And Verification Binder

The binder should preserve:

- source requirement;
- derived requirement;
- allocation;
- rationale;
- design element;
- code or model element;
- test procedure;
- verification result;
- waiver;
- nonconformance;
- review state.

This is a core wedge. Aerospace buyers pay for traceability because missing
traceability blocks certification and mission readiness.

### W10513 NASA Systems Engineering Lifecycle Packet

The systems engineering packet should align to lifecycle evidence:

- stakeholder expectations;
- technical requirements;
- logical decomposition;
- design solution;
- product implementation;
- integration;
- verification;
- validation;
- transition;
- technical risk;
- decision analysis;
- configuration management;
- technical data management.

Kolm can turn scattered engineering artifacts into reviewable lifecycle packets.

### W10514 Software Assurance And Safety Evidence Binder

The software assurance binder should compile:

- software classification;
- criticality;
- software safety requirement;
- assurance activity;
- IV&V result;
- static analysis;
- test coverage;
- defect;
- hazard linkage;
- release readiness;
- open risk.

The product should not claim safety. It should show which assurance evidence
exists and what remains open.

### W10515 DO-178C/FAA Airborne Software Evidence Boundary

For civil airborne systems, the compiler should track:

- software level;
- planning document;
- requirements;
- design;
- code;
- traceability;
- verification cases;
- verification procedures;
- verification results;
- structural coverage;
- problem reports;
- configuration index;
- accomplishment summary.

AI assistance must remain evidence-producing unless a certification authority
accepts the workflow. The output is certification support, not certification.

### W10516 MIL-STD-882E Hazard Analysis Binder

The hazard binder should preserve:

- hazard ID;
- mishap;
- severity;
- probability;
- initial risk;
- mitigation;
- verification;
- residual risk;
- acceptance authority;
- safety assessment report linkage.

Kolm should produce hazard evidence packets and ensure that AI-generated
recommendations do not bypass risk acceptance.

### W10517 DoD Responsible AI Mission Packet

For defense AI, the packet should include:

- responsible AI principle mapping;
- accountability owner;
- data lineage;
- model lineage;
- test environment;
- operational context;
- human judgment point;
- failure mode;
- monitoring plan;
- rollback or disablement;
- use limitation;
- red-team result.

This binds AI to operational governance rather than policy slogans.

### W10518 CCSDS Space Data And Telemetry Binder

Space operations need data-standard awareness:

- packet;
- frame;
- file delivery;
- time code;
- telemetry channel;
- command history;
- ground station;
- contact window;
- latency;
- loss;
- replay;
- archive.

The binder should help mission teams connect AI analysis to exact packets and
time windows.

### W10519 Mission Operations Command Boundary

The mission command boundary is absolute.

Allowed:

- summarize telemetry;
- correlate anomalies;
- draft procedures;
- check command preconditions;
- prepare review packet;
- simulate outcome;
- identify missing evidence.

Blocked without explicit authority:

- transmit command;
- arm system;
- release weapon;
- alter flight path;
- disable safety feature;
- waive hazard;
- approve launch;
- certify airworthiness.

Every artifact must carry command authority state.

### W10520 Test Range, Flight Test, And Telemetry Replay Capsule

The replay capsule should include:

- test objective;
- configuration;
- instrumentation;
- procedure;
- environmental conditions;
- operator actions;
- telemetry window;
- anomalies;
- data quality;
- pass/fail criteria;
- post-test review.

This lets teams reproduce AI analysis during test readiness reviews and anomaly
boards.

### W10521 Anomaly Review And Failure Investigation Compiler

Anomaly packets should compile:

- observed behavior;
- expected behavior;
- telemetry evidence;
- command history;
- configuration baseline;
- environmental context;
- suspect component;
- fault tree candidate;
- reproduction attempt;
- corrective action;
- board decision.

The system should say "root cause candidate," not "root cause," until an
authorized board decides.

### W10522 Configuration, Interface, And Digital Thread Binder

Compile:

- part;
- serial;
- software build;
- firmware;
- interface control document;
- requirement allocation;
- test result;
- waiver;
- change request;
- baseline;
- deployment state.

This creates a mission digital thread with replayable AI outputs.

### W10523 Model-Based Systems Engineering Bridge

The MBSE bridge should link:

- model element;
- requirement;
- behavior;
- interface;
- constraint;
- test;
- hazard;
- analysis;
- configuration;
- evidence receipt.

AI should not hallucinate model structure. It should propose model updates with
traceable deltas and review state.

### W10524 Autonomy And Human-On-The-Loop Guard

Autonomy needs explicit operational design domain:

- environment;
- mission phase;
- allowed actions;
- prohibited actions;
- human role;
- fallback;
- sensor confidence;
- communication state;
- lost-link behavior;
- safety monitor;
- abort criteria.

The product should compile autonomy safety cases, not market "autonomous mission
success."

### W10525 Mission K-Score

Invent `Mission K-Score`:

- `requirements_traceability`;
- `verification_completeness`;
- `hazard_control_evidence`;
- `configuration_replayability`;
- `telemetry_lineage`;
- `operator_review_agreement`;
- `anomaly_candidate_precision`;
- `certification_artifact_completeness`;
- `command_boundary_integrity`;
- `software_assurance_evidence`;
- `mission_readiness_packet_quality`;
- `residual_risk_visibility`.

This score measures whether AI improved mission assurance, not whether it wrote
convincing text.

### W10526 Mission Distillation Moat

Distill assistants from mission evidence:

- requirements traceability assistant;
- hazard analysis assistant;
- test readiness reviewer;
- telemetry anomaly assistant;
- procedure drafter;
- configuration diff reviewer;
- certification evidence packager;
- mission debrief assistant.

The moat is reviewed mission data, anomaly boards, test outcomes, and
configuration history. Competitors with generic models cannot safely reproduce
that context.

### W10527 Classified, Export-Controlled, And Need-To-Know Membrane

The membrane should enforce:

- classification;
- CUI;
- ITAR/EAR;
- export jurisdiction;
- need-to-know;
- program access;
- coalition release;
- data residency;
- model training exclusion;
- audit log.

The artifact must compile boundary evidence and refuse unsafe export.

### W10528 Mission Assurance Clean Room

The clean room should support cross-program learning without exposing secrets:

- abstract failure motifs;
- remove mission identifiers;
- preserve subsystem class;
- preserve hazard class;
- preserve test pattern;
- suppress sensitive telemetry;
- bind export-control policy;
- record derived models.

This enables learning from failures across programs without leaking program
data.

### W10529 Certification Evidence Marketplace Labels

Labels:

- `requirements-traceable`;
- `hazard-linked`;
- `verification-bound`;
- `validation-bound`;
- `configuration-baselined`;
- `telemetry-replayable`;
- `command-blocked`;
- `certification-support-only`;
- `software-assurance-evidence`;
- `export-controlled`;
- `authority-review-required`.

Labels must be computed from receipts.

### W10530 Benchmark Suite

Fixtures:

- missing requirement trace;
- hazard without verified mitigation;
- telemetry anomaly with benign explanation;
- command precondition failure;
- configuration mismatch;
- software test coverage gap;
- certification objective missing artifact;
- false root-cause trap;
- export-control boundary trap;
- operator procedure ambiguity.

Metrics:

- missing evidence recall;
- false root-cause rate;
- traceability precision;
- command boundary correctness;
- review agreement;
- replay completeness.

### W10531 Dashboard Contract

Dashboard:

- open requirements;
- verification gaps;
- validation gaps;
- hazard residual risk;
- software assurance findings;
- configuration mismatches;
- telemetry anomalies;
- anomaly board queue;
- certification evidence status;
- export-control blocks;
- command-boundary violations.

This should serve engineering and mission assurance teams, not demo-driven AI.

### W10532 Implementation Sequence

Build order:

1. `MissionIR` and `MissionAssuranceEvidenceReceipt`.
2. Requirements traceability binder.
3. Hazard analysis binder.
4. Configuration and interface binder.
5. Software assurance binder.
6. Telemetry replay capsule.
7. Command boundary guard.
8. Mission K-Score.
9. Certification evidence labels.
10. Dashboard.

First sellable module: requirements/hazard/verification evidence compiler.

### W10533 Smoke Test

Synthetic smoke:

1. Load requirements, hazards, design elements, tests, and telemetry snippets.
2. Bind one requirement to a test result.
3. Leave one hazard without verified mitigation.
4. Add a configuration mismatch.
5. Add a telemetry anomaly.
6. Compile `MissionAssuranceEvidenceReceipt`.
7. Generate anomaly candidate packet.
8. Run command boundary guard.
9. Run export-control membrane.
10. Confirm no mission command or certification approval is emitted.

Passing state:

- traceability gap detected;
- hazard gap detected;
- anomaly remains candidate;
- command blocked;
- receipt hash stable.

### W10534 Readiness Gate

Ready only if:

- system context exists;
- lifecycle phase exists;
- requirement lineage exists;
- hazard authority exists for safety items;
- configuration baseline exists;
- verification evidence exists;
- command authority state exists;
- export boundary exists;
- no mission/safety/certification decision is automated without authority.

### W10535 Claim Honesty Gate

Blocked without proof:

- "certified";
- "flight ready";
- "safe";
- "mission approved";
- "autonomous";
- "root cause found";
- "requirements complete";
- "DO-178C compliant";
- "NERC-like compliance for mission";
- "export safe."

Allowed:

- "evidence packet compiled";
- "traceability gap found";
- "hazard mitigation candidate";
- "telemetry anomaly candidate";
- "certification support packet";
- "operator review required."

### W10536 Outcome Warranty Scope

Warrant only evidence operations first:

- faster traceability review;
- fewer missing test links;
- faster anomaly packet preparation;
- fewer configuration mismatch escapes;
- faster certification artifact assembly;
- improved review packet completeness.

Never warranty safety, mission success, or certification without the relevant
authority.

### W10537 Product Matrix Integration

This layer strengthens Kolm:

- One AI API: route mission engineering tasks with authority-aware boundaries.
- Compile/distill: compile reviewed anomaly, hazard, and traceability work into
  owned mission assistants.
- Enterprise-to-device: run field, test range, and air-gapped mission assistants
  locally with signed evidence.

It also makes the "owned AI" claim credible in the most demanding environments.

### W10538 Ultimate Mission Assurance Objective

The final state:

> Kolm turns aerospace, defense, and space AI from risky assistance into mission
> evidence artifacts that know requirements, hazards, configurations, tests,
> telemetry, software assurance, certification objectives, command boundaries,
> authorities, and replay state.

This unlocks:

- requirements engineering;
- hazard analysis;
- software assurance;
- test readiness;
- anomaly review;
- certification support;
- mission operations;
- air-gapped field engineering;
- defense acquisition evidence;
- space telemetry analysis.

Aerospace, defense, space mission, certification, and safety sources:

- NASA Systems Engineering Handbook: https://www.nasa.gov/reference/systems-engineering-handbook/
- NASA Software Engineering Procedural Requirements and Standards: https://www.nasa.gov/intelligent-systems-division/software-management-office/nasa-software-engineering-procedural-requirements-standards-and-related-resources/
- NASA Software Engineering and Software Assurance Electronic Handbook: https://swehb.nasa.gov/
- NASA NPR 7150.2C Software Engineering Requirements: https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/displayAll.cfm?Internal_ID=N_PR_7150_002C_
- NASA Software Safety Standard reference: https://standards.nasa.gov/sites/default/files/standards/NASA/C-Change-2/0/nasa-std-871913c_with_change_2_0.pdf
- FAA Software and Airborne Electronic Hardware: https://www.faa.gov/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/software_regs
- RTCA DO-178 overview: https://www.rtca.org/do-178/
- FAA AC 20-115D DO-178C recognition: https://www.faa.gov/regulations_policies/advisory_circulars/index.cfm/go/document.information/documentID/1032046
- DoD Responsible AI resources: https://www.ai.mil/About/Resources/Pathway-to-AI-Readiness/Responsible-AI/
- DoD Responsible AI Strategy and Implementation Pathway PDF: https://www.ai.mil/Portals/137/Documents/Resources%20Page/DoD%20Responsible%20AI%20Strategy%20and%20Implementation%20Pathway.pdf
- U.S. Army System Safety page for MIL-STD-882E: https://safety.army.mil/ON-DUTY/System-Safety
- CCSDS publications: https://public.ccsds.org/Pubs

## Media, Advertising, Content Rights, Provenance, And Monetization Evidence Compiler Layer

Media, advertising, and content-rights markets are where AI ownership becomes
commercially concrete. Publishers need training-use terms, advertisers need
inventory quality, creators need attribution, platforms need provenance, agencies
need rights clearance, and buyers need measurement evidence. Generic AI systems
collapse all of this into "content generation" and miss the business truth:
media is an evidence, rights, and monetization graph.

The category is not "AI content platform." The category is content rights and
monetization evidence compilation. Kolm should produce artifacts that know the
asset, creator, source, edit chain, license, usage policy, ad inventory, campaign,
measurement, brand-safety state, payment, and review authority.
