- residual risk register;
- TEE-K score.

### W13351 Confidential Compute Build Sequence

Invention: `ConfidentialComputeBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Attestation evidence schema.
2. Key release policy.
3. Reproducible enclave build.
4. Attested inference receipt.
5. Confidential route broker.
6. Telemetry firewall.
7. Confidential clean room.
8. TEE-K.

### W13352 Confidential Compute Simulation

Invention: `ConfidentialComputeSimulator`.

Scenarios:

- attestation mismatch;
- image vulnerability;
- region mismatch;
- key release denied;
- GPU unavailable;
- telemetry leak attempt;
- side-channel warning.

Expected:

- workload blocks or downgrades with precise reason.

### W13353 Confidential Compute Refusal Policy

Invention: `ConfidentialComputeRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse confidential claims when:

- attestation absent;
- measurement stale;
- vulnerability denylist hit;
- key release bypassed;
- telemetry leak risk unresolved;
- provider cannot prove boundary.

The refusal should recommend DP, federation, or local-only alternatives.

### W13354 Confidential Compute Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- AWS Nitro Enclaves: https://aws.amazon.com/ec2/nitro/nitro-enclaves/
- Intel confidential computing: https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/security/confidential-computing.html
- AMD confidential computing: https://www.amd.com/en/products/processors/server/epyc/confidential-computing.html
- NVIDIA confidential computing: https://docs.nvidia.com/confidential-computing/index.html

Internal evidence:

- attestation fixtures;
- key release simulations;
- enclave build receipts;
- confidential inference receipts;
- TEE-K reports.

## Verifiable Inference, Zero-Knowledge ML, Homomorphic Encryption, MPC, And Cryptographic Proof Layer

Confidential compute reduces trust in infrastructure, but verifiable compute can reduce trust in the operator. Kolm should support cryptographic receipts for selected tasks where proving correct execution matters more than raw speed.

### W13355 Verifiable Inference Evidence Graph

Invention: `VerifiableInferenceEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- model circuit or program;
- input commitment;
- output commitment;
- proof system;
- verifier;
- proving time;
- verification time;
- public inputs;
- private inputs;
- proof scope.

Sources:

- RISC Zero zkVM docs: https://docs.rs/risc0-zkvm/latest/index.html
- SP1 repository: https://github.com/succinctlabs/sp1
- EZKL docs: https://docs.ezkl.xyz/
- Microsoft SEAL: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/project/microsoft-seal/documentation/
- CrypTen: https://crypten.ai/

### W13356 ZK Proof-Of-Inference Compiler

Invention: `ZKInferenceCompiler`.

Compile small models and deterministic validators into proof systems.

Good first targets:

- structured output validator;
- redaction classifier;
- route policy;
- score threshold kernel;
- small tabular model;
- artifact verifier.

Do not start with huge LLM inference. Start with high-value small proofs.

### W13357 ZK-Friendly Artifact Subset

Invention: `ZKFriendlyKolmSubset`.

Define which `.kolm` artifacts can be proven efficiently:

- quantized linear models;
- decision trees;
- finite-state validators;
- small neural classifiers;
- scoring kernels;
- route policies.

The compiler should say:

- proof-ready;
- proof-expensive;
- not proof-suitable;
- use TEE instead.

### W13358 Proof Cost Planner

Invention: `ProofCostPlanner`.

Estimate:

- circuit size;
- proving time;
- verification time;
- memory;
- cloud prover cost;
- latency impact;
- proof value.

Then recommend:

- ZK proof;
- TEE receipt;
- signed runtime trace;
- normal audit log.

This prevents cryptography from becoming expensive theater.

### W13359 Homomorphic Eval Planner

Invention: `HomomorphicEvalPlanner`.

Use homomorphic encryption only where appropriate:

- simple linear scoring;
- private aggregate metrics;
- encrypted feature inference;
- sensitive benchmark scoring.

Planner outputs:

- scheme family;
- approximate precision;
- depth budget;
- key requirements;
- unsupported operations.

### W13360 MPC Model Comparison

Invention: `MPCModelComparison`.

Use secure multiparty computation for cross-party model comparison:

- each party keeps data private;
- model scores are computed securely;
- only aggregate winner or approved statistics are revealed.

Use cases:

- vendor bakeoff;
- consortium benchmark;
- legal discovery classifier;
- healthcare model comparison.

### W13361 Verifiable K-Score Kernel

Invention: `VerifiableKScoreKernel`.

Compile the K-Score threshold decision into a verifiable computation.

Proof statement:

- given committed metrics and threshold;
- the published pass/fail result was computed correctly;
- private raw examples remain hidden.

This protects benchmark credibility.

### W13362 Proof-Carrying Eval Report

Invention: `ProofCarryingEvalReport`.

Eval reports can include:

- signed raw metrics;
- commitments to private examples;
- verifier instructions;
- proof of score computation;
- public subset;
- private aggregate.

This is how Kolm can publish credible benchmark claims without exposing sensitive customer data.

### W13363 ZK Redaction Certificate

Invention: `ZKRedactionCertificate`.

For sensitive documents, prove:

- redaction policy was applied;
- every detected sensitive span was replaced;
- output hash matches redacted output;
- raw text remains private.

First target should be deterministic redaction pipelines, not general language reasoning.

### W13364 Cryptographic Route Receipt

Invention: `CryptographicRouteReceipt`.

For a route decision, produce:

- policy hash;
- model choice;
- provider choice;
- cost class;
- privacy class;
- proof or attestation;
- output commitment.

This helps regulated customers prove why a request went where it went.

### W13365 Private Benchmark Leaderboard

Invention: `PrivateBenchmarkLeaderboard`.

Allow competitors or customers to submit private results:

- commit to raw outputs;
- prove scoring correctness;
- reveal aggregate score;
- hide private data.

This could make KolmBench credible in industries that cannot publish raw fixtures.

### W13366 ZK/TEE Hybrid Proof

Invention: `HybridProofReceipt`.

Combine:

- TEE attestation for large model execution;
- ZK proof for deterministic post-processing;
- signature for artifact lineage;
- C2PA-style manifest for provenance.

This is practical: prove the small critical parts, attest the expensive parts.

### W13367 Proof System Risk Register

Invention: `ProofSystemRiskRegister`.

Track:

- trusted setup;
- circuit bug;
- compiler bug;
- proof system version;
- verifier version;
- proving key;
- audit status;
- performance limits.

Do not let "zero knowledge" become a new unscoped claim.

### W13368 Verifiable Compute Downgrade Rule

Invention: `VerifiableComputeDowngradeRule`.

Downgrade when:

- proof absent;
- verifier unavailable;
- circuit mismatch;
- input commitment missing;
- proof too expensive for SLA;
- proof system risk too high.

Output alternative:

- TEE receipt;
- signed trace;
- manual audit.

### W13369 Crypto Primitive Selector

Invention: `CryptoPrimitiveSelector`.

Given a workload, choose:

- DP;
- federated learning;
- secure aggregation;
- TEE;
- ZK;
- MPC;
- homomorphic encryption;
- signed provenance;
- local-only.

Decision criteria:

- threat model;
- performance;
- data sensitivity;
- proof need;
- customer trust boundary;
- implementation maturity.

### W13370 CRYPTO-K Metric

Invention: `CRYPTO-K`.

Measure cryptographic assurance:

- correct primitive selection;
- proof coverage;
- verification success;
- performance overhead;
- risk register freshness;
- key management quality;
- downgrade correctness.

This lets Kolm avoid overusing cryptography and underusing it.

### W13371 Verifiable Compute Product Surface

Invention: `VerifiableComputeProductSurface`.

Backend objects:

- proof job;
- verifier;
- proof receipt;
- input commitment;
- output commitment;
- crypto primitive choice;
- CRYPTO-K score.

### W13372 Verifiable Compute Build Sequence

Invention: `VerifiableComputeBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Crypto primitive selector.
2. Verifiable K-Score kernel.
3. Cryptographic route receipt.
4. ZK-friendly artifact subset.
5. Proof cost planner.
6. Hybrid proof receipt.
7. Private benchmark leaderboard.
8. CRYPTO-K.

### W13373 Verifiable Compute Simulation

Invention: `VerifiableComputeSimulator`.

Scenarios:

- tiny validator proof;
- private benchmark score;
- redaction certificate;
- route receipt;
- TEE plus ZK hybrid;
- proof too slow.

Expected:

- system selects the cheapest primitive that satisfies the threat model.

### W13374 Verifiable Compute Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- RISC Zero zkVM: https://docs.rs/risc0-zkvm/latest/index.html
- SP1: https://github.com/succinctlabs/sp1
- EZKL: https://docs.ezkl.xyz/
- Microsoft SEAL: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/project/microsoft-seal/documentation/
- CrypTen: https://crypten.ai/

Internal evidence:

- proof receipts;
- verifier fixtures;
- primitive selector tests;
- private leaderboard simulations;
- CRYPTO-K reports.

## Data Rights, Provenance, Supply Chain Trust, Consent, Licensing, And Artifact Economy Layer

The final privacy frontier is ownership. Kolm's artifact economy should prevent data laundering, license confusion, supply-chain ambiguity, and unsupported claims. Every artifact should carry who can use it, why, under which rights, and with what proof.

### W13375 Data Rights Evidence Graph

Invention: `DataRightsEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- source;
- owner;
- consent;
- license;
- retention;
- allowed transformation;
- allowed training;
- allowed redistribution;
- derivative artifact;
- revocation.

Sources:

- Sigstore Cosign: https://docs.sigstore.dev/cosign/signing/overview/
- SLSA provenance: https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.1/provenance
- C2PA specifications: https://spec.c2pa.org/specifications/specifications/2.4/index.html

### W13376 License-Aware Distillation

Invention: `LicenseAwareDistiller`.

Before distillation, check:

- source data license;
- model license;
- teacher terms;
- output redistribution terms;
- attribution;
- commercial restriction;
- field-of-use restriction.

If rights are incompatible, block artifact publication or restrict use.

This protects Kolm from building a marketplace on unclear derivatives.

### W13377 Consent Revocation Compiler

Invention: `ConsentRevocationCompiler`.

When data consent is revoked:

- find affected traces;
- find derived artifacts;
- determine whether deletion, retraining, or usage restriction is required;
- update manifest;
- notify owners;
- downgrade claims.

This is hard, but it becomes a major enterprise differentiator.

### W13378 Artifact Supply Chain Attestation

Invention: `ArtifactSupplyChainAttestation`.

Every artifact should have:

- source repository;
- build command;
- builder identity;
- dependency graph;
- SBOM;
- signature;
- timestamp;
- provenance predicate;
- verification command.

Use Sigstore and SLSA-style patterns for `.kolm` artifacts.

### W13379 Prompt And Trace Provenance

Invention: `PromptTraceProvenance`.

Prompt and trace data need provenance:

- who captured;
- from which app;
- under which policy;
- with which redaction;
- with which consent;
- at what time;
- with which model response.

This lets customers prove artifact lineage without exposing raw traces.

### W13380 C2PA-Inspired Artifact Manifest

Invention: `C2PAInspiredArtifactManifest`.

Adapt media provenance ideas to AI artifacts:

- ingredient manifests;
- transformation assertions;
- signatures;
- claim generators;
- redaction assertions;
- training assertions;
- evaluation assertions.

Kolm should not copy media standards blindly. It should use the same provenance discipline for model artifacts.

### W13381 Model Terms Compliance Engine

Invention: `ModelTermsComplianceEngine`.

Track provider and model terms:

- distillation allowed;
- caching allowed;
- output reuse allowed;
- fine-tuning allowed;
- benchmarking allowed;
- public display allowed;
- regulated use allowed.

Policy engine blocks incompatible workflows before they create legal risk.

### W13382 Data Lineage Query Language

Invention: `KolmLineageQL`.

Query examples:

- which artifacts used this dataset?
- which artifacts depend on this teacher?
- which benchmarks used private traces?
- which marketplace artifacts need attribution?
- which artifacts are affected by revoked consent?

This is required for serious enterprise governance.

### W13383 Rights-Aware Marketplace Ranking

Invention: `RightsAwareMarketplaceRanker`.

Rank artifacts by:

- quality;
- cost;
- license clarity;
- provenance completeness;
- privacy score;
- support level;
- proof coverage;
- revocation risk.

This discourages low-quality or legally ambiguous artifacts from winning on price alone.

### W13384 Royalty And Contribution Engine

Invention: `ArtifactRoyaltyEngine`.

If artifacts derive from recipes, datasets, teachers, or consortium contributions, compute:

- contribution share;
- allowed monetization;
- revenue split;
- credit;
- expiration;
- dispute state.

This turns the artifact marketplace into a durable economy.

### W13385 Data Room Export

Invention: `EnterpriseDataRoomExport`.

For procurement and audits, export:

- artifact manifest;
- provenance;
- licenses;
- privacy evidence;
- eval reports;
- proof receipts;
- supply-chain attestations;
- open gates.

This reduces enterprise sales friction.

### W13386 Rights Conflict Detector

Invention: `RightsConflictDetector`.

Detect:

- conflicting licenses;
- expired consent;
- missing attribution;
- forbidden redistribution;
- public benchmark using private-only data;
- teacher output used beyond terms.

Output:

- block;
- restrict;
- request approval;
- remove ingredient;
- retrain.

### W13387 Provenance Transparency Log

Invention: `KolmArtifactTransparencyLog`.

Store append-only records:

- artifact published;
- artifact revoked;
- proof attached;
- benchmark published;
- license updated;
- consent revoked;
- vulnerability disclosed.

This creates ecosystem trust and makes artifact tampering visible.

### W13388 Revocation-Aware Runtime

Invention: `RevocationAwareRuntime`.

Runtime checks:

- artifact revoked;
- license expired;
- consent invalidated;
- vulnerability critical;
- proof stale;
- marketplace trust downgraded.

Actions:

- warn;
- block;
- fallback;
- request update.

### W13389 Enterprise Contract Compiler

Invention: `EnterpriseContractCompiler`.

Compile enterprise contract terms into:

- route policy;
- retention policy;
- provider policy;
- artifact reuse policy;
- audit export policy;
- marketplace restrictions.

This aligns legal agreements with actual runtime behavior.

### W13390 ECON-K Metric

Invention: `ECON-K`.

Measure artifact economy trust:

- provenance completeness;
- license clarity;
- revocation handling;
- royalty correctness;
- supply-chain integrity;
- marketplace trust score;
- data room completeness.

### W13391 Rights And Provenance Product Surface

Invention: `RightsProvenanceProductSurface`.

Backend objects:

- data right;
- consent grant;
- license;
- artifact ingredient;
- provenance assertion;
- transparency log entry;
- data room export;
- ECON-K score.

### W13392 Rights Layer Build Sequence

Invention: `RightsLayerBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Data rights evidence graph.
2. Prompt trace provenance.
3. Artifact supply-chain attestation.
4. Rights conflict detector.
5. Revocation-aware runtime.
6. Enterprise contract compiler.
7. Transparency log.
8. ECON-K.

### W13393 Rights Layer Simulation

Invention: `RightsLayerSimulator`.

Simulate:

- revoked consent;
- conflicting license;
- expired model terms;
- marketplace artifact dispute;
- missing attribution;
- artifact vulnerability.

Expected:

- affected artifacts identified;
- runtime blocks or downgrades;
- data room updates;
- transparency log records the event.

### W13394 Rights Layer Sources And Internal Evidence

Primary sources:

- Sigstore Cosign: https://docs.sigstore.dev/cosign/signing/overview/
- SLSA provenance: https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.1/provenance
- C2PA specification: https://spec.c2pa.org/specifications/specifications/2.4/index.html
- OpenDP: https://docs.opendp.org/en/stable/

Internal evidence:

- rights graph fixtures;
- license conflict tests;
- consent revocation simulations;
- supply-chain attestations;
- transparency log samples;
- ECON-K reports.

## Mechanistic Interpretability, Causal Science, Model Editing, Adversarial Robustness, And Reproducible Research Layer

The prior layers make Kolm provable, private, federated, attested, and governed. The next frontier is to understand and control the model internals and the experiment process itself. Kolm should not only produce smaller artifacts; it should know which internal circuits, causal mechanisms, edits, risks, and experimental assumptions make the artifact work.

### W13395 Mechanistic Interpretability Evidence Graph

Invention: `MechanisticEvidenceGraph`.

Track internal model evidence:

- model;
- layer;
- activation site;
- feature;
- sparse autoencoder;
- circuit;
- causal patch;
- steering vector;
- failure mode;
- distillation target;
- artifact effect.

Primary sources:

- Anthropic Transformer Circuits: https://www.anthropic.com/research/a-mathematical-framework-for-transformer-circuits
- Towards Monosemanticity: https://transformer-circuits.pub/2023/monosemantic-features/index.html
- TransformerLens: https://github.com/TransformerLensOrg/TransformerLens
- Activation patching best practices: https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.16042

### W13396 Sparse Autoencoder Feature Atlas

Invention: `KolmSAEFeatureAtlas`.

Train sparse autoencoders over teacher and student activations to identify reusable features:

- task features;
- safety features;
- refusal features;
- tool-use features;
- hallucination features;
- format features;
- domain features.

Product effect:

Distillation can target features instead of only outputs. A student is not done when it matches responses; it is done when it preserves required internal capabilities and loses unwanted ones.

### W13397 Activation Patching Workbench

Invention: `ActivationPatchingWorkbench`.

Create controlled interventions:

- clean input;
- corrupted input;
- patched activation;
- recovered behavior;
- causal importance score.

Use it to answer:

- which layer stores the task?;
- which attention head routes the key evidence?;
- which residual stream feature causes hallucination?;
- which student layer lost the teacher capability?

This turns debugging into causal measurement.

### W13398 Causal Circuit Distillation

Invention: `CircuitDistillation`.

Distill not only logits, but circuit behavior:

- teacher circuit identifies feature;
- student circuit attempts same feature;
- mismatch creates targeted training data;
- evaluator checks behavior and internal alignment.

This is stronger than vanilla knowledge distillation because it preserves the mechanism that matters.

### W13399 Teacher-Student Circuit Alignment

Invention: `TeacherStudentCircuitAlignment`.

Compare teacher and student internal representations:

- feature activation overlap;
- causal patch transfer;
- attention pattern similarity;
- failure feature absence;
- format feature preservation.

Output:

- aligned;
- behavior-only match;
- missing capability circuit;
- unsafe transferred circuit;
- student shortcut detected.

### W13400 Feature Drift Monitor

Invention: `FeatureDriftMonitor`.

After deployment, monitor whether important features shift:

- domain feature frequency;
- refusal feature activation;
- uncertainty feature activation;
- hallucination feature activation;
- tool-use feature activation.

When feature distribution changes, trigger:

- teacher shadowing;
- fresh eval;
- active learning;
- rollback.

### W13401 Monosemanticity Score

Invention: `MonosemanticityScore`.

Measure whether learned features are interpretable enough to use operationally:

- sparsity;
- feature purity;
- automatic explanation consistency;
- intervention specificity;
- cross-model stability;
- human review agreement.

A low score does not mean the model is bad. It means the feature should not be used as evidence for product claims.

### W13402 Circuit-Based Regression Tests

Invention: `CircuitRegressionTest`.

Turn circuits into tests:

- feature must activate on positive examples;
- feature must stay low on negative examples;
- intervention must change output in expected direction;
- student must preserve protected circuit;
- unsafe circuit must be absent or guarded.

This gives Kolm a new class of regression test beyond input-output fixtures.

### W13403 Hallucination Circuit Detector

Invention: `HallucinationCircuitDetector`.

Find internal states associated with unsupported generation:

- low evidence attention;
- high fluency but low citation grounding;
- contradictory retrieval features;
- stale memory feature;
- unsupported tool result feature.

Use it as:

- runtime warning;
- distillation target;
- retrieval repair trigger;
- refusal gate.

### W13404 Tool-Use Circuit Gate

Invention: `ToolUseCircuitGate`.

Detect when a model is internally preparing unsafe tool use:

- action feature;
- credential feature;
- shell command feature;
- irreversible side-effect feature;
- permission bypass feature.

The gate does not replace policy. It gives policy another signal before execution.

### W13405 Reasoning Circuit Passport

Invention: `ReasoningCircuitPassport`.

For reasoning-heavy artifacts, store:

- reasoning task class;
- circuit evidence;
- activation patch results;
- shortcut tests;
- chain leakage policy;
- behavior eval;
- failure mode.

This tells customers what is known about the reasoning mechanism without exposing private chain-of-thought.

### W13406 Multimodal Circuit Atlas

Invention: `MultimodalCircuitAtlas`.

Extend circuit analysis to:

- image features;
- text features;
- audio features;
- cross-modal bindings;
- OCR hallucination;
- spatial grounding;
- medical image concepts.

This supports the enterprise-any-device and multimodal roadmap.

### W13407 Interpretability Privacy Guard

Invention: `InterpretabilityPrivacyGuard`.

Feature explanations can leak sensitive concepts. Guard:

- raw example leakage;
- rare phrase leakage;
- patient or customer feature labels;
- private domain labels;
- proprietary workflow names.

Output only safe explanations unless the user has rights to the underlying traces.

### W13408 Circuit Marketplace

Invention: `CircuitRecipeMarketplace`.

Share reusable circuit recipes:

- refusal detector;
- hallucination detector;
- tool-use detector;
- citation grounding detector;
- PHI concept detector;
- domain feature pack.

Each recipe includes:

- model scope;
- feature ids;
- intervention tests;
- privacy scope;
- license;
- known failure modes.

### W13409 Circuit Audit Report

Invention: `CircuitAuditReport`.

For an artifact, publish:

- tested circuits;
- preserved teacher features;
- removed unsafe features;
- unknown internals;
- circuit regression results;
- feature drift alerts;
- claim scope.

This is a new enterprise audit asset.

### W13410 Mechanistic Failure Taxonomy

Invention: `MechanisticFailureTaxonomy`.

Failure classes:

- feature missing;
- feature polysemantic;
- shortcut circuit;
- unsafe feature preserved;
- teacher-student mismatch;
- spurious attention route;
- intervention not causal;
- feature explanation unsafe.

Each failure maps to a repair strategy.

### W13411 CIRCUIT-K Metric

Invention: `CIRCUIT-K`.

Measure interpretability maturity:

- circuit coverage;
- causal intervention strength;
- feature purity;
- student alignment;
- unsafe feature suppression;
- drift monitor coverage;
- privacy-safe explanation rate.

CIRCUIT-K should never be marketed as full understanding. It is a scoped evidence score.

### W13412 Mechanistic Simulation Harness

Invention: `MechanisticHarness`.

Simulate:

- teacher has correct circuit;
- student matches outputs using shortcut;
- sparse feature detects unsupported generation;
- unsafe tool feature activates;
- domain feature drifts.

Expected:

- student fails circuit alignment despite passing simple eval;
- targeted active learning is recommended.

### W13413 Mechanistic Build Sequence

Invention: `MechanisticBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Mechanistic evidence graph.
2. Activation patching workbench.
3. SAE feature atlas.
4. Circuit regression tests.
5. Teacher-student circuit alignment.
6. Hallucination and tool-use gates.
7. Circuit audit report.
8. CIRCUIT-K.

### W13414 Mechanistic Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- Anthropic Transformer Circuits: https://www.anthropic.com/research/a-mathematical-framework-for-transformer-circuits
- Towards Monosemanticity: https://transformer-circuits.pub/2023/monosemantic-features/index.html
- Anthropic interpretability engineering: https://www.anthropic.com/research/engineering-challenges-interpretability
- TransformerLens: https://github.com/TransformerLensOrg/TransformerLens
- Activation patching best practices: https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.16042

Internal evidence:

- feature atlas;
- patching fixtures;
- circuit tests;
- teacher-student alignment reports;
- CIRCUIT-K simulations.

## Causal Inference, Counterfactual Evaluation, Transportability, And Decision Optimization Layer

Kolm should know not only what correlates with quality, but what causes quality, cost, latency, leakage, drift, and customer value. This turns the product from an optimizer into an experimental science platform.

### W13415 Causal Evidence Graph

Invention: `CausalEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- causal question;
- treatment;
- outcome;
- confounder;
- instrument;
- intervention;
- counterfactual;
- estimate;
- refutation;
- decision.

Sources:

- DoWhy: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/project/dowhy/
- EconML: https://www.microsoft.com/research/project/econml/
- CausalML docs: https://causalml.readthedocs.io/

### W13416 Structural Causal Model Compiler

Invention: `KolmSCMCompiler`.

Compile product questions into structural causal models:

- does distillation reduce cost without reducing quality?;
- does quantization cause rare-class failure?;
- does retrieval improve factuality?;
- does local routing reduce latency?;
- does active learning improve future K-Score?

Output:

- graph;
- assumptions;
- identifiable effect;
- estimation method;
- refutation tests.

### W13417 Counterfactual Eval Generator

Invention: `CounterfactualEvalGenerator`.

Generate paired evals:

- same input with different policy;
- same task with and without retrieval;
- same example before and after redaction;
- same workflow with cloud fallback removed;
- same artifact with a feature erased.

Counterfactual evals expose causal impact instead of aggregate noise.

### W13418 Intervention Planner

Invention: `InterventionPlanner`.

Pick the next experiment:

- add examples;
- change student architecture;
- alter quantization;
- add retrieval;
- adjust route threshold;
- change safety filter.

Rank by expected causal information gain, not only expected score gain.

### W13419 Uplift Distillation

Invention: `UpliftDistillation`.

Estimate which examples benefit from distillation, local runtime, teacher fallback, or human review.

For each segment:

- treatment effect;
- uncertainty;
- expected ROI;
- harm risk;
- recommended policy.

This makes the route optimizer causal instead of purely predictive.

### W13420 Causal ROI Model

Invention: `CausalROIModel`.

Measure what actually caused savings:

- cache;
- route threshold;
- student model;
- batch scheduler;
- prompt compression;
- quantization;
- provider change.

This avoids attributing every cost reduction to the newest feature.

### W13421 Spurious Correlation Detector

Invention: `SpuriousCorrelationDetector`.

Detect shortcuts:

- label correlated with format;
- route correlated with customer;
- quality correlated with prompt length;
- safety correlated with template;
- domain correlated with provider.

Output:

- suspected confounder;
- counterfactual test;
- data collection recommendation.

### W13422 Transportability Compiler

Invention: `TransportabilityCompiler`.

Determine whether a result transfers:

- from one customer to another;
- from cloud to edge;
- from synthetic to real;
- from English to another language;
- from private holdout to public benchmark.

If not transportable, downgrade claims and recommend new data.

### W13423 Causal Feature Selector

Invention: `CausalFeatureSelector`.

Select features based on causal contribution, not mere prediction.

Use for:

- routing;
- distillation data selection;
- safety filtering;
- drift detection;
- ROI prediction.

Goal:

Reduce brittle policies that work only because of accidental correlations.

### W13424 Causal Route Policy

Invention: `CausalRoutePolicy`.

Route based on estimated treatment effect:

- student likely sufficient;
- teacher likely needed;
- retrieval likely helps;
- human review likely prevents harm;
- local device likely violates latency.

The route policy should carry confidence and refutation status.

### W13425 Causal Benchmark Leakage Detector

Invention: `CausalBenchmarkLeakageDetector`.

Detect when benchmark gains are caused by contamination:

- training exposure;
- prompt template overlap;
- synthetic generator overlap;
- public leaderboard overfit;
- hidden label leakage.

If contamination likely caused the gain, the report should downgrade.

### W13426 Counterfactual Fairness Gate

Invention: `CounterfactualFairnessGate`.

For sensitive domains, test whether decisions change under protected-attribute counterfactuals where task-relevant facts remain fixed.

Outputs:

- pass;
- fail;
- not identifiable;
- requires domain review.

This becomes part of enterprise governance for healthcare, finance, insurance, and hiring-like workflows.

### W13427 Synthetic Counterfactual Generator

Invention: `SyntheticCounterfactualGenerator`.

Generate controlled examples:

- change one causal factor;
- hold confounders fixed;
- preserve policy scope;
- mark synthetic provenance.

Use to fill gaps where real counterfactuals are unavailable.

### W13428 Causal Active Learning

Invention: `CausalActiveLearning`.

Ask for labels that reduce causal uncertainty:

- confounded segment;
- high-leverage treatment boundary;
- fairness uncertainty;
- route threshold uncertainty;
- rare outcome.

This makes labeling more valuable than uncertainty sampling alone.

### W13429 Causal Drift Monitor

Invention: `CausalDriftMonitor`.

Detect shifts in:

- treatment effect;
- confounder distribution;
- segment mix;
- outcome definition;
- policy intervention.

If the causal model is stale, the route policy downgrades.

### W13430 Causal Data Contract

Invention: `CausalDataContract`.

Every causal claim needs:

- outcome definition;
- treatment definition;
- confounder set;
- data window;
- identification assumptions;
- refutation tests;
- validity scope.

No ROI or quality uplift claim should ship without this contract.

### W13431 CAUSE-K Metric

Invention: `CAUSE-K`.

Measure causal maturity:

- causal graph coverage;
- identifiability rate;
- refutation pass rate;
- transportability evidence;
- counterfactual eval coverage;
- confounder monitoring;
- causal claim honesty.

CAUSE-K makes product optimization accountable.

### W13432 Causal Simulation Harness

Invention: `CausalHarness`.

Simulate:

- route threshold appears beneficial due to confounding;
- quantization hurts rare class only;
- retrieval helps one domain and harms another;
- benchmark improvement comes from leakage.

Expected:

- causal model blocks broad claim and recommends targeted experiment.

### W13433 Causal Build Sequence

Invention: `CausalBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Causal data contract.
2. Counterfactual eval generator.
3. Spurious correlation detector.
4. Causal ROI model.
5. Uplift distillation.
6. Transportability compiler.
7. Causal route policy.
8. CAUSE-K.

### W13434 Causal Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- DoWhy: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/project/dowhy/
- DoWhy paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2011.04216
- EconML: https://www.microsoft.com/research/project/econml/
- CausalML: https://causalml.readthedocs.io/
- Interventional causal representation learning: https://proceedings.mlr.press/v202/ahuja23a.html

Internal evidence:

- causal contracts;
- counterfactual fixtures;
- refutation reports;
- transportability reports;
- CAUSE-K simulations.

## Model Editing, Machine Unlearning, Representation Steering, And Feature Surgery Layer

Kolm needs a disciplined way to patch, erase, steer, and version model behavior without pretending that all edits are safe. Editing should be scoped, reversible, tested, and connected to artifact lineage.

### W13435 Model Edit Evidence Graph

Invention: `ModelEditEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- edit intent;
- target fact or capability;
- method;
- model location;
- before behavior;
- after behavior;
- specificity;
- generalization;
- side effects;
- rollback path.

Sources:

- ROME: https://rome.baulab.info/
- ROME paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2202.05262
- MEMIT: https://memit.baulab.info/
- MEMIT paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.07229

### W13436 Fact Patch Compiler

Invention: `FactPatchCompiler`.

Compile a fact correction into:

- target prompt set;
- edited association;
- locality test;
- paraphrase generalization test;
- unrelated fact preservation test;
- artifact version diff.

Use model editing only when retraining or distillation is too slow and scope is narrow.

### W13437 Machine Unlearning Manifest

Invention: `UnlearningManifest`.

Every unlearning request should store:

- forget set;
- retain set;
- reason;
- legal basis;
- method;
- benchmark;
- attack test;
- residual risk;
- claim scope.

No system should say "forgotten" without an adversarial test.

### W13438 Retain-Forget Split Compiler

Invention: `RetainForgetSplitCompiler`.

Build high-quality splits:

- specific forget examples;
- paraphrases;
- related non-forget examples;
- utility retain examples;
- adversarial prompts;
- leakage probes.

This prevents unlearning methods from passing weak tests while retaining hidden knowledge.

### W13439 Hazardous Capability Scrubber

Invention: `HazardousCapabilityScrubber`.

For hazardous domains, remove or suppress specific capabilities while preserving benign utility.

Inputs:

- hazard taxonomy;
- WMDP-like tests;
- benign retain set;
- adversarial recovery prompts;
- safety policy.

Outputs:

- scrubbed artifact;
- residual risk;
- refusal scope.

### W13440 Concept Erasure Guard

Invention: `ConceptErasureGuard`.

Concept erasure must be tested for:

- residual concept leakage;
- utility loss;
- proxy concept leakage;
- adversarial recovery;
- subgroup harm.

Use closed-form erasure where possible and treat deep model erasure as scoped, not absolute.

### W13441 Steering Vector Controller

Invention: `SteeringVectorController`.

Use activation steering as a runtime control:

- increase groundedness;
- reduce toxicity;
- increase refusal;
- improve format adherence;
- suppress unsafe tool intent.

Rules:

- steering vector must be scoped;
- effect must be measured;
- side effects must be logged;
- unsafe oversteering must refuse.

### W13442 Representation Firewall

Invention: `RepresentationFirewall`.

Monitor internal representations for forbidden or risky activation states:

- private data feature;
- hazardous capability feature;
- tool abuse feature;
- hallucination feature;
- policy bypass feature.

Use it as a second line of defense alongside output filters.

### W13443 Reversible Edit Journal

Invention: `ReversibleEditJournal`.

Every model edit must be reversible:

- base hash;
- edit delta;
- edit reason;
- evaluator;
- approval;
- rollback delta;
- side-effect report.

This makes model editing operationally safe enough for enterprise use.

### W13444 Edit Specificity Test

Invention: `EditSpecificityTest`.

Measure:

- edited target changed;
- paraphrases changed;
- neighboring facts preserved;
- unrelated facts preserved;
- style preserved;
- safety preserved.

If specificity is poor, the system recommends distillation or retrieval patch instead.

### W13445 Catastrophic Forgetting Guard

Invention: `EditForgettingGuard`.

After edits or unlearning, test:

- core task quality;
- rare-class quality;
- domain quality;
- safety behavior;
- calibration;
- routing behavior.

Do not let a successful deletion destroy the artifact.

### W13446 Customer Data Deletion Compiler

Invention: `CustomerDeletionCompiler`.

When a customer requests deletion:

- trace data lineage;
- identify affected artifacts;
- decide whether removal, restriction, or retrain is required;
- run unlearning or rebuild;
- produce deletion evidence.

This connects legal rights to model operations.

### W13447 Unlearning Attack Harness

Invention: `UnlearningAttackHarness`.

Attack unlearning claims:

- paraphrase recovery;
- evolutionary prompt search;
- nearest-neighbor probe;
- membership inference;
- hidden knowledge test;
- multi-turn recovery.

Only after attacks fail can a claim be upgraded.

### W13448 Edit-To-Distill Handoff

Invention: `EditToDistillHandoff`.

Some edits are temporary patches. Convert stable edits into:

- training examples;
- constraints;
- eval fixtures;
- distillation targets;
- artifact release notes.

This prevents the system from accumulating brittle model surgery.

### W13449 Feature Surgery Compiler

Invention: `FeatureSurgeryCompiler`.

Use SAE features and causal patching to remove, suppress, or amplify targeted features.

Steps:

- identify feature;
- prove causal effect;
- test collateral damage;
- choose edit method;
- verify behavior and internals.

### W13450 Model Patch Marketplace

Invention: `ModelPatchMarketplace`.

Share scoped patches:

- fact update;
- safety patch;
- domain correction;
- format patch;
- redaction patch.

Every patch includes:

- model scope;
- tests;
- side effects;
- license;
- rollback.

### W13451 EDIT-K Metric

Invention: `EDIT-K`.

Measure model-edit maturity:

- specificity;
- generalization;
- retain quality;
- attack resistance;
- reversibility;
- lineage completeness;
- legal evidence.

### W13452 Model Edit Simulation Harness

Invention: `EditHarness`.

Simulate:

- narrow fact patch;
- broad harmful unlearning;
- concept erasure;
- steering vector deployment;
- customer deletion request.

Expected:

- system chooses edit, retrain, retrieval, or refusal based on scope and risk.

### W13453 Model Edit Build Sequence

Invention: `EditBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Edit evidence graph.
2. Retain-forget split compiler.
3. Edit specificity test.
4. Reversible edit journal.
5. Unlearning attack harness.
6. Customer deletion compiler.
7. Feature surgery compiler.
8. EDIT-K.

### W13454 Model Editing Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- ROME: https://rome.baulab.info/
- ROME paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2202.05262
- MEMIT: https://memit.baulab.info/
- MEMIT paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.07229
- LEACE concept erasure: https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.03819
- WMDP benchmark: https://www.wmdp.ai/
- TOFU benchmark: https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.06121

Internal evidence:

- edit manifests;
- retain-forget fixtures;
- attack harness results;
- deletion evidence packets;
- EDIT-K reports.

## Adversarial Robustness, Prompt Injection, Trojan Detection, Red Team, And AI Security Layer

Kolm should treat every artifact as a security object. The product must map attacks to mitigations, tests, runtime controls, and evidence, not rely on a single safety filter.

### W13455 AI Threat Evidence Graph

Invention: `AIThreatEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- threat;
- tactic;
- technique;
- affected surface;
- exploit path;
- mitigation;
- test;
- detection;
- residual risk.

Sources:

- NIST adversarial machine learning taxonomy: https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/ai/100/2/e2025/final
- OWASP LLM Top 10: https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/
- MITRE ATLAS: https://atlas.mitre.org/

### W13456 Prompt Injection Attack Compiler

Invention: `PromptInjectionAttackCompiler`.

Generate direct and indirect prompt injection tests:

- web content;
- retrieved documents;
- email;
- tool output;
- PDF;
- code comment;
- calendar event.

Each attack maps to:

- expected containment;
- violated boundary;
- mitigation;
- regression test.

### W13457 Jailbreak Replay Lab

Invention: `JailbreakReplayLab`.

Store and replay jailbreaks:

- single-turn;
- multi-turn;
- encoding tricks;
- roleplay;
- policy inversion;
- tool-mediated;
- retrieval-mediated.

Replay against:

- teacher;
- student;
- route;
- safety filter;
- refusal compiler.

### W13458 Tool Output Sanitizer

Invention: `ToolOutputSanitizer`.

Tool outputs can carry instructions. Sanitize:

- hidden prompt injection;
- credential requests;
- shell commands;
- malicious URLs;
- data exfiltration instructions;
- policy override text.

Use structured trust levels for every tool result.

### W13459 Data Poisoning Detector

Invention: `CapturePoisoningDetector`.

Detect poisoned capture traces:

- anomalous labels;
- rare trigger phrases;
- policy override attempts;
- suspicious repetition;
- inconsistent teacher outputs;
- domain outliers.

Poisoned traces quarantine before distillation.

### W13460 Backdoor And Trojan Scanner

Invention: `ModelTrojanScanner`.

Scan artifacts for:

- trigger sensitivity;
- anomalous activation cluster;
- pruning response;
- rare-token behavior;
- input patch behavior;
- suspicious feature pathway.

Output:

- clean;
- suspect;
- blocked;
- needs red-team.

### W13461 Vector Store Security Auditor

Invention: `VectorStoreSecurityAuditor`.

Audit retrieval:

- poisoned documents;
- stale documents;
- unauthorized documents;
- adversarial chunks;
- embedding collisions;
- metadata spoofing;
- cross-tenant leakage.

RAG cannot be trusted without retrieval security.

### W13462 Secure Retrieval Policy

Invention: `SecureRetrievalPolicy`.

Every retrieval result carries:

- source trust;
- tenant;
- freshness;
- license;
- sensitivity;
- injection risk;
- citation status.

The model receives sanitized content plus metadata, not raw untrusted authority.

### W13463 Continuous Red-Team Fuzzer

Invention: `ContinuousRedTeamFuzzer`.

Fuzz:

- prompts;
- tool outputs;
- retrieval chunks;
- route policies;
- validators;
- safety filters;
- model memory.

Every found issue becomes:

- regression case;
- mitigation task;
- severity score;
- evidence graph node.

### W13464 Attack-To-Training Compiler

Invention: `AttackToTrainingCompiler`.

Convert successful attacks into:

- negative examples;
- refusal examples;
- tool precondition tests;
- retrieval filters;
- policy constraints;
- active learning tasks.

This closes the loop between red team and distillation.

### W13465 Adversarial Benchmark Generator

Invention: `AdversarialBenchmarkGenerator`.

Generate benchmark variants:

- obfuscated intent;
- irrelevant distractors;
- poisoned context;
- contradictory citations;
- tool misuse;
- jailbreak wrapper.

The benchmark should measure robustness, not just clean-task quality.

### W13466 Model Extraction Risk Gate

Invention: `ModelExtractionRiskGate`.

Detect and mitigate:

- high-volume probing;
- boundary search;
- logit leakage;
- repeated rare queries;
- adaptive extraction;
- artifact marketplace scraping.

Actions:

- rate limit;
- watermark;
- refuse;
- require auth;
- return lower-detail outputs.

### W13467 Denial-Of-Wallet Guard

Invention: `DenialOfWalletGuard`.

Protect against cost attacks:

- long context abuse;
- tool loop abuse;
- expensive provider forcing;
- repeated proof jobs;
- benchmark spam;
- image/video generation spam.

Output:

- budget throttle;
- cheaper fallback;
- approval required;
- block.

### W13468 Agentic Abuse Policy

Invention: `AgenticAbusePolicy`.

Map agent risks:

- excessive agency;
- hidden side effects;
- tool chain escalation;
- credential exposure;
- self-replication;
- unsafe browsing;
- external communication.

Tie each to explicit preconditions and monitoring.

### W13469 Security Event Kill Chain

Invention: `AIKillChainRecorder`.

For incidents, record:

- initial prompt;
- retrieved context;
- tool output;
- model decision;
- side effect;
- policy failure;
- mitigation;
- recovery.

This creates incident learning data.

### W13470 Adversarial Residual Risk Packet

Invention: `AdversarialRiskPacket`.

Every high-risk artifact gets:

- tested threats;
- untested threats;
- known bypasses;
- red-team date;
- mitigation status;
- residual risk;
- owner.

### W13471 SEC-K Metric

Invention: `SEC-K`.

Measure AI security:

- threat coverage;
- red-team pass rate;
- prompt injection containment;
- poisoning detection;
- backdoor scan coverage;
- retrieval security;
- incident response time;
- residual risk closure.

### W13472 Security Simulation Harness

Invention: `SecurityHarness`.

Simulate:

- indirect prompt injection in retrieval;
- poisoned trace in distillation;
- tool output command injection;
- backdoor trigger;
- extraction attack;
- denial-of-wallet attack.

Expected:

- control catches or downgrades with clear evidence.

### W13473 Security Build Sequence

Invention: `SecurityBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. AI threat evidence graph.
2. Prompt injection compiler.
3. Tool output sanitizer.
4. Capture poisoning detector.
5. Vector store auditor.
6. Continuous red-team fuzzer.
7. Attack-to-training compiler.
8. SEC-K.

### W13474 Security Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- NIST AI 100-2e2025: https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/ai/100/2/e2025/final
- OWASP LLM Top 10: https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/
- OWASP MCP Top 10: https://owasp.org/www-project-mcp-top-10/
- MITRE ATLAS: https://atlas.mitre.org/
- NIST trojan detection publication: https://www.nist.gov/publications/baseline-pruning-based-approach-trojan-detection-neural-networks

Internal evidence:

- attack corpus;
- red-team reports;
- sanitizer fixtures;
- poisoning detector reports;
- SEC-K simulations.

## Reproducible Research, Experiment Governance, Benchmark Validity, And Autonomous Science Layer

Kolm's research engine should not be a pile of ideas. It should become an experiment factory where every invention has hypotheses, ablations, replication packets, negative results, and validity checks.

### W13475 Research Evidence Graph

Invention: `ResearchEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- hypothesis;
- experiment;
- dataset;
- model;
- metric;
- ablation;
- result;
- negative result;
- replication status;
- product decision.

Sources:

- NeurIPS checklist: https://nips.cc/public/guides/PaperChecklist
- ACM artifact badging: https://www.acm.org/publications/policies/artifact-review-and-badging-current
- NeurIPS reproducibility report: https://arxiv.org/abs/2003.12206

### W13476 Experiment Planner

Invention: `ExperimentPlanner`.

For each roadmap wave, generate:

- hypothesis;
- baseline;
- treatment;
- metric;
- budget;
- expected failure mode;
- decision threshold.

No research wave is implementation-ready until its experiment plan exists.

### W13477 Hypothesis Registry

Invention: `HypothesisRegistry`.

Store hypotheses before running experiments:

- claim;
- expected direction;
- metric;
- sample;
- stopping rule;
- owner.

This prevents narrative retrofitting after results are known.

### W13478 Ablation Compiler

Invention: `AblationCompiler`.

Generate ablations:

- remove component;
- change threshold;
- swap model;
- remove feature;
- change data split;
- disable route fallback.

A product claim is weak if the system never tested whether the component caused the improvement.

### W13479 Replication Packet Compiler

Invention: `ReplicationPacketCompiler`.

Package:

- code hash;
- data manifest;
- environment;
- command;
- seed;
- expected metrics;
- known nondeterminism;
- license.

This makes internal research reproducible before publication.

### W13480 Negative Result Ledger

Invention: `NegativeResultLedger`.

Record failed ideas:

- what was tried;
- why it failed;
- what metric moved;
- what side effect appeared;
- whether to retry later.

This prevents the team from rediscovering the same dead ends.

### W13481 Benchmark Contamination Guard

Invention: `BenchmarkContaminationGuard`.

Detect:

- training overlap;
- public leaderboard leakage;
- prompt template reuse;
- synthetic generator reuse;
- hidden duplicate examples;
- eval target in capture logs.

If contamination exists, downgrade the benchmark result.

### W13482 Metric Validity Auditor

Invention: `MetricValidityAuditor`.

For every metric:

- what it measures;
- what it does not measure;
- known failure cases;
- correlation with human judgment;
- sensitivity to dataset;
- gaming risk.

K-Score must have this auditor to become the yardstick.

### W13483 Human Evaluation Calibration

Invention: `HumanEvalCalibration`.

Calibrate human labels:

- inter-rater agreement;
- rubric clarity;
- fatigue;
- domain expertise;
- disagreement resolution;
- gold examples.

Without calibrated humans, "human preference" is weak evidence.

### W13484 Experiment Budget Optimizer

Invention: `ExperimentBudgetOptimizer`.

Allocate compute and labeling to experiments by:

- expected information gain;
- revenue impact;
- risk reduction;
- dependency unlock;
- probability of success;
- cost.

This keeps the massive roadmap rational.

### W13485 Lab Notebook Provenance

Invention: `LabNotebookProvenance`.

Every experiment notebook should bind:

- author;
- code hash;
- data hash;
- model hash;
- environment;
- run id;
- result artifact.

This prevents ad hoc local experiments from becoming unverifiable product claims.

### W13486 Statistical Significance Gate

Invention: `StatsSignificanceGate`.

Before promoting an improvement, check:

- confidence interval;
- multiple comparisons;
- segment size;
- effect size;
- practical significance;
- regression risk.

Do not ship tiny noisy improvements as breakthroughs.

### W13487 Research Adjudication Protocol

Invention: `ResearchAdjudicationProtocol`.

When experiments conflict:

- compare data;
- compare methods;
- inspect assumptions;
- run deciding experiment;
- record dissent;
- decide claim language.

This keeps research conflict productive.

### W13488 Publication Compiler

Invention: `PublicationCompiler`.

For publishable work, generate:

- abstract;
- method;
- dataset statement;
- ethics statement;
- limitations;
- reproducibility checklist;
- artifact packet.

This supports the user's roadmap of publishing serious distillation research.

### W13489 Research Grant Marketplace

Invention: `ResearchGrantMarketplace`.

Invite external researchers to work on:

- K-Score calibration;
- private distillation;
- circuit alignment;
- robust unlearning;
- federated benchmarks.

Each grant produces:

- replication packet;
- license;
- benchmark contribution;
- product decision.

### W13490 Roadmap Evidence Gate

Invention: `RoadmapEvidenceGate`.

Every wave must state:

- problem;
- invention;
- expected metric movement;
- proof needed;
- owner;
- dependency;
- current status.

No wave is allowed to be "done" by vibes.

### W13491 SCI-K Metric

Invention: `SCI-K`.

Measure research quality:

- hypothesis registration;
- ablation coverage;
- replication packet completeness;
- negative result recording;
- contamination guard coverage;
- statistical validity;
- product decision traceability.

SCI-K turns research maturity into an operational metric.

### W13492 Research Simulation Harness

Invention: `ResearchHarness`.

Simulate:

- false positive improvement;
- contaminated benchmark;
- missing ablation;
- noisy human labels;
- unreplicable notebook;
- contradictory experiments.

Expected:

- claim is blocked or downgraded until evidence improves.

### W13493 Research Build Sequence

Invention: `ResearchBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Hypothesis registry.
2. Experiment planner.
3. Replication packet compiler.
4. Ablation compiler.
5. Benchmark contamination guard.
6. Metric validity auditor.
7. Negative result ledger.
8. SCI-K.

### W13494 Research Sources And Internal Evidence

Primary sources:

- NeurIPS Paper Checklist: https://nips.cc/public/guides/PaperChecklist
- ACM Artifact Review and Badging: https://www.acm.org/publications/policies/artifact-review-and-badging-current
- NeurIPS reproducibility program: https://arxiv.org/abs/2003.12206
- Nature Methods reproducibility standards: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41592-021-01256-7

Internal evidence:

- experiment plans;
- hypothesis registry;
- ablation reports;
- replication packets;
- contamination scans;
- SCI-K reports.

## Hardware-Aware Quantization, Compiler IR, Runtime Scheduling, Edge Deployment, And Energy Intelligence Layer

Kolm's product will only feel 100x if compiled artifacts run materially cheaper, faster, safer, and more portably than raw model calls. This layer makes runtime performance a first-class research object: quantization is chosen by task and hardware, compiler targets are proved by conformance, memory is scheduled like a system resource, and energy becomes part of the product score.

### W13495 Hardware-Aware Optimization Evidence Graph

Invention: `HardwareOptimizationEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- source model;
- student artifact;
- quantization;
- compiler;
- runtime;
- device;
- kernel;
- memory plan;
- benchmark;
- energy profile;
- failure mode.

Primary sources:

- FlashAttention: https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.14135
- vLLM documentation: https://docs.vllm.ai/
- TensorRT-LLM: https://docs.nvidia.com/tensorrt-llm/
- Triton: https://openai.com/index/triton/
- TVM Relax: https://tvm.apache.org/docs/deep_dive/relax/index.html

### W13496 Task-Adaptive Quantization Planner

Invention: `TaskAdaptiveQuantizationPlanner`.

Choose quantization per task, not by default:

- extraction;
- classification;
- reasoning;
- code;
- healthcare text;
- multimodal;
- tool calling;
- safety filter.

Inputs:

- activation distribution;
- rare-class sensitivity;
- calibration set;
- target hardware;
- latency target;
- memory target;
- quality floor.

Output:

- recommended quantization;
- rejected alternatives;
- calibration proof;
- fallback plan.

### W13497 Activation-Aware Quantization Compiler

Invention: `ActivationAwareQuantCompiler`.

Use activation statistics from real customer traces:

- outlier channels;
- attention spikes;
- rare token paths;
- safety feature activations;
- tool-use activations.

Then choose:

- AWQ-like protected weights;
- SmoothQuant-style activation smoothing;
- GPTQ-style second-order weight reconstruction;
- mixed precision for risky layers.

This is how Kolm turns proprietary telemetry into runtime advantage.

### W13498 Rare-Class Quantization Guard

Invention: `RareClassQuantizationGuard`.

Quantization often hides aggregate quality loss. Guard rare classes:

- PHI class;
- fraud class;
- refusal class;
- tool safety class;
- low-resource language;
- domain-specific code path.

The guard blocks quantized artifacts that pass average K-Score but fail critical slices.

### W13499 Quantization Recipe Search

Invention: `QuantizationRecipeSearch`.

Search:

- bit width by layer;
- group size;
- activation smoothing;
- KV cache quantization;
- outlier retention;
- embedding precision;
- output head precision.

Objective:

- maximize K-Score under memory, latency, energy, and device constraints.

This is the best quantization tool: not one method, but an evidence-guided recipe searcher.

### W13500 Quantization Distortion Attribution

Invention: `QuantizationDistortionAttributor`.

When quality drops, attribute the cause:

- layer;
- head;
- MLP block;
- token class;
- feature;
- circuit;
- calibration gap.

Output:

- restore precision here;
- collect calibration examples;
- use alternate quantizer;
- keep teacher fallback for this slice.

### W13501 KV Cache Quantization Controller

Invention: `KVCacheQuantizationController`.

KV cache dominates serving memory for long contexts. Control:

- bit width by request;
- retention by token importance;
- protection for high-attention evidence tokens;
- compression for low-value context;
- fallback to full precision for risky spans.

This complements ITKV and should be measured against factuality and citation grounding, not only throughput.

### W13502 Quantization-Aware Distillation Loop

Invention: `QuantAwareDistillLoop`.

Train the student for the quantized runtime it will actually use.

Loop:

- train student;
- quantize;
- evaluate slice failures;
- generate targeted examples;
- distill again with quantization noise;
- repeat until cost-quality frontier stabilizes.

This avoids producing students that are only good before deployment.

### W13503 Quantization Safety Case

Invention: `QuantizationSafetyCase`.

For regulated artifacts, document:

- quantizer;
- calibration data;
- protected layers;
- slice evals;
- failure cases;
- fallback policy;
- approved devices.

This lets enterprise customers trust low-bit deployment.

### W13504 Quantized Artifact Compatibility Matrix

Invention: `QuantizedArtifactCompatibilityMatrix`.

Track which quantized artifact works on:

- CUDA;
- ROCm;
- Metal;
- Vulkan;
- WebGPU;
- WebNN;
- CPU;
- NPU;
- mobile runtime.

Include:

- kernel support;
- performance;
- numerical drift;
- unsupported ops;
- known bugs.

### W13505 Dynamic Precision Runtime

Invention: `DynamicPrecisionRuntime`.

Change precision at runtime:

- low precision for easy tokens;
- higher precision for uncertain spans;
- higher precision for safety-critical decisions;
- higher precision near tool calls;
- full precision during drift.

This creates a cost-quality dial invisible to the user but visible to telemetry.

### W13506 Quantization Benchmark Firewall

Invention: `QuantizationBenchmarkFirewall`.

Prevent misleading low-bit claims:

- require slice reports;
- require calibration disclosure;
- require device report;
- require energy report;
- require long-context report;
- require safety slice report.

Average benchmark-only quantization claims should be downgraded.

### W13507 Quantization Patch Marketplace

Invention: `QuantizationRecipeMarketplace`.

Share hardware-specific recipes:

- model family;
- device;
- quantizer;
- calibration set class;
- slice results;
- kernel requirement;
- license.

This creates a defensible runtime recipe economy.

### W13508 QUANT-K Metric

Invention: `QUANT-K`.

Measure quantization quality:

- compression ratio;
- K-Score retention;
- rare-slice retention;
- latency reduction;
- energy reduction;
- compatibility coverage;
- safety case completeness.

### W13509 Quantization Simulation Harness

Invention: `QuantizationHarness`.

Simulate:

- easy extraction task;
- rare PHI class;
- long-context RAG;
- tool-calling workflow;
- mobile edge device;
- low-resource language.

Expected:

- planner chooses different recipes for each rather than one universal quantization.

### W13510 Quantization Build Sequence

Invention: `QuantizationBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Quantized artifact compatibility matrix.
2. Slice-aware quantization eval.
3. Quantization distortion attribution.
4. Recipe search.
5. Dynamic precision runtime.
6. Quantization-aware distillation loop.
7. Safety case.
8. QUANT-K.

### W13511 Quantization Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- GPTQ: https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.17323
- SmoothQuant: https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.10438
- AWQ: https://huggingface.co/papers/2306.00978
- bitsandbytes documentation: https://huggingface.co/docs/bitsandbytes/v0.45.4/en/index
- TorchAO paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.16099

Internal evidence:

- quantization recipes;
- slice evals;
- compatibility matrix;
- energy reports;
- QUANT-K simulations.

### W13512 Hardware-Aware Calibration Set Builder

Invention: `HardwareCalibrationSetBuilder`.

Build calibration sets for:

- activation outliers;
- long prompts;
- rare tokens;
- high-safety examples;
- tool calls;
- domain-specific language;
- multimodal inputs.

Calibration must reflect the target deployment, not a generic sample.

### W13513 Quantization Refusal Policy

Invention: `QuantizationRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse low-bit deployment when:

- rare slice fails;
- safety slice fails;
- runtime lacks kernel support;
- calibration set is not representative;
- energy savings are not real;
- fallback unavailable.

The product should recommend the nearest deployable precision.

### W13514 Quantization Sources Continuity Note

This quantization layer builds on DAQ and ITKV but goes further:

- DAQ optimizes student training;
- ITKV optimizes cache memory;
- this layer chooses the whole quantization strategy across model, cache, compiler, runtime, device, safety, and marketplace.

## Compiler IR, Kernel Autotuning, Runtime Portability, And Artifact Lowering Layer

Kolm needs a compiler that treats `.kolm` as a high-level artifact and lowers it into runtime-specific programs with tests, conformance, and rollback. This is how "run anywhere" becomes real engineering.

### W13515 Kolm Portable IR

Invention: `KolmPortableIR`.

Represent:

- model graph;
- tokenizer;
- preprocessor;
- postprocessor;
- validators;
- route policy;
- safety filter;
- memory plan;
- quantization plan.

Then lower into:

- ONNX Runtime GenAI;
- TVM Relax;
- IREE;
- TensorRT-LLM;
- MLC LLM;
- ExecuTorch;
- WebNN.

### W13516 Multi-Level Lowering Pipeline

Invention: `MultiLevelArtifactLowering`.

Levels:

1. `.kolm` semantic artifact.
2. portable graph IR.
3. runtime graph.
4. tensor program.
5. kernel schedule.
6. device package.
7. runtime receipt.

Each level has verification fixtures and rollback.

### W13517 Compiler Conformance Suite

Invention: `CompilerConformanceSuite`.

For every backend:

- same inputs;
- same tokenizer behavior;
- same schema validation;
- same safety policy;
- bounded numerical drift;
- same refusal behavior.

This prevents "portable" from meaning "maybe similar".

### W13518 Kernel Autotuning Lab

Invention: `KernelAutotuningLab`.

Autotune:

- matmul;
- attention;
- layernorm;
- rotary embedding;
- sampling;
- tokenizer;
- validation kernels.

Targets:

- CUDA;
- ROCm;
- Metal;
- Vulkan;
- CPU SIMD;
- NPU;
- WebGPU.

### W13519 Triton Kernel Generator

Invention: `KolmTritonKernelGenerator`.

Generate and tune Triton kernels for:

- quantized matmul;
- sparse attention;
- KV cache copy;
- fused sampling;
- feature monitor;
- validator acceleration.

Every generated kernel must come with numerical and performance tests.

### W13520 TVM Relax Lowering Target

Invention: `KolmToTVMRelax`.

Use TVM Relax-style dynamic graph optimization for:

- dynamic sequence length;
- route-dependent shapes;
- optional retrieval;
- streaming output;
- validator branches.

This lets Kolm compile dynamic AI workflows, not just static neural nets.

### W13521 IREE Embedded Target

Invention: `KolmToIREE`.

Use IREE-style deployment for:

- mobile;
- embedded;
- edge accelerators;
- WebAssembly-like packaging;
- ahead-of-time runtime delivery.

The goal is small, reliable artifacts for constrained devices.

### W13522 ONNX Runtime GenAI Target

Invention: `KolmToOnnxRuntimeGenAI`.

Generate ONNX Runtime GenAI packages with:

- model config;
- tokenizer config;
- quantization;
- provider selection;
- test vectors;
- metadata.

This targets Windows, local devices, and enterprise ONNX ecosystems.

### W13523 TensorRT-LLM Target

Invention: `KolmToTensorRTLLM`.

Generate TensorRT-LLM deployment packs:

- engine build plan;
- precision mode;
- tensor parallel settings;
- plugin requirements;
- benchmark profile;
- fallback route.

Use for high-throughput NVIDIA deployments.

### W13524 MLC LLM Target

Invention: `KolmToMLCLLM`.

Compile selected artifacts to MLC LLM-style runtime targets:

- Vulkan;
- Metal;
- CUDA;
- WebGPU;
- mobile apps.

This is a path to broad device support without hand-maintaining every runtime.

### W13525 Compiler Pass Marketplace

Invention: `CompilerPassMarketplace`.

Share passes:

- quantization pass;
- fusion pass;
- safety monitor pass;
- tokenizer optimization;
- runtime packaging;
- proof instrumentation.

Each pass includes:

- supported IR version;
- tests;
- safety class;
- license;
- performance evidence.

### W13526 Numerical Drift Auditor

Invention: `NumericalDriftAuditor`.

Compare outputs across compiler targets:

- logits;
- tokens;
- schema validity;
- safety decisions;
- score decisions;
- feature monitors.

Flag drift by severity and affected product claim.

### W13527 Compiler Rollback Receipt

Invention: `CompilerRollbackReceipt`.

Every compiled package carries:

- compiler version;
- pass list;
- target runtime;
- test vector results;
- performance profile;
- rollback package.

This makes compiler changes operationally safe.

### W13528 Artifact Lowering Debugger

Invention: `ArtifactLoweringDebugger`.

When a target fails:

- identify unsupported op;
- identify tokenizer mismatch;
- identify precision drift;
- identify kernel issue;
- identify memory plan issue.

Output:

- patch target;
- change IR;
- choose different runtime;
- refuse target.

### W13529 Compiler Security Sandbox

Invention: `CompilerSecuritySandbox`.

Compiler passes are code execution. Sandbox:

- third-party passes;
- model conversion scripts;
- kernel generators;
- package builders.

Record supply-chain evidence for every compiled artifact.

### W13530 COMPILER-K Metric

Invention: `COMPILER-K`.

Measure compiler maturity:

- target coverage;
- conformance pass rate;
- numerical drift control;
- performance gain;
- rollback coverage;
- security sandbox coverage;
- pass provenance.

### W13531 Compiler Simulation Harness

Invention: `CompilerHarness`.

Simulate:

- ONNX target mismatch;
- TensorRT plugin missing;
- WebNN unsupported op;
- Metal precision drift;
- tokenizer mismatch;
- unsafe compiler pass.

Expected:

- compiler selects, patches, or refuses the target with evidence.

### W13532 Compiler Build Sequence

Invention: `CompilerBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Portable IR.
2. Conformance suite.
3. ONNX Runtime GenAI target.
4. ExecuTorch or IREE target.
5. TensorRT-LLM target.
6. Numerical drift auditor.
7. Compiler rollback receipts.
8. COMPILER-K.

### W13533 Compiler Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- TVM Relax: https://tvm.apache.org/docs/deep_dive/relax/index.html
- TVM paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/1802.04799
- MLIR paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2002.11054
- IREE docs: https://iree.dev/reference/
- ONNX Runtime GenAI: https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/genai/
- TensorRT-LLM: https://docs.nvidia.com/tensorrt-llm/

Internal evidence:

- IR schema;
- target packages;
- conformance fixtures;
- drift reports;
- COMPILER-K reports.

### W13534 Compiler Refusal Policy

Invention: `CompilerRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse a target when:

- unsupported op changes semantics;
- tokenizer mismatch cannot be bounded;
- safety policy cannot lower;
- numerical drift affects decision;
- runtime lacks required isolation;
- target package cannot be reproduced.

## Memory Scheduling, Attention Systems, Continuous Batching, Streaming, And Inference Economics Layer

Serving economics depend on memory as much as FLOPs. Kolm should compile memory behavior: prefill, decode, KV cache, streaming, batching, and speculative execution become planned resources.

### W13535 Runtime Memory Evidence Graph

Invention: `RuntimeMemoryEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- request;
- context length;
- KV allocation;
- prefill cost;
- decode cost;
- batch group;
- cache hit;
- eviction;
- spill;
- latency.

Sources:

- vLLM and PagedAttention: https://docs.vllm.ai/
- FlashAttention: https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.14135
- TensorRT-LLM: https://docs.nvidia.com/tensorrt-llm/

### W13536 Paged KV Cache Planner

Invention: `PagedKVPlanner`.

Plan KV cache like virtual memory:

- page size;
- allocation;
- sharing;
- eviction;
- compaction;
- prefix reuse;
- tenant isolation.

This lets Kolm serve many workflows without wasting memory.

### W13537 Importance-Tiered Context Memory

Invention: `ContextImportanceMemory`.

Rank context tokens by:

- citation importance;
- instruction importance;
- safety importance;
- recency;
- attention score;
- tool dependency.

Use ranking for:

- KV compression;
- eviction;
- retrieval refresh;
- teacher fallback.

### W13538 Continuous Batching Optimizer

Invention: `ContinuousBatchingOptimizer`.

Batch requests by:

- decode stage;
- latency class;
- model;
- precision;
- context length;
- tenant priority;
- safety risk.

Optimize throughput without violating interactive latency.

### W13539 Streaming Compilation

Invention: `StreamingArtifactCompiler`.

Compile streaming behavior:

- first token target;
- chunk size;
- validator checkpoints;
- citation checkpoints;
- tool-call pause;
- rollback of partial output.

Streaming must be correct, not just fast.

### W13540 Speculative Decoding Planner

Invention: `SpeculativeDecodingPlanner`.

Choose draft model or student:

- acceptance rate;
- risk class;
- cost reduction;
- latency reduction;
- safety slice;
- fallback model.

Do not use speculative decoding for high-risk spans unless verification is strong.

### W13541 Prefix Cache Governance

Invention: `PrefixCacheGovernance`.

Prefix caching can leak or cross tenants. Govern:

- cache scope;
- tenant;
- sensitivity;
- policy version;
- prompt hash;
- expiration;
- invalidation.

This makes caching enterprise-safe.

### W13542 Memory Pressure Degradation Plan

Invention: `MemoryPressureDegradationPlan`.

Under memory pressure:

- lower context;
- compress KV;
- route smaller model;
- disable expensive branch;
- fallback to teacher;
- queue request;
- refuse high-risk action.

Each degradation has a customer-visible reason.

### W13543 Long Context Quality Guard

Invention: `LongContextQualityGuard`.

Long context can look supported while quality degrades. Guard:

- citation grounding;
- lost-in-middle;
- instruction retention;
- stale memory conflict;
- context compression loss.

Route to retrieval or chunking when context is unsafe.

### W13544 Attention Kernel Selector

Invention: `AttentionKernelSelector`.

Choose attention implementation:

- FlashAttention;
- paged attention;
- sliding window;
- sparse attention;
- block attention;
- CPU fallback;
- WebGPU attention.

Selection depends on context, hardware, precision, and safety requirements.

### W13545 Request Shape Profiler

Invention: `RequestShapeProfiler`.

Profile:

- input length;
- output length;
- tool count;
- retrieval count;
- batchability;
- memory footprint;
- cacheability.

Use this for pricing, routing, scheduling, and product ROI.

### W13546 Inference Cost Attribution

Invention: `InferenceCostAttributor`.

Attribute cost to:

- prefill;
- decode;
- retrieval;
- tool calls;
- proof;
- safety filters;
- retries;
- cache misses.

This makes ROI calculators honest and actionable.

### W13547 Latency SLO Compiler

Invention: `LatencySLOCompiler`.

Compile user SLOs into:

- model choice;
- precision;
- batch policy;
- queue policy;
- fallback policy;
- streaming policy.

If SLO and quality conflict, return the tradeoff explicitly.

### W13548 Memory Safety Monitor

Invention: `MemorySafetyMonitor`.

Monitor:

- OOM risk;
- cache fragmentation;
- tenant cache isolation;
- request starvation;
- batch unfairness;
- spill latency.

Turn memory incidents into evidence and scheduler improvements.

### W13549 SERVE-K Metric

Invention: `SERVE-K`.

Measure serving maturity:

- throughput;
- tail latency;
- memory utilization;
- cache hit rate;
- degradation correctness;
- cost attribution;
- tenant fairness.

### W13550 Runtime Scheduling Simulation

Invention: `SchedulingHarness`.

Simulate:

- burst traffic;
- long-context request;
- cache poisoning risk;
- tenant priority conflict;
- memory pressure;
- provider outage.

Expected:

- scheduler preserves SLOs or degrades explicitly.

### W13551 Runtime Scheduling Build Sequence

Invention: `RuntimeSchedulingBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Request shape profiler.
2. Inference cost attribution.
3. Paged KV planner.
4. Prefix cache governance.
5. Continuous batching optimizer.
6. Latency SLO compiler.
7. Memory pressure degradation.
8. SERVE-K.

### W13552 Runtime Scheduling Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- vLLM docs: https://docs.vllm.ai/
- PagedAttention paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.06180
- FlashAttention: https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.14135
- FlashAttention-3: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.08608
- TensorRT-LLM: https://docs.nvidia.com/tensorrt-llm/

Internal evidence:

- scheduling simulator;
- memory traces;
- cost attribution reports;
- latency SLO reports;
- SERVE-K reports.

### W13553 Runtime Refusal Policy

Invention: `RuntimeRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse or delay when:

- memory pressure would break safety;
- latency SLO impossible;
- cache isolation unavailable;
- fallback not ready;
- long-context quality guard fails.

### W13554 Runtime Economics Passport

Invention: `RuntimeEconomicsPassport`.

Every artifact carries:

- expected cost;
- expected latency;
- memory profile;
- cache profile;
- scale curve;
- degradation plan;
- target hardware.

## Edge, Mobile, Browser, Embedded, WebNN, ExecuTorch, LiteRT, And Any-Device Deployment Layer

The "enterprise models down to any device" product surface needs a device compiler, not a generic export button. Device deployment must include capability detection, package generation, offline behavior, telemetry, battery/thermal limits, and rollback.

### W13555 Device Deployment Evidence Graph

Invention: `DeviceDeploymentEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- device class;
- runtime;
- accelerator;
- memory;
- battery;
- thermal;
- offline mode;
- package;
- benchmark;
- telemetry;
- rollback.

Sources:

- ExecuTorch: https://docs.pytorch.org/executorch/stable/index.html
- LiteRT: https://ai.google.dev/edge/litert/overview
- WebNN: https://www.w3.org/TR/webnn/
- MLC LLM: https://llm.mlc.ai/docs/get_started/introduction.html

### W13556 Device Capability Probe

Invention: `DeviceCapabilityProbe`.

Probe:

- CPU;
- GPU;
- NPU;
- memory;
- supported runtimes;
- WebGPU;
- WebNN;
- battery state;
- thermal state.

Then choose artifact package and quality mode.

### W13557 Edge Package Compiler

Invention: `EdgePackageCompiler`.

Generate:

- model package;
- tokenizer;
- validators;
- policy;
- telemetry hooks;
- fallback config;
- rollback package.

Targets:

- iOS;
- Android;
- desktop;
- browser;
- embedded Linux;
- worker runtime.

### W13558 ExecuTorch Target Pack

Invention: `KolmExecuTorchPack`.

Use ExecuTorch-style deployment for PyTorch-derived artifacts:

- export graph;
- quantize;
- package;
- run device tests;
- record backend support.

Attach device-specific evidence to the artifact.

### W13559 LiteRT Target Pack

Invention: `KolmLiteRTPack`.

Use LiteRT-style deployment for Google AI Edge ecosystems:

- conversion;
- quantization;
- acceleration;
- on-device benchmark;
- compatibility notes.

### W13560 WebNN Browser Target

Invention: `KolmWebNNTarget`.

Compile browser-capable artifacts:

- WebNN graph;
- WebGPU fallback;
- WASM fallback;
- local storage policy;
- no-secret telemetry;
- offline mode.

This opens zero-install local AI for demos, docs, and customer apps.

### W13561 Device Thermal Governor

Invention: `DeviceThermalGovernor`.

Runtime adjusts:

- model size;
- precision;
- batch size;
- output length;
- refresh rate;
- fallback.

Goal:

Keep device experience reliable instead of benchmark-fast for 30 seconds.

### W13562 Battery-Aware Inference Planner

Invention: `BatteryAwarePlanner`.

Plan inference by:

- battery level;
- charging state;
- energy per token;
- task urgency;
- offline need;
- user preference.

For mobile, energy is product quality.

### W13563 Offline Sync Protocol

Invention: `OfflineArtifactSync`.

Support:

- artifact download;
- signature verification;
- policy sync;
- local eval;
- usage telemetry queue;
- revocation check on reconnect.

This enables regulated field deployments.

### W13564 Device Telemetry Minimizer

Invention: `DeviceTelemetryMinimizer`.

Collect only:

- aggregate latency;
- energy bucket;
- error class;
- artifact hash;
- device class;
- no raw prompt unless explicitly allowed.

This keeps edge deployment privacy-respecting.

### W13565 Browser Demo Runner Contract

Invention: `BrowserKolmRunnerContract`.

For homepage/demo product moments:

- runs locally when possible;
- displays artifact manifest;
- verifies signature;
- shows privacy mode;
- falls back gracefully if unsupported.

This gives frontend a real product primitive instead of a fake video widget.

### W13566 Edge Model Update Policy

Invention: `EdgeUpdatePolicy`.

Control:

- staged rollout;
- rollback;
- critical revocation;
- compatibility check;
- user consent;
- offline grace period.

Device fleets need model update discipline.

### W13567 Device Fleet Health Monitor

Invention: `DeviceFleetHealthMonitor`.

Monitor:

- crash rate;
- latency;
- battery impact;
- thermal throttling;
- artifact version;
- policy version;
- offline duration.

This makes edge AI manageable at enterprise scale.

### W13568 Embedded Safety Envelope

Invention: `EmbeddedSafetyEnvelope`.

For constrained devices:

- hard output bounds;
- local refusal;
- offline fallback;
- watchdog;
- memory guard;
- version guard.

This supports field devices, kiosks, wearables, and clinical tools.

### W13569 DEVICE-K Metric

Invention: `DEVICE-K`.

Measure any-device maturity:

- target coverage;
- install success;
- latency;
- energy;
- thermal stability;
- offline reliability;
- telemetry minimization;
- rollback readiness.

### W13570 Device Simulation Harness

Invention: `DeviceHarness`.

Simulate:

- low memory phone;
- browser without WebNN;
- NPU driver mismatch;
- offline enterprise laptop;
- thermal throttling;
- stale artifact revocation.

Expected:

- package selects correct runtime or refuses with clear reason.

### W13571 Device Build Sequence

Invention: `DeviceBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Device capability probe.
2. Edge package compiler.
3. Browser runner contract.
4. Offline sync protocol.
5. Thermal and battery governors.
6. Fleet health monitor.
7. Embedded safety envelope.
8. DEVICE-K.

### W13572 Device Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- ExecuTorch: https://docs.pytorch.org/executorch/stable/index.html
- LiteRT: https://ai.google.dev/edge/litert/overview
- WebNN specification: https://www.w3.org/TR/webnn/
- MLC LLM: https://llm.mlc.ai/docs/get_started/introduction.html
- ONNX Runtime GenAI: https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/genai/

Internal evidence:

- device probes;
- package tests;
- offline sync fixtures;
- thermal reports;
- DEVICE-K reports.

### W13573 Device Refusal Policy

Invention: `DeviceRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse device deployment when:

- runtime unsupported;
- memory insufficient;
- safety policy cannot run locally;
- rollback unavailable;
- telemetry privacy cannot be preserved;
- thermal risk too high.

### W13574 Any-Device Artifact Passport

Invention: `AnyDeviceArtifactPassport`.

Every device-ready artifact carries:

- target list;
- runtime list;
- package hash;
- device tests;
- performance profile;
- energy profile;
- offline policy;
- update policy.

## Energy, Carbon, Cloud Economics, Capacity Planning, And Runtime Business Intelligence Layer

The product has to prove cost and operational value. A 100x AI company should not only compile models; it should compile economics, carbon, capacity, and ROI into decision evidence.

### W13575 Energy Evidence Graph

Invention: `EnergyEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- device;
- runtime;
- model;
- precision;
- tokens;
- energy estimate;
- measured energy;
- carbon estimate;
- cost;
- savings.

Sources:
