- TensorRT-LLM: https://docs.nvidia.com/tensorrt-llm/
- vLLM docs: https://docs.vllm.ai/
- ExecuTorch: https://docs.pytorch.org/executorch/stable/index.html

### W13576 Energy-Per-Token Profiler

Invention: `EnergyPerTokenProfiler`.

Measure:

- prefill joules;
- decode joules;
- retrieval joules;
- tool call joules;
- proof joules;
- device thermal cost.

This turns energy from a slide into a runtime metric.

### W13577 Carbon-Aware Routing

Invention: `CarbonAwareRouting`.

Route by:

- quality;
- latency;
- cost;
- region;
- grid carbon intensity;
- data residency;
- customer preference.

Do not let carbon routing violate privacy or quality policy.

### W13578 Capacity Planning Compiler

Invention: `CapacityPlanningCompiler`.

Given traffic, compile:

- required GPUs;
- required CPUs;
- memory;
- regions;
- batch policy;
- fallback policy;
- cost curve.

This becomes a sales and infrastructure planning tool.

### W13579 Savings Attribution Engine

Invention: `SavingsAttributionEngine`.

Attribute savings to:

- distillation;
- quantization;
- routing;
- caching;
- batching;
- provider mix;
- prompt compression;
- device execution.

This makes ROI credible.

### W13580 Procurement Mode Optimizer

Invention: `ProcurementModeOptimizer`.

Recommend:

- API pass-through;
- managed Kolm;
- BYOC;
- reserved GPU;
- edge deployment;
- hybrid.

Inputs:

- spend;
- compliance;
- latency;
- engineering capacity;
- predictability.

### W13581 GPU Reservation Planner

Invention: `GPUReservationPlanner`.

Plan:

- spot;
- reserved;
- on-demand;
- customer cloud;
- device fleet.

Tie reservation strategy to artifact roadmap and traffic forecast.

### W13582 Unit Economics Passport

Invention: `UnitEconomicsPassport`.

For every artifact:

- cost per 1k requests;
- cost per successful task;
- latency p50/p95;
- energy per task;
- gross margin impact;
- fallback cost;
- customer savings.

This powers pricing and account UI truth.

### W13583 Margin Guard

Invention: `MarginGuard`.

Detect:

- customer route losing money;
- proof jobs too expensive;
- teacher fallback spike;
- cache disabled;
- bad provider price;
- long-context abuse.

Actions:

- optimize;
- alert;
- change plan;
- require approval.

### W13584 SLA Economics Compiler

Invention: `SLAEconomicsCompiler`.

Compile SLA terms into:

- capacity reserve;
- fallback requirement;
- monitoring;
- penalty estimate;
- plan price floor.

This prevents selling enterprise SLAs below cost.

### W13585 Runtime Price Simulator

Invention: `RuntimePriceSimulator`.

Simulate pricing:

- per token;
- per artifact;
- per savings share;
- per seat;
- enterprise committed spend;
- compute credits.

Output:

- margin;
- adoption friction;
- customer ROI;
- risk.

### W13586 Carbon And Energy Audit Export

Invention: `EnergyAuditExport`.

Export:

- artifact energy profile;
- region;
- runtime;
- workload;
- carbon estimate;
- savings from optimization;
- uncertainty.

This helps enterprise sustainability reporting.

### W13587 Capacity Incident Simulator

Invention: `CapacityIncidentSimulator`.

Simulate:

- GPU shortage;
- provider outage;
- traffic spike;
- region failure;
- proof backlog;
- cache invalidation.

Expected:

- system preserves critical workloads and downgrades gracefully.

### W13588 ROI Claim Guard

Invention: `ROIClaimGuard`.

Block ROI claims unless:

- baseline exists;
- attribution method exists;
- confidence interval exists;
- customer scope exists;
- time window exists.

This prevents marketing from overclaiming savings.

### W13589 ECONOMICS-K Metric

Invention: `ECONOMICS-K`.

Measure runtime business quality:

- cost attribution completeness;
- margin safety;
- savings proof;
- capacity readiness;
- SLA feasibility;
- energy reporting;
- customer ROI confidence.

### W13590 Economics Simulation Harness

Invention: `EconomicsHarness`.

Simulate:

- enterprise spend migration;
- model fallback spike;
- quantization rollout;
- GPU reservation;
- device offload;
- carbon-aware routing.

Expected:

- decision engine picks the profitable and customer-beneficial path.

### W13591 Economics Build Sequence

Invention: `EconomicsBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Unit economics passport.
2. Savings attribution engine.
3. Inference cost attribution integration.
4. Capacity planning compiler.
5. Margin guard.
6. Procurement mode optimizer.
7. Energy-per-token profiler.
8. ECONOMICS-K.

### W13592 Economics Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- TensorRT-LLM: https://docs.nvidia.com/tensorrt-llm/
- vLLM docs: https://docs.vllm.ai/
- ExecuTorch: https://docs.pytorch.org/executorch/stable/index.html
- WebNN: https://www.w3.org/TR/webnn/

Internal evidence:

- unit economics passports;
- energy profiles;
- ROI attribution reports;
- capacity simulations;
- ECONOMICS-K reports.

### W13593 Hardware-Runtime Cross-Metric Stack

Invention: `HardwareRuntimeMetricStack`.

Tie together:

- QUANT-K;
- COMPILER-K;
- SERVE-K;
- DEVICE-K;
- ECONOMICS-K;
- K-Score;
- PRIV-K;
- SEC-K.

The artifact is only truly production-ready when quality, privacy, security, portability, latency, cost, energy, and margin all fit the target scope.

### W13594 Hardware-Runtime Ultimate Design

Invention: `KolmRuntimeOptimizer`.

Ultimate design:

1. Capture task traces.
2. Distill student candidates.
3. Search quantization recipes.
4. Lower to portable IR.
5. Compile target packages.
6. Simulate memory and serving economics.
7. Probe devices.
8. Run slice and safety evals.
9. Generate runtime passport.
10. Deploy with telemetry and rollback.

This is the backend engine that makes the site claim concrete: Kolm turns AI behavior into owned artifacts that run on the right hardware at the right cost with evidence.

## Data-Centric Distillation, Weak Supervision, Data Valuation, Label Quality, And Active Dataset Engineering Layer

The deepest model moat is not only architecture or runtime. It is knowing which data changed which capability, which examples are poisonous, which labels are weak but useful, which traces should be bought, which should be deleted, and which should never enter a student. Kolm should become a data-centric AI compiler for distillation.

### W13595 Data-Centric Evidence Graph

Invention: `DataCentricEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- example;
- source;
- label;
- weak label function;
- human label;
- teacher label;
- confidence;
- data value;
- quality issue;
- artifact effect;
- consent scope.

Primary sources:

- Snorkel weak supervision: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7075849/
- Cleanlab: https://github.com/cleanlab/cleanlab
- Data Shapley: https://arxiv.org/abs/1904.02868
- Active learning survey: https://arxiv.org/abs/1708.02383

### W13596 Weak Supervision Compiler

Invention: `WeakSupervisionCompiler`.

Compile organizational knowledge into labeling functions:

- regex rules;
- ontology matches;
- heuristic functions;
- teacher-model votes;
- policy rules;
- SME decision trees;
- historical workflow outcomes.

The compiler estimates conflicts, coverage, accuracy, and abstention before labels train a student.

### W13597 Label Function Marketplace

Invention: `LabelFunctionMarketplace`.

Reusable label functions should become artifacts:

- domain;
- task;
- coverage;
- conflict rate;
- validation score;
- license;
- privacy scope.

This lets enterprises reuse expertise without sharing raw data.

### W13598 Label Quality Auditor

Invention: `LabelQualityAuditor`.

Detect:

- mislabeled examples;
- ambiguous examples;
- duplicate conflicts;
- class imbalance;
- out-of-distribution examples;
- annotator drift;
- teacher disagreement.

Output:

- keep;
- relabel;
- quarantine;
- synthesize counterexample;
- escalate to expert.

### W13599 Distillation Data Shapley

Invention: `DistillationDataShapley`.

Estimate each trace's contribution to:

- K-Score;
- rare-class recall;
- safety quality;
- latency improvement;
- quantization robustness;
- calibration.

Do not value examples only by accuracy. Value them by product metric movement.

### W13600 Gradient-Similarity Data Valuation

Invention: `GradientSimilarityValuator`.

Use gradient similarity to identify:

- helpful examples;
- harmful examples;
- redundant examples;
- rare but valuable examples;
- privacy-expensive examples.

This supports faster valuation than repeated retraining.

### W13601 Example Marginal ROI Ledger

Invention: `ExampleROILedger`.

For each labeled or captured example, record:

- acquisition cost;
- privacy cost;
- label cost;
- training value;
- eval value;
- future reuse value.

The product can then recommend what data to collect next.

### W13602 Active Capture Prioritizer

Invention: `ActiveCapturePrioritizer`.

Tell customers which production traffic to capture more of:

- uncertain segment;
- high-cost route;
- rare failure;
- low-confidence student decision;
- high-business-value task;
- underrepresented language.

This turns capture from passive logging into targeted data acquisition.

### W13603 Label Budget Optimizer

Invention: `LabelBudgetOptimizer`.

Allocate human labeling budget by:

- expected K-Score gain;
- uncertainty;
- rarity;
- legal sensitivity;
- downstream revenue;
- proof requirement.

This helps customers spend less while training better students.

### W13604 Data Deduplication Compiler

Invention: `SemanticDedupCompiler`.

Deduplicate by:

- exact hash;
- near text;
- semantic embedding;
- workflow id;
- answer equivalence;
- policy equivalence.

Deduplication must preserve rare but meaningful variants, not blindly remove them.

### W13605 Hard Negative Miner

Invention: `HardNegativeMiner`.

Mine hard negatives:

- similar input, different label;
- same entity, different policy;
- same format, different class;
- adversarial citation;
- near-duplicate with important difference.

These examples improve student decision boundaries.

### W13606 Data Slicing Compiler

Invention: `DataSlicingCompiler`.

Automatically create slices:

- domain;
- language;
- customer segment;
- input length;
- policy class;
- safety class;
- tool class;
- device target.

Every K-Score should report slices, not only aggregates.

### W13607 Dataset Influence Debugger

Invention: `DatasetInfluenceDebugger`.

When artifact behavior changes, show:

- which examples caused it;
- which labels caused it;
- which weak rules caused it;
- which examples would reverse it;
- which slices were harmed.

This makes data debugging operational.

### W13608 Label Conflict Resolver

Invention: `LabelConflictResolver`.

Resolve disagreements between:

- human;
- teacher;
- weak label;
- policy rule;
- previous model;
- customer override.

Output:

- accepted label;
- uncertainty;
- owner;
- required review;
- audit record.

### W13609 Dataset Risk Passport

Invention: `DatasetRiskPassport`.

Every dataset carries:

- consent;
- license;
- privacy class;
- quality score;
- leakage risk;
- bias risk;
- contamination risk;
- valuation summary.

No dataset becomes training data without a passport.

### W13610 Data-Centric Repair Loop

Invention: `DataCentricRepairLoop`.

When eval fails:

1. Find failure slice.
2. Identify missing or harmful examples.
3. Generate labeling tasks.
4. Add weak supervision or human labels.
5. Revalue data.
6. Retrain or repair.

This makes improvement systematic.

### W13611 DATA-K Metric

Invention: `DATA-K`.

Measure training data readiness:

- label quality;
- slice coverage;
- valuation confidence;
- duplication control;
- conflict closure;
- risk passport completeness;
- active learning efficiency.

### W13612 Data-Centric Simulation Harness

Invention: `DataCentricHarness`.

Simulate:

- noisy labels;
- weak label conflict;
- rare class undercoverage;
- duplicate-heavy capture;
- privacy-expensive example;
- harmful example.

Expected:

- optimizer selects relabel, drop, keep, synthesize, or collect more data.

### W13613 Data-Centric Build Sequence

Invention: `DataCentricBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Dataset risk passport.
2. Label quality auditor.
3. Data slicing compiler.
4. Active capture prioritizer.
5. Distillation data valuation.
6. Label conflict resolver.
7. Data-centric repair loop.
8. DATA-K.

### W13614 Data-Centric Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- Snorkel: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7075849/
- Cleanlab: https://github.com/cleanlab/cleanlab
- Data Shapley: https://arxiv.org/abs/1904.02868
- Active learning survey: https://arxiv.org/abs/1708.02383

Internal evidence:

- label quality reports;
- weak supervision functions;
- data valuation reports;
- active capture plans;
- DATA-K simulations.

## Lakehouse Lineage, Data Contracts, Feature Stores, Table Formats, And Training Data Supply Chain Layer

Kolm artifacts depend on data pipelines. If the upstream table, schema, lineage, or feature definition changes, the model claim changes. This layer turns the modern data stack into artifact evidence.

### W13615 Training Data Supply Chain Graph

Invention: `TrainingDataSupplyChainGraph`.

Track:

- raw object;
- table;
- snapshot;
- transform job;
- feature;
- training split;
- eval split;
- artifact;
- route policy.

Primary sources:

- OpenLineage: https://openlineage.io/
- Feast: https://feast.dev/
- Apache Iceberg spec: https://iceberg.apache.org/spec/
- Delta Lake docs: https://docs.delta.io/
- lakeFS docs: https://docs.lakefs.io/

### W13616 OpenLineage Adapter For AI Training

Invention: `KolmOpenLineageAdapter`.

Emit lineage for:

- capture ingestion;
- redaction;
- labeling;
- feature generation;
- distillation;
- eval;
- artifact packaging.

This lets existing data teams see AI artifact lineage in their standard tools.

### W13617 Data Contract Compiler

Invention: `TrainingDataContractCompiler`.

Compile contracts for:

- schema;
- freshness;
- volume;
- null rate;
- class distribution;
- privacy class;
- allowed use;
- lineage completeness.

If a contract fails, artifact training or promotion should block.

### W13618 Point-In-Time Correct Feature Builder

Invention: `PointInTimeFeatureBuilder`.

Training features must be point-in-time correct:

- no future leakage;
- same feature definition at train and serve;
- timestamp join checks;
- late-arriving event handling.

This is essential for enterprise workflows with time-dependent decisions.

### W13619 Feature Store Context Bridge

Invention: `FeatureStoreContextBridge`.

Use feature stores as governed context for AI:

- customer risk features;
- product metadata;
- account state;
- fraud signals;
- workflow state.

Bind feature versions into artifact manifests so context drift is visible.

### W13620 Table Snapshot Artifact Binding

Invention: `TableSnapshotBinding`.

Bind artifacts to:

- Iceberg snapshot;
- Delta version;
- lakeFS commit;
- object store manifest;
- data contract version.

This makes training data reproducible.

### W13621 Schema Evolution Guard

Invention: `SchemaEvolutionGuard`.

Detect schema changes that affect:

- input parser;
- label mapping;
- feature semantics;
- eval fixture;
- privacy classifier;
- route policy.

Output:

- safe evolution;
- retrain required;
- eval required;
- block.

### W13622 Data Freshness SLO Compiler

Invention: `DataFreshnessSLOCompiler`.

Compile freshness requirements:

- feature freshness;
- benchmark freshness;
- teacher output freshness;
- policy freshness;
- consent freshness.

Stale data should downgrade claims.

### W13623 Training Split Lineage

Invention: `TrainingSplitLineage`.

Record exact split membership:

- train;
- validation;
- test;
- private holdout;
- public benchmark;
- customer holdout.

Prevent contamination and make unlearning possible.

### W13624 Transform Reproducibility Checker

Invention: `TransformReproChecker`.

Check whether a data transform can reproduce:

- same rows;
- same order where needed;
- same schema;
- same labels;
- same redactions;
- same derived features.

If not, the artifact evidence is weak.

### W13625 Data Pipeline Drift Monitor

Invention: `PipelineDriftMonitor`.

Detect drift in:

- source table;
- transform output;
- label distribution;
- feature distribution;
- redaction rate;
- missingness.

This catches upstream changes before model quality breaks.

### W13626 Data Quality Gate For Distillation

Invention: `DistillationDataQualityGate`.

Before training:

- validate schema;
- validate freshness;
- validate labels;
- validate privacy;
- validate splits;
- validate lineage.

Fail closed for high-risk artifacts.

### W13627 Feature Provenance Passport

Invention: `FeatureProvenancePassport`.

Every feature should state:

- definition;
- owner;
- source;
- freshness;
- online/offline parity;
- privacy class;
- known caveats.

This prevents feature misuse in AI routes.

### W13628 Lakehouse Artifact Registry

Invention: `LakehouseArtifactRegistry`.

Register `.kolm` artifacts next to table snapshots:

- artifact id;
- table versions;
- feature views;
- lineage events;
- eval reports;
- deployment status.

This makes Kolm legible inside enterprise data platforms.

### W13629 Data Impact Analysis Engine

Invention: `DataImpactAnalysisEngine`.

When a table or feature changes, answer:

- which artifacts are affected?;
- which evals are stale?;
- which customers are impacted?;
- which proofs are stale?;
- which routes should block?

### W13630 Training Data Time Travel

Invention: `TrainingDataTimeTravel`.

Recreate historical training data using:

- table snapshots;
- lineage events;
- transform versions;
- label versions;
- consent versions.

This is required for audits and incident review.

### W13631 PIPELINE-K Metric

Invention: `PIPELINE-K`.

Measure data supply chain maturity:

- lineage completeness;
- contract pass rate;
- snapshot binding coverage;
- feature parity;
- drift monitoring;
- reproducibility.

### W13632 Pipeline Simulation Harness

Invention: `PipelineHarness`.

Simulate:

- schema change;
- late events;
- feature skew;
- label transform bug;
- table rollback;
- lineage gap.

Expected:

- affected artifacts are identified and claims downgrade.

### W13633 Pipeline Build Sequence

Invention: `PipelineBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Table snapshot binding.
2. Training split lineage.
3. Data contract compiler.
4. OpenLineage adapter.
5. Feature provenance passport.
6. Pipeline drift monitor.
7. Data impact analysis.
8. PIPELINE-K.

### W13634 Pipeline Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- OpenLineage: https://openlineage.io/
- Feast: https://feast.dev/
- Apache Iceberg: https://iceberg.apache.org/spec/
- Delta Lake: https://docs.delta.io/
- lakeFS: https://docs.lakefs.io/

Internal evidence:

- lineage events;
- data contracts;
- snapshot bindings;
- feature passports;
- PIPELINE-K reports.

## Ontology, Knowledge Graph, Semantic Layer, Entity Resolution, And GraphRAG Grounding Layer

Kolm should not reduce enterprise knowledge to chunks. Many customer workflows are entity, relationship, rule, and ontology problems. A strong product should compile enterprise semantics into artifacts.

### W13635 Semantic Evidence Graph

Invention: `SemanticEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- entity;
- relationship;
- ontology class;
- document chunk;
- table row;
- policy rule;
- citation;
- retrieval path;
- answer claim.

Primary sources:

- W3C OWL: https://www.w3.org/OWL
- W3C RDF-star and SPARQL-star: https://www.w3.org/2021/12/rdf-star.html
- Neo4j GraphRAG: https://neo4j.com/labs/genai-ecosystem/graphrag/
- LlamaIndex Knowledge Graph RAG: https://docs.llamaindex.ai/en/stable/examples/query_engine/knowledge_graph_rag_query_engine/

### W13636 Ontology Compiler

Invention: `KolmOntologyCompiler`.

Compile customer domain concepts:

- classes;
- relations;
- constraints;
- synonyms;
- allowed values;
- hierarchy;
- provenance.

Use the ontology to guide:

- extraction;
- retrieval;
- validation;
- distillation;
- evaluation.

### W13637 Entity Resolution Engine

Invention: `EntityResolutionEngine`.

Resolve:

- duplicate customers;
- providers;
- claims;
- products;
- documents;
- devices;
- accounts.

Use deterministic keys, probabilistic matching, embeddings, and human review where needed.

### W13638 Schema Matching Compiler

Invention: `SchemaMatchingCompiler`.

Map customer schemas to Kolm concepts:

- field name;
- type;
- examples;
- ontology relation;
- privacy class;
- feature semantics.

This reduces enterprise onboarding friction.

### W13639 GraphRAG Path Verifier

Invention: `GraphRAGPathVerifier`.

Every graph-grounded answer should cite:

- nodes;
- edges;
- documents;
- confidence;
- freshness;
- policy scope.

If no valid path supports the answer, the artifact must refuse or downgrade.

### W13640 Knowledge Graph Builder With Weak Nodes

Invention: `WeakNodeKnowledgeGraphBuilder`.

Build graphs with:

- deterministic strong nodes;
- LLM-extracted weak nodes;
- confidence;
- provenance;
- validation status.

Weak nodes cannot support strong claims until verified.

### W13641 Semantic Retrieval Router

Invention: `SemanticRetrievalRouter`.

Choose retrieval mode:

- vector;
- keyword;
- graph path;
- SQL;
- ontology rule;
- hybrid.

Decision depends on query type and required proof.

### W13642 Ontology-Aware Distillation

Invention: `OntologyAwareDistillation`.

Teach students:

- entity types;
- relation constraints;
- synonym resolution;
- hierarchy;
- forbidden relations;
- citation patterns.

This improves extraction and retrieval tasks without needing massive models.

### W13643 Semantic Consistency Checker

Invention: `SemanticConsistencyChecker`.

Detect:

- entity contradiction;
- impossible relation;
- stale fact;
- ontology violation;
- citation mismatch;
- unit mismatch.

Use for output validation and eval scoring.

### W13644 Knowledge Graph Drift Monitor

Invention: `KnowledgeGraphDriftMonitor`.

Detect:

- entity distribution change;
- relationship change;
- ontology change;
- source freshness drift;
- weak-node error rate.

When graph drift occurs, RAG and students may need refresh.

### W13645 Fact Lineage Passport

Invention: `FactLineagePassport`.

Every important fact has:

- source;
- extraction method;
- graph path;
- confidence;
- validation;
- timestamp;
- license;
- usage scope.

This makes grounded answers inspectable.

### W13646 Semantic Cache

Invention: `SemanticCache`.

Cache by:

- entity;
- relation;
- graph path;
- query intent;
- policy scope.

This is safer than raw prompt cache for enterprise knowledge.

### W13647 Ontology Marketplace

Invention: `OntologyMarketplace`.

Share domain ontologies:

- healthcare claims;
- finance risk;
- support tickets;
- procurement;
- legal contracts;
- device telemetry.

Each ontology carries license, version, tests, and mapping templates.

### W13648 Graph-Based Benchmark Generator

Invention: `GraphBenchmarkGenerator`.

Generate evals from graphs:

- single-hop;
- multi-hop;
- contradiction;
- stale fact;
- missing edge;
- access-controlled fact.

This creates grounded RAG benchmarks from customer knowledge.

### W13649 Semantic Access Control

Invention: `SemanticAccessControl`.

Access control should operate on:

- entity class;
- relation;
- document source;
- tenant;
- sensitivity;
- purpose.

Not all facts from an accessible document are equally accessible.

### W13650 SEMANTIC-K Metric

Invention: `SEMANTIC-K`.

Measure semantic grounding:

- ontology coverage;
- entity resolution quality;
- graph path support;
- contradiction rate;
- freshness;
- access-control correctness;
- citation completeness.

### W13651 Semantic Simulation Harness

Invention: `SemanticHarness`.

Simulate:

- duplicate entities;
- wrong relation;
- stale graph fact;
- weak node hallucination;
- inaccessible fact;
- multi-hop query.

Expected:

- answer grounds, refuses, or asks for verification.

### W13652 Semantic Build Sequence

Invention: `SemanticBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Ontology compiler.
2. Entity resolution engine.
3. Fact lineage passport.
4. GraphRAG path verifier.
5. Semantic consistency checker.
6. Semantic retrieval router.
7. Graph benchmark generator.
8. SEMANTIC-K.

### W13653 Semantic Refusal Policy

Invention: `SemanticRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse or downgrade when:

- no graph path;
- stale fact;
- weak node only;
- access control conflict;
- ontology contradiction;
- entity unresolved.

### W13654 Semantic Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- W3C OWL: https://www.w3.org/OWL
- W3C RDF-star and SPARQL-star: https://www.w3.org/2021/12/rdf-star.html
- Neo4j GraphRAG: https://neo4j.com/labs/genai-ecosystem/graphrag/
- LlamaIndex Knowledge Graph RAG: https://docs.llamaindex.ai/en/stable/examples/query_engine/knowledge_graph_rag_query_engine/

Internal evidence:

- ontology fixtures;
- entity resolution reports;
- graph path proofs;
- semantic evals;
- SEMANTIC-K reports.

## Process Mining, Workflow Discovery, Event Logs, Operational Twin, And Business Process Distillation Layer

Enterprise AI is often workflow automation. Kolm should mine real workflows, discover the process model, identify where AI helps, and compile workflow artifacts with evidence.

### W13655 Process Evidence Graph

Invention: `ProcessEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- event;
- object;
- case;
- actor;
- activity;
- timestamp;
- system;
- policy;
- AI decision;
- outcome.

Primary sources:

- IEEE Process Mining Manifesto: https://www.tf-pm.org/resources/manifesto
- XES standard overview: https://www.tf-pm.org/resources/xes-standard/about-xes
- OCEL 2.0: https://www.ocel-standard.org/

### W13656 Event Log Compiler

Invention: `KolmEventLogCompiler`.

Compile application events into:

- XES-like case logs;
- OCEL object-centric logs;
- AI call logs;
- tool logs;
- approval logs.

This gives Kolm the raw material to understand customer workflows.

### W13657 Object-Centric Workflow Miner

Invention: `ObjectCentricWorkflowMiner`.

Discover workflows involving multiple objects:

- customer;
- ticket;
- claim;
- invoice;
- device;
- artifact;
- approval.

Object-centric mining is better than forcing one case id onto complex enterprise work.

### W13658 AI Opportunity Miner

Invention: `AIOpportunityMiner`.

Find workflow steps where AI can:

- classify;
- extract;
- summarize;
- route;
- validate;
- approve with human review;
- detect anomaly.

Rank by ROI, risk, data availability, and proofability.

### W13659 Process Conformance Checker

Invention: `AIProcessConformanceChecker`.

Compare observed AI workflow with approved process:

- missing approval;
- skipped validation;
- wrong route;
- delayed step;
- unauthorized tool;
- policy bypass.

This becomes governance evidence.

### W13660 Workflow Bottleneck Detector

Invention: `WorkflowBottleneckDetector`.

Detect:

- long wait states;
- repeated rework;
- human review overload;
- failed automation;
- handoff delay;
- tool error loop.

Then recommend AI or process changes.

### W13661 Process-Aware Distillation

Invention: `ProcessAwareDistillation`.

Distill behavior using process context:

- step;
- previous event;
- next expected event;
- SLA;
- actor role;
- object state.

This improves models that need workflow state to answer correctly.

### W13662 Operational Digital Twin

Invention: `OperationalDigitalTwin`.

Simulate:

- current workflow;
- AI-inserted workflow;
- human review load;
- SLA impact;
- cost impact;
- failure impact.

This predicts whether automation actually helps.

### W13663 Workflow Policy Compiler

Invention: `WorkflowPolicyCompiler`.

Compile process rules:

- approval before deploy;
- human review above risk;
- dual control for finance;
- escalation after SLA breach;
- no AI decision without evidence.

Bind these into agent plans and route policies.

### W13664 Process Variant Clustering

Invention: `ProcessVariantClusterer`.

Cluster real workflow variants:

- standard;
- exception;
- high-value;
- risky;
- manual workaround;
- broken automation.

Each variant can get a different artifact or route policy.

### W13665 Process Outcome Attribution

Invention: `ProcessOutcomeAttributor`.

Attribute outcomes to:

- AI step;
- human step;
- data quality;
- policy delay;
- tool error;
- external dependency.

This prevents blaming or crediting the model incorrectly.

### W13666 Workflow Capture Filter

Invention: `WorkflowCaptureFilter`.

Capture workflow events that matter:

- decision points;
- exceptions;
- handoffs;
- errors;
- approvals;
- customer outcomes.

Avoid capturing irrelevant noise and sensitive data unnecessarily.

### W13667 Process Mining For Active Learning

Invention: `ProcessActiveLearning`.

Ask humans to label:

- bottleneck decisions;
- exception routes;
- disputed approvals;
- high-rework steps;
- bad outcomes.

This makes labeling business-process-aware.

### W13668 Workflow Artifact Compiler

Invention: `WorkflowArtifactCompiler`.

Compile:

- process model;
- AI step artifacts;
- policies;
- monitors;
- dashboards;
- rollback plan.

The output is an operational AI workflow, not a standalone model.

### W13669 Process Compliance Packet

Invention: `ProcessCompliancePacket`.

For regulated workflows, produce:

- event log;
- process model;
- conformance report;
- AI decision evidence;
- approval trail;
- exception list.

This helps audits and enterprise buyers.

### W13670 PROCESS-K Metric

Invention: `PROCESS-K`.

Measure workflow automation quality:

- conformance;
- bottleneck reduction;
- SLA improvement;
- rework reduction;
- AI step quality;
- audit completeness;
- human load balance.

### W13671 Process Simulation Harness

Invention: `ProcessHarness`.

Simulate:

- support triage;
- claims processing;
- artifact approval;
- incident response;
- procurement review;
- healthcare prior authorization.

Expected:

- miner finds AI opportunities and blocks unsafe automation.

### W13672 Process Build Sequence

Invention: `ProcessBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Event log compiler.
2. Object-centric workflow miner.
3. Conformance checker.
4. AI opportunity miner.
5. Process-aware distillation.
6. Operational digital twin.
7. Workflow artifact compiler.
8. PROCESS-K.

### W13673 Process Refusal Policy

Invention: `ProcessRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse automation when:

- event log incomplete;
- approval policy unknown;
- outcome metric absent;
- conformance fails;
- human override unavailable;
- high-risk process lacks audit.

### W13674 Process Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- Process Mining Manifesto: https://www.tf-pm.org/resources/manifesto
- XES overview: https://www.tf-pm.org/resources/xes-standard/about-xes
- OCEL 2.0: https://www.ocel-standard.org/
- OCEL specification: https://www.ocel-standard.org/specification/overview

Internal evidence:

- event logs;
- workflow models;
- conformance reports;
- operational twin simulations;
- PROCESS-K reports.

## Synthetic Data Quality, Simulation Data, Scenario Generation, Privacy Firewalls, And Evaluation Augmentation Layer

Synthetic data can be a breakthrough or a liability. Kolm should generate synthetic examples only when they improve measured capability, preserve privacy, avoid contamination, and carry provenance.

### W13675 Synthetic Data Evidence Graph

Invention: `SyntheticDataEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- generator;
- source inspiration;
- privacy firewall;
- scenario;
- label;
- validation;
- human review;
- artifact effect;
- benchmark scope.

Primary sources:

- Gretel synthetic data resources: https://gretel.ai/
- Tonic synthetic data resources: https://www.tonic.ai/
- Mostly AI synthetic data resources: https://mostly.ai/
- Synthetic data privacy concerns should also be tested against OpenDP: https://docs.opendp.org/en/stable/

### W13676 Scenario Generator

Invention: `ScenarioGenerator`.

Generate scenario families:

- rare class;
- edge case;
- policy conflict;
- ambiguous input;
- adversarial input;
- long-context case;
- cross-lingual case;
- device-specific case.

Each scenario must state the capability it tests.

### W13677 Synthetic Label Verifier

Invention: `SyntheticLabelVerifier`.

Verify generated labels using:

- deterministic rules;
- ontology checks;
- teacher agreement;
- human spot checks;
- counterfactual consistency;
- schema validation.

Synthetic labels without verification should not train critical artifacts.

### W13678 Synthetic Privacy Firewall

Invention: `SyntheticPrivacyFirewallV2`.

Block synthetic examples that:

- are too close to real examples;
- contain rare strings;
- leak identities;
- reproduce private workflows;
- violate consent;
- include customer-specific secrets.

This extends the earlier privacy firewall into a full generation gate.

### W13679 Synthetic Diversity Optimizer

Invention: `SyntheticDiversityOptimizer`.

Optimize diversity by:

- semantic coverage;
- ontology coverage;
- process variant;
- language;
- input length;
- error type;
- device context.

Avoid generating many superficially different but behaviorally identical examples.

### W13680 Rare-Case Amplifier

Invention: `RareCaseAmplifier`.

Generate examples for:

- rare PHI class;
- low-frequency fraud pattern;
- unusual support escalation;
- uncommon language;
- edge device failure;
- policy exception.

The system must measure whether rare-case performance improves without harming common cases.

### W13681 Synthetic Benchmark Split Guard

Invention: `SyntheticBenchmarkSplitGuard`.

Prevent train/eval contamination:

- generator seed separation;
- scenario family separation;
- source inspiration separation;
- template separation;
- semantic near-duplicate checks.

Synthetic benchmarks are useless if training saw the same template.

### W13682 Simulation-To-Real Data Gap

Invention: `SyntheticSim2RealGap`.

Measure:

- distribution gap;
- error gap;
- language gap;
- workflow gap;
- policy gap;
- model overfit to synthetic style.

Use real holdouts to calibrate synthetic value.

### W13683 Synthetic Data Valuation

Invention: `SyntheticDataValuator`.

Estimate each synthetic example's value:

- improves K-Score;
- improves rare slice;
- improves safety;
- worsens calibration;
- causes overfit;
- duplicates real data.

This prevents synthetic data floods.

### W13684 Controlled Counterfactual Augmentation

Invention: `ControlledCounterfactualAugmenter`.

Generate examples where exactly one factor changes:

- policy;
- entity;
- date;
- amount;
- language;
- protected attribute;
- tool output.

Use for causality, fairness, and robustness.

### W13685 Synthetic Adversarial Curriculum

Invention: `SyntheticAdversarialCurriculum`.

Generate adversarial examples in stages:

1. clean edge case;
2. distractor;
3. prompt injection;
4. contradiction;
5. policy conflict;
6. multi-turn attack.

Train and evaluate progressively.

### W13686 Synthetic Data License Passport

Invention: `SyntheticDataLicensePassport`.

Record:

- generator;
- source rights;
- model terms;
- allowed use;
- redistribution rights;
- privacy checks;
- attribution.

Synthetic does not automatically mean rights-free.

### W13687 Human-In-The-Loop Synthetic Review

Invention: `SyntheticReviewQueue`.

Queue only high-value synthetic examples for human review:

- rare class;
- high risk;
- label uncertain;
- policy-critical;
- benchmark candidate.

This keeps review cost efficient.

### W13688 Synthetic-To-Weak Supervision Loop

Invention: `SyntheticWeakSupervisionLoop`.

Use synthetic examples to test and improve labeling functions:

- generate boundary cases;
- run labeling functions;
- detect conflict;
- refine function;
- validate on real holdout.

This improves weak supervision quality.

### W13689 Synthetic Artifact Stress Pack

Invention: `SyntheticStressPack`.

Every artifact gets stress examples:

- privacy;
- security;
- semantics;
- workflow;
- runtime;
- device;
- economics.

This pack is distinct from clean benchmark data.

### W13690 SYNTHDATA-K Metric

Invention: `SYNTHDATA-K`.

Measure synthetic data quality:

- privacy safety;
- diversity;
- label validity;
- rare-case lift;
- contamination control;
- real-world transfer;
- rights clarity.

### W13691 Synthetic Data Simulation Harness

Invention: `SyntheticDataHarness`.

Simulate:

- generator copies private example;
- synthetic overfit;
- rare-case improvement;
- benchmark contamination;
- label error;
- rights conflict.

Expected:

- system blocks, downgrades, or promotes with evidence.

### W13692 Synthetic Build Sequence

Invention: `SyntheticBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Synthetic data evidence graph.
2. Privacy firewall.
3. Scenario generator.
4. Label verifier.
5. Split guard.
6. Synthetic data valuation.
7. Stress pack compiler.
8. SYNTHDATA-K.

### W13693 Synthetic Refusal Policy

Invention: `SyntheticRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse synthetic data use when:

- privacy firewall fails;
- label unverifiable;
- rights unclear;
- split contamination risk high;
- real-world transfer unproven;
- synthetic examples harm calibration.

### W13694 Synthetic Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- OpenDP: https://docs.opendp.org/en/stable/
- Gretel: https://gretel.ai/
- Tonic: https://www.tonic.ai/
- Mostly AI: https://mostly.ai/
- Synthetic data risk should be connected to benchmark contamination and privacy gates described earlier in this document.

Internal evidence:

- synthetic generation manifests;
- privacy firewall results;
- label verification reports;
- split guard checks;
- SYNTHDATA-K reports.

## Enterprise Trust Operating System, Regulatory Evidence, Trust Center, Risk Management, And Procurement Layer

Kolm should not merely have compliance pages. It should compile technical evidence into buyer-ready trust packets that map to risk frameworks, regulations, contract obligations, security controls, and procurement questions. The product moat is making trust operational instead of performative.

### W13695 Enterprise Trust Evidence Graph

Invention: `EnterpriseTrustEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- control;
- evidence;
- owner;
- product surface;
- artifact;
- customer contract;
- regulation;
- audit packet;
- expiration;
- residual risk.

Primary sources:

- NIST AI RMF: https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework
- EU AI Act Explorer: https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act-explorer
- ISO/IEC 27001: https://www.iso.org/standard/27001
- AICPA Trust Services Criteria: https://us.aicpa.org/content/dam/aicpa/interestareas/frc/assuranceadvisoryservices/downloadabledocuments/trust-services-criteria-redlined.pdf

### W13696 AI Risk Control Mapper

Invention: `AIRiskControlMapper`.

Map every artifact and feature to:

- govern;
- map;
- measure;
- manage;
- security;
- privacy;
- safety;
- transparency;
- accountability.

This gives Kolm a native NIST AI RMF evidence layer rather than a PDF afterthought.

### W13697 EU AI Act Obligation Compiler

Invention: `EUAIActObligationCompiler`.

Classify customer use cases:

- prohibited risk;
- high-risk;
- transparency obligation;
- GPAI-related;
- limited risk;
- out of scope.

For each, compile required evidence:

- data governance;
- technical documentation;
- logging;
- transparency;
- human oversight;
- accuracy and robustness;
- cybersecurity.

### W13698 SOC2 Control Evidence Pack

Invention: `SOC2EvidencePack`.

Map Kolm controls to Trust Services Criteria:

- security;
- availability;
- processing integrity;
- confidentiality;
- privacy.

Each control links to:

- test;
- log;
- policy;
- owner;
- artifact surface.

This reduces sales friction because security questionnaires become generated evidence.

### W13699 ISO27001 ISMS Bridge

Invention: `ISO27001ISMSBridge`.

Connect product controls to ISMS evidence:

- risk assessment;
- asset inventory;
- access control;
- cryptography;
- supplier risk;
- incident response;
- business continuity.

Kolm artifacts become governed assets inside the ISMS.

### W13700 HIPAA And BAA Evidence Compiler

Invention: `HIPAAEvidenceCompiler`.

For healthcare customers:

- PHI data flow;
- redaction controls;
- audit logs;
- access controls;
- retention;
- breach workflow;
- BAA obligations.

Do not claim HIPAA compliance broadly. Claim specific controls and agreements.

### W13701 GDPR And DPA Evidence Compiler

Invention: `GDPREvidenceCompiler`.

Compile:

- controller/processor role;
- purpose;
- lawful basis;
- data subject rights;
- subprocessors;
- transfer mechanism;
- retention;
- deletion.

Bind to data rights and artifact lineage so privacy obligations are executable.

### W13702 Trust Center Evidence API

Invention: `TrustCenterEvidenceAPI`.

Expose:

- certification status;
- control status;
- artifact evidence;
- subprocessor list;
- security docs;
- incident status;
- open readiness gates.

Frontend should render this API, not hardcode trust claims.

### W13703 Security Questionnaire Compiler

Invention: `SecurityQuestionnaireCompiler`.

Generate answers for:

- SIG Lite;
- CAIQ;
- vendor risk forms;
- enterprise custom questionnaires;
- procurement intake.

Answers must cite evidence nodes and refuse unknown claims.

### W13704 Audit Room Generator

Invention: `AuditRoomGenerator`.

Generate customer-specific audit rooms:

- requested controls;
- artifacts;
- evidence;
- logs;
- certifications;
- DPAs;
- BAA;
- subprocessors;
- expiration.

This becomes a sales acceleration engine.

### W13705 Control Expiration Monitor

Invention: `ControlExpirationMonitor`.

Monitor:

- certificate expiration;
- policy review date;
- vendor review date;
- penetration test date;
- benchmark freshness;
- model card freshness.

Expired evidence downgrades trust claims.

### W13706 Trust Claim Refusal Engine

Invention: `TrustClaimRefusalEngine`.

Block claims like:

- SOC 2 certified;
- HIPAA compliant;
- EU AI Act ready;
- zero data retention;
- fully private;
- production proven.

Unless matching evidence scope exists.

### W13707 Enterprise Risk Register

Invention: `EnterpriseAIRiskRegister`.

Track:

- risk;
- severity;
- likelihood;
- mitigation;
- evidence;
- owner;
- accepted residual risk;
- review date.

Customers can export it into their GRC systems.

### W13708 Contract-To-Control Compiler

Invention: `ContractToControlCompiler`.

Convert customer contract terms into:

- route policies;
- storage policies;
- retention policies;
- region policies;
- audit export policies;
- SLA monitors.

This is how legal promises become runtime behavior.

### W13709 Procurement Readiness Score

Invention: `ProcurementReadinessScore`.

Score readiness for:

- security review;
- legal review;
- finance review;
- architecture review;
- compliance review;
- executive approval.

Each missing item gets a build ticket or proof request.

### W13710 Trust Evidence Diff

Invention: `TrustEvidenceDiff`.

When controls change, show:

- new evidence;
- stale evidence;
- downgraded claim;
- impacted customers;
- impacted contracts;
- required notices.

This prevents silent trust drift.

### W13711 TRUST-K Metric

Invention: `TRUST-K`.

Measure trust maturity:

- control coverage;
- evidence freshness;
- claim correctness;
- questionnaire automation;
- audit room completeness;
- residual risk closure;
- contract-to-control coverage.

### W13712 Trust Simulation Harness

Invention: `TrustHarness`.

Simulate:

- expired SOC 2 evidence;
- missing DPA;
- region commitment conflict;
- high-risk EU AI Act use case;
- security questionnaire unknown answer.

Expected:

- claims downgrade and procurement gaps become explicit.

### W13713 Trust Build Sequence

Invention: `TrustBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Enterprise trust evidence graph.
2. Trust claim refusal engine.
3. Trust Center Evidence API.
4. Security questionnaire compiler.
5. Contract-to-control compiler.
6. Audit room generator.
7. Control expiration monitor.
8. TRUST-K.

### W13714 Trust Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- NIST AI RMF: https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework
- EU AI Act Explorer: https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act-explorer

- trust graph fixtures;
- questionnaire answers;

- TRUST-K reports.

## Developer Ecosystem, Protocol Integrations, SDK Contracts, Agent Tooling, Kubernetes, And BYOC Platform Layer

Kolm becomes a category-defining product only if developers can integrate it everywhere without losing governance. The ecosystem surface must make API, SDK, CLI, MCP, Kubernetes, BYOC, and cloud-native deployment coherent.

### W13715 Ecosystem Evidence Graph

Invention: `EcosystemEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- API route;
- SDK method;
- CLI command;
- MCP tool;
- Kubernetes resource;
- Terraform module;
- integration test;
- customer workflow.

Sources:

- OpenAPI specification: https://spec.openapis.org/
- Model Context Protocol: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/latest
- Kubernetes docs: https://kubernetes.io/docs/
- CNCF: https://www.cncf.io/

### W13716 OpenAPI Contract Compiler

Invention: `OpenAPIContractCompiler`.

Every public API must generate:

- OpenAPI operation;
- SDK binding;
- CLI equivalent;
- auth scope;
- example;
- error contract;
- smoke probe.

This prevents hidden routes and stale docs.

### W13717 SDK Parity Matrix

Invention: `SDKParityMatrix`.

Track parity across:

- TypeScript;
- Python;
- Go;
- Rust;
- Swift;
- Kotlin;
- C;
- MCP;
- CLI.

No product surface is done until the intended SDK tier is explicit.

### W13718 MCP Tool Contract Compiler

Invention: `MCPToolContractCompiler`.

Compile safe tool contracts:

- schema;
- auth;
- side effects;
- idempotency;
- approval;
- logging;
- rollback.

This gives agent ecosystems governed access to Kolm.

### W13719 MCP Security Boundary

Invention: `MCPSecurityBoundary`.

Because tool protocols enable code and data access, enforce:

- transport trust;
- tool sandbox;
- credential minimization;
- output sanitization;
- tenant isolation;
- audit logs.

Never market MCP integrations as safe without this boundary.

### W13720 Kubernetes Operator

Invention: `KolmKubernetesOperator`.

Resources:

- KolmArtifact;
- KolmRoute;
- KolmDistillJob;
- KolmEval;
- KolmRuntime;

The operator reconciles desired AI state like cloud-native infrastructure.

### W13721 Terraform BYOC Module

Provision:

- storage;
- compute;
- network;
- IAM;
- secrets;
- monitoring;
- artifact registry.

Output:

- readiness report;
- cost estimate;
- drift detection.

### W13722 BYOC Control Plane Contract

Invention: `BYOCControlPlaneContract`.

Define what Kolm controls and what customer cloud controls:

- keys;
- data;
- models;
- jobs;
- logs;
- telemetry;
- updates.

This prevents enterprise architecture confusion.

### W13723 Integration Test Marketplace

Invention: `IntegrationTestMarketplace`.

- OpenAI migration;

- Snowflake;

- MCP tools.

Each integration carries conformance evidence.

Adapter certification includes:

- API compatibility;
- streaming;

- rate limits;
- cost accounting;
- privacy settings;

- model metadata.

Certified adapters become trustable routing targets.

### W13725 Local Developer Sandbox

- mock gateway;

- local artifact registry;
- sample captures;
- eval fixtures;
- account-like status.

This reduces time-to-aha without requiring cloud setup.

### W13726 Golden Path Generator

Invention: `GoldenPathGenerator`.

Generate project scaffolds:

- API migration;

Each scaffold includes tests and proof commands.

### W13727 Integration Drift Detector

- provider API change;
- SDK behavior change;

- auth change.

Then flag affected routes and docs.

Invention: `DevExperienceKLoop`.

- time to first trust export;

Plugins can add:

- providers;

Rank plugins by:

## Product Operating System, Roadmap Governance, Customer Feedback, Support Intelligence, And Execution Excellence Layer

- wave;

## Document AI, OCR, Layout Understanding, Tables, Forms, Contracts, And Evidence Extraction Layer

Kolm should not treat enterprise documents as plain text. Most regulated AI workflows involve PDFs, scans, tables, signatures, page layout, forms, invoices, claims, contracts, images, and mixed-quality OCR. This layer compiles visual documents into signed, scored, provenance-aware artifacts.

### W13795 Document Evidence Graph

Invention: `DocumentEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- source file;
- page;
- region;
- text span;
- table cell;
- figure;
- signature;
- extraction;
- confidence;
- citation;
- artifact output.

Primary sources:

- LayoutLM: https://arxiv.org/abs/1912.13318
- LayoutLMv3: https://arxiv.org/abs/2204.08387
- Donut OCR-free document understanding: https://arxiv.org/abs/2111.15664
- PaddleOCR docs: https://www.paddleocr.ai/main/en/index/index.html
- Tesseract docs: https://tesseract-ocr.github.io/tessdoc/

### W13796 Layout-Aware Distillation

Invention: `LayoutAwareDistillation`.

Distill teacher behavior with:

- text;
- bounding boxes;
- reading order;
- page region;
- table structure;
- image region;
- document type.

The student learns that "total due" in a footer is different from the same words in boilerplate.

### W13797 OCR Confidence Fusion

Invention: `OCRConfidenceFusion`.

Fuse:

- OCR engine confidence;
- visual token confidence;
- language model correction confidence;
- domain validator confidence;
- human review.

Output:

- extracted value;
- uncertainty;
- review requirement;
- evidence span.

### W13798 OCR-Free Document Student

Invention: `OCRFreeDocumentStudent`.

For poor scans or complex layouts, use OCR-free document understanding:

- page image in;
- structured output out;
- layout preserved;
- evidence regions attached.

Use where OCR introduces more errors than it removes.

### W13799 Table Structure Compiler

Invention: `TableStructureCompiler`.

Extract and validate:

- rows;
- columns;
- headers;
- merged cells;
- footnotes;
- totals;
- units;
- table continuation across pages.

Tables should become typed evidence, not markdown guesses.

### W13800 Contract Clause Extractor

Invention: `ContractClauseExtractor`.

Compile clauses into:

- clause type;
- exact span;
- parties;
- obligation;
- deadline;
- exception;
- risk;
- confidence.

Bind extraction to citations and review workflow.

### W13801 Form Field Grounder

Invention: `FormFieldGrounder`.

For forms:

- detect fields;
- link labels to values;
- identify missing required fields;
- detect checkbox states;
- preserve page coordinates.

This improves claims, onboarding, compliance, and government workflows.

### W13802 Document Redaction Verifier

Invention: `DocumentRedactionVerifier`.

Verify:

- sensitive spans found;
- visual redaction applied;
- hidden text removed;
- metadata scrubbed;
- thumbnails scrubbed;
- OCR layer scrubbed.

PDF redaction must be verified at file level, not just visually.

### W13803 Document Citation Validator

Invention: `DocumentCitationValidator`.

Every generated claim from a document must cite:

- file;
- page;
- region;
- text span;
- extraction method;
- confidence.

If citation is missing or mismatched, downgrade or refuse.

### W13804 Multi-Document Evidence Merger

Invention: `MultiDocumentEvidenceMerger`.

Merge evidence across:

- contract;
- amendment;
- invoice;
- email;
- policy;
- claim file.

Detect contradictions, missing documents, and stale versions.

### W13805 Document Provenance Passport

Invention: `DocumentProvenancePassport`.

For every document:

- source;
- hash;
- version;
- chain of custody;
- OCR engine;
- extraction model;
- redaction status;
- allowed use.

### W13806 Document Workflow Compiler

Invention: `DocumentWorkflowCompiler`.

Compile document tasks:

- intake;
- classify;
- extract;
- validate;
- compare;
- summarize;
- route for approval.

Output a workflow artifact, not a one-off prompt.

### W13807 Document Benchmark Generator

Invention: `DocumentBenchmarkGenerator`.

Generate benchmarks for:

- scan noise;
- handwriting;
- tables;
- rotated pages;
- multi-page forms;
- contracts;
- medical claims;
- low-resource languages.

Separate OCR error from reasoning error.

### W13808 Document Human Review Queue

Invention: `DocumentReviewQueue`.

Queue for review when:

- low OCR confidence;
- high-value field;
- legal obligation;
- PHI/PII;
- conflicting documents;
- missing citation.

This turns human review into targeted quality control.

### W13809 Document Privacy Firewall

Invention: `DocumentPrivacyFirewall`.

Block:

- hidden text leaks;
- image region leaks;
- metadata leaks;
- embedded file leaks;
- OCR cache leaks;
- cross-tenant document reuse.

### W13810 DOCUMENT-K Metric

Invention: `DOCUMENT-K`.

Measure:

- extraction accuracy;
- citation correctness;
- layout fidelity;
- table accuracy;
- redaction verification;
- review efficiency;
- provenance completeness.

### W13811 Document Simulation Harness

Invention: `DocumentHarness`.

Simulate:

- scanned invoice;
- contract with amendment;
- table across pages;
- hidden PDF text;
- rotated form;
- medical claim.

Expected:

- artifact extracts, cites, redacts, or asks for review with evidence.

### W13812 Document Build Sequence

Invention: `DocumentBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Document evidence graph.
2. Document citation validator.
3. OCR confidence fusion.
4. Table structure compiler.
5. Document redaction verifier.
6. Form field grounder.
7. Document workflow compiler.
8. DOCUMENT-K.

### W13813 Document Refusal Policy

Invention: `DocumentRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse or downgrade when:

- OCR confidence too low;
- citation missing;
- redaction unverifiable;
- hidden text present;
- table structure ambiguous;
- chain of custody absent.

### W13814 Document Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- LayoutLM: https://arxiv.org/abs/1912.13318
- LayoutLMv3: https://arxiv.org/abs/2204.08387
- Donut: https://arxiv.org/abs/2111.15664
- PaddleOCR: https://www.paddleocr.ai/main/en/index/index.html
- Tesseract: https://tesseract-ocr.github.io/tessdoc/

Internal evidence:

- document fixtures;
- OCR comparison reports;
- citation validation reports;
- redaction verifier outputs;
- DOCUMENT-K reports.

## Speech, Voice, Meetings, Contact Centers, Audio Events, Speaker Diarization, And Real-Time Conversation Layer

Voice is a production AI surface with high value and high risk. Kolm should compile audio streams into private, cited, speaker-aware artifacts that know what was said, who said it, what was decided, and which parts require review.

### W13815 Voice Evidence Graph

Invention: `VoiceEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- audio file;
- stream segment;
- speaker;
- transcript span;
- confidence;
- language;
- emotion or prosody signal;
- action item;
- consent;
- artifact output.

Primary sources:

- Whisper: https://github.com/openai/whisper
- Whisper paper: https://cdn.openai.com/papers/whisper.pdf
- Mozilla Common Voice: https://www.mozillafoundation.org/en/common-voice/
- OpenTelemetry GenAI conventions: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/

### W13816 Streaming ASR Distillation

Invention: `StreamingASRDistillation`.

Distill speech models for:

- low latency;
- domain vocabulary;
- accents;
- code switching;
- noisy environments;
- edge devices.

Measure partial transcript quality, not only final transcript quality.

### W13817 Speaker-Aware Transcript Compiler

Invention: `SpeakerAwareTranscriptCompiler`.

Compile:

- diarization;
- speaker role;
- turn boundaries;
- interruptions;
- consent markers;
- confidence by segment.

This is required for meetings, healthcare visits, sales calls, and support.

### W13818 Domain Vocabulary Adapter

Invention: `DomainVocabularyAdapter`.

Adapt speech recognition to:

- medical terms;
- legal clauses;
- product names;
- customer names;
- drug names;
- ticket ids.

Use lexicons, context, and post-ASR correction with evidence.

### W13819 Real-Time Conversation Router

Invention: `RealtimeConversationRouter`.

Route audio segments to:

- local ASR;
- cloud ASR;
- domain corrector;
- summarizer;
- escalation detector;
- compliance monitor.

Selection depends on latency, privacy, language, and risk.

### W13820 Meeting Decision Extractor

Invention: `MeetingDecisionExtractor`.

Extract:

- decisions;
- owners;
- deadlines;
- dissent;
- open questions;
- follow-ups.

Each item cites transcript spans and speaker turns.

### W13821 Contact Center Outcome Compiler

Invention: `ContactCenterOutcomeCompiler`.

Compile calls into:

- intent;
- resolution;
- sentiment;
- compliance flags;
- refund or escalation;
- next action;
- customer outcome.

Bind outcome to CRM or ticket workflow.

### W13822 Audio Event Detector

Invention: `AudioEventDetector`.

Detect non-speech events:

- alarm;
- cough;
- crash;
- machine noise;
- silence;
- hold music;
- background speech.

Use cases include field service, healthcare, contact centers, and safety.

### W13823 Voice Privacy Guard

Invention: `VoicePrivacyGuard`.

Protect:

- biometric voice identity;
- sensitive transcript spans;
- background speech;
- accidental participants;
- raw audio retention.

Offer transcript-only, redacted-audio, and local-only modes.

### W13824 Consent-Aware Recording Policy

Invention: `ConsentAwareRecordingPolicy`.

Track:

- jurisdiction;
- participant consent;
- recording notice;
- retention;
- transcript sharing;
- model training permission.

No voice artifact should ignore recording consent.

### W13825 Voice Summarization Verifier

Invention: `VoiceSummaryVerifier`.

Verify summaries against:

- transcript spans;
- speaker attribution;
- time ranges;
- decision statements;
- action items.

Unsupported meeting summaries should downgrade.

### W13826 Multilingual Voice Bridge

Invention: `MultilingualVoiceBridge`.

Support:

- transcription;
- translation;
- bilingual meetings;
- code switching;
- translated summaries.

Keep source transcript and translated claim separate.

### W13827 Voice Edge Runtime

Invention: `VoiceEdgeRuntime`.

Run local voice models for:

- privacy;
- offline field service;
- low latency;
- device control;
- call center edge appliances.

Bind energy, memory, and privacy evidence.

### W13828 Voice Red-Team Corpus

Invention: `VoiceRedTeamCorpus`.

Attack:

- inaudible prompt injection;
- adversarial audio;
- speaker spoofing;
- background instruction;
- transcript poisoning.

Voice security must include the audio channel, not just text.

### W13829 Voice Benchmark Generator

Invention: `VoiceBenchmarkGenerator`.

Generate benchmarks for:

- noisy audio;
- accents;
- domain vocabulary;
- overlapping speakers;
- low bandwidth;
- streaming latency;
- compliance phrases.

### W13830 VOICE-K Metric

Invention: `VOICE-K`.

Measure:

- word error rate;
- speaker attribution;
- summary faithfulness;
- action item precision;
- latency;
- privacy compliance;
- consent enforcement.

### W13831 Voice Simulation Harness

Invention: `VoiceHarness`.

Simulate:

- support call;
- medical visit;
- sales meeting;
- bilingual call;
- noisy field recording;
- adversarial background audio.

Expected:

- transcript, summary, compliance flags, and refusal decisions carry evidence.

### W13832 Voice Build Sequence

Invention: `VoiceBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Voice evidence graph.
2. Speaker-aware transcript compiler.
3. Consent-aware recording policy.
4. Voice summarization verifier.
5. Domain vocabulary adapter.
6. Real-time conversation router.
7. Voice privacy guard.
8. VOICE-K.

### W13833 Voice Refusal Policy

Invention: `VoiceRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse or downgrade when:

- consent absent;
- speaker attribution uncertain;
- domain vocabulary unresolved;
- summary unsupported;
- raw audio retention violates policy;
- adversarial audio detected.

### W13834 Voice Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- Whisper repository: https://github.com/openai/whisper
- Whisper paper: https://cdn.openai.com/papers/whisper.pdf
- Mozilla Common Voice: https://www.mozillafoundation.org/en/common-voice/
- OpenTelemetry GenAI conventions: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/

Internal evidence:

- voice fixtures;
- transcript confidence reports;
- consent policy tests;
- voice red-team reports;
- VOICE-K reports.

## Vision, Image, Video, Streaming Perception, Segmentation, Visual Instruction, And Multimodal Evidence Layer

Vision and video introduce spatial grounding, temporal grounding, visual uncertainty, and safety risks that text-only artifacts cannot handle. Kolm should compile visual perception into evidence-carrying artifacts with bounding boxes, masks, frames, timestamps, and multimodal citations.

### W13835 Visual Evidence Graph

Invention: `VisualEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- image;
- frame;
- region;
- object;
- mask;
- caption;
- question;
- answer;
- confidence;
- artifact output.

Primary sources:

- CLIP: https://openai.com/research/clip
- LLaVA: https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.08485
- Segment Anything: https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.02643
- SAM 2: https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.00714
- Video-LLaVA: https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.10122

### W13836 Visual Instruction Distillation

Invention: `VisualInstructionDistillation`.

Distill vision-language behavior for:

- inspection;
- screenshots;
- forms;
- UI automation;
- medical images;
- field service;
- retail shelf analysis.

Each output must cite visual regions.

### W13837 Visual Grounding Verifier

Invention: `VisualGroundingVerifier`.

Verify that answers point to:

- bounding box;
- mask;
- frame range;
- OCR text;
- visual evidence.

If the model cannot ground an answer, the claim downgrades.

### W13838 Segmentation-Aware Artifact

Invention: `SegmentationAwareArtifact`.

Use segmentation masks as:

- evidence;
- redaction regions;
- object references;
- measurement basis;
- safety zones.

This makes visual reasoning less vague.

### W13839 Video Temporal Evidence Compiler

Invention: `VideoTemporalEvidenceCompiler`.

For video:

- event start;
- event end;
- frame evidence;
- actor;
- object;
- motion;
- confidence.

Video answers need timestamps, not just captions.

### W13840 Streaming Video Monitor

Invention: `StreamingVideoMonitor`.

Run low-latency monitoring for:

- safety event;
- process deviation;
- device issue;
- patient movement;
- field inspection;
- quality defect.

Tie events to runtime assurance.

### W13841 Visual Token Budget Optimizer

Invention: `VisualTokenBudgetOptimizer`.

Choose:

- crop;
- resize;
- frame sample;
- region selection;
- slow-fast video path;
- OCR path.

Goal:

Keep vision cost bounded while preserving evidence.

### W13842 Multi-Image Reasoning Compiler

Invention: `MultiImageReasoningCompiler`.

Reason across:

- before/after images;
- multiple pages;
- multiple camera angles;
- product variants;
- medical series.

Output contradictions and changes with visual evidence.

### W13843 Visual Redaction Firewall

Invention: `VisualRedactionFirewall`.

Redact:

- faces;
- license plates;
- screens;
- documents;
- badges;
- patient identifiers;
- proprietary equipment.

Verify redaction on pixels, metadata, and derived captions.

### W13844 Visual Adversarial Robustness Lab

Invention: `VisualAdversarialLab`.

Attack:

- stickers;
- blur;
- crop;
- low light;
- adversarial patches;
- image prompt injection;
- hidden text.

Visual AI needs robustness evidence.

### W13845 Screenshot Product Understanding

Invention: `ScreenshotProductUnderstanding`.

For developer workflows:

- parse UI screenshot;
- identify controls;
- detect error state;
- map to DOM or app state;
- suggest fix.

This supports product support, QA, and account debugging.

### W13846 Visual Quality Inspection Pack

Invention: `VisualQualityInspectionPack`.

Use cases:

- manufacturing defects;
- packaging;
- shelf compliance;
- document scan quality;
- medical image triage.

Each inspection requires region evidence and false-negative risk.

### W13847 Visual Dataset Consent Guard

Invention: `VisualDatasetConsentGuard`.

Track:

- image rights;
- faces;
- location;
- medical content;
- customer property;
- training permission.

Visual data is often more identifying than text.

### W13848 Visual Benchmark Generator

Invention: `VisualBenchmarkGenerator`.

Generate:

- image QA;
- video QA;
- segmentation;
- OCR+vision;
- multi-image comparison;
- adversarial visual prompts.

### W13849 VISION-K Metric

Invention: `VISION-K`.

Measure:

- answer accuracy;
- grounding correctness;
- segmentation quality;
- temporal localization;
- visual redaction;
- robustness;
- token efficiency.

### W13850 Vision Simulation Harness

Invention: `VisionHarness`.

Simulate:

- screenshot bug;
- inspection defect;
- hidden text injection;
- multi-image contradiction;
- video event;
- face redaction.

Expected:

- artifact cites region, frame, mask, or refuses.

### W13851 Vision Build Sequence

Invention: `VisionBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Visual evidence graph.
2. Visual grounding verifier.
3. Visual redaction firewall.
4. Visual token optimizer.
5. Video temporal evidence compiler.
6. Visual adversarial lab.
7. Visual benchmark generator.
8. VISION-K.

### W13852 Vision Refusal Policy

Invention: `VisionRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse or downgrade when:

- no region evidence;
- low image quality;
- temporal evidence missing;
- redaction unverifiable;
- visual injection detected;
- consent scope missing.

### W13853 Vision Product Surface

Invention: `VisionProductSurface`.

Backend objects:

- visual artifact;
- region evidence;
- segmentation mask;
- frame event;
- visual benchmark;
- visual redaction report;
- VISION-K score.

### W13854 Vision Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- CLIP: https://openai.com/research/clip
- LLaVA: https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.08485
- Segment Anything: https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.02643
- SAM 2: https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.00714
- Video-LLaVA: https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.10122

Internal evidence:

- visual fixtures;
- region citation reports;
- segmentation tests;
- video event reports;
- VISION-K reports.

## Embodied AI, Robotics, Spatial Reasoning, 3D, Sensor Fusion, Field Devices, And Vision-Language-Action Layer

Kolm's "any device" ambition eventually reaches physical systems. Even if robotics is not the first market, the architecture should support embodied and sensor-rich workflows: spatial evidence, action constraints, safety envelopes, and sim-to-real proof.

### W13855 Embodied Evidence Graph

Invention: `EmbodiedEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- observation;
- instruction;
- world state;
- object;
- action;
- policy;
- safety envelope;
- simulator;
- real trace;
- outcome.

Primary sources:

- RT-1: https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.06817
- RT-2: https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.15818
- Open X-Embodiment: https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.08864
- Habitat: https://aihabitat.org/

### W13856 Vision-Language-Action Artifact

Invention: `VLAKolmArtifact`.

Represent:

- perception input;
- language instruction;
- action space;
- safety constraints;
- control rate;
- fallback policy;
- simulator evidence.

This is a physical-world extension of `.kolm`.

### W13857 Spatial Grounding Compiler

Invention: `SpatialGroundingCompiler`.

Compile:

- object location;
- relation;
- affordance;
- zone;
- path;
- forbidden region.

Use for field service, robotics, warehouse, AR, and medical workflows.

### W13858 Sensor Fusion Adapter

Invention: `SensorFusionAdapter`.

Fuse:

- camera;
- depth;
- LiDAR;
- IMU;
- GPS;
- microphone;
- telemetry.

Each sensor has confidence, latency, and failure modes.

### W13859 Action Safety Shield

Invention: `ActionSafetyShield`.

Before action:

- check preconditions;
- check collision risk;
- check zone constraints;
- check human proximity;
- check irreversible effect;
- check fallback.

This extends tool safety to physical actions.

### W13860 Sim-To-Real Evidence Compiler

Invention: `SimToRealEvidenceCompiler`.

Compare:

- simulator scenario;
- real-world trace;
- policy behavior;
- failure mode;
- transfer gap.

Downgrade claims when sim-to-real gap is too large.

### W13861 Embodiment Adapter Registry

Invention: `EmbodimentAdapterRegistry`.

Register device-specific action adapters:

- robot arm;
- drone;
- mobile robot;
- field sensor;
- industrial PLC;
- AR headset.

Each adapter has safety tests and limits.

### W13862 Spatial Memory Map

Invention: `SpatialMemoryMap`.

Store:

- object map;
- region map;
- event map;
- hazard map;
- confidence;
- time validity.

Useful for field service, robotics, inspection, and clinics.

### W13863 Physical World Audit Receipt

Invention: `PhysicalWorldAuditReceipt`.

For an action:

- observation hash;
- policy hash;
- action;
- safety shield status;
- operator approval;
- outcome;
- rollback or recovery.

### W13864 Field Device Command Compiler

Invention: `FieldDeviceCommandCompiler`.

Compile natural-language instructions into safe device commands:

- validate device;
- validate action;
- validate state;
- simulate;
- require approval if risky.

### W13865 Embodied Data Capture Policy

Invention: `EmbodiedCapturePolicy`.

Physical sensors collect sensitive data. Govern:

- bystander privacy;
- location;
- faces;
- worksite secrets;
- safety incidents;
- retention.

### W13866 Spatial Benchmark Generator

Invention: `SpatialBenchmarkGenerator`.

Generate benchmarks:

- object relation;
- navigation;
- manipulation precondition;
- safety zone;
- multi-sensor conflict;
- sim-to-real mismatch.

### W13867 Embodied Runtime Monitor

Invention: `EmbodiedRuntimeMonitor`.

Monitor:

- action latency;
- sensor dropout;
- safety shield triggers;
- unexpected object;
- operator override;
- environment drift.

### W13868 Human-In-The-Loop Action Gate

Invention: `HumanActionGate`.

Require human confirmation for:

- irreversible action;
- safety-critical movement;
- uncertain perception;
- policy conflict;
- novel environment.

### W13869 EMBODIED-K Metric

Invention: `EMBODIED-K`.

Measure:

- spatial grounding;
- action safety;
- sim-to-real transfer;
- sensor reliability;
- audit receipt completeness;
- fallback readiness.

### W13870 Embodied Simulation Harness

Invention: `EmbodiedHarness`.

Simulate:

- warehouse pick;
- field inspection;
- robot arm action;
- drone survey;
- AR instruction;
- sensor dropout.

Expected:

- system acts only inside safety envelope or asks for approval.

### W13871 Embodied Build Sequence

Invention: `EmbodiedBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Embodied evidence graph.
2. Spatial grounding compiler.
3. Sensor fusion adapter.
4. Action safety shield.
5. Sim-to-real evidence compiler.
6. Embodiment adapter registry.
7. Physical audit receipt.
8. EMBODIED-K.

### W13872 Embodied Refusal Policy

Invention: `EmbodiedRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse physical action when:

- perception uncertain;
- safety envelope missing;
- simulator evidence absent;
- human proximity unknown;
- adapter uncertified;
- rollback impossible.

### W13873 Embodied Product Surface

Invention: `EmbodiedProductSurface`.

Backend objects:

- world state;
- spatial map;
- action artifact;
- sensor fusion report;
- safety shield report;
- physical audit receipt;
- EMBODIED-K score.

### W13874 Embodied Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- RT-1: https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.06817
- RT-2: https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.15818
- Open X-Embodiment: https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.08864
- Habitat: https://aihabitat.org/

Internal evidence:

- spatial fixtures;
- action shield tests;
- sensor fusion reports;
- sim-to-real reports;
- EMBODIED-K reports.

## Time Series, Tabular, Forecasting, Anomaly Detection, Sensor Streams, And Operational Intelligence Layer

Many enterprise AI problems are not chat or documents. They are time series, tabular records, logs, metrics, telemetry, fraud signals, supply chain data, and operational anomalies. Kolm should compile those into artifacts with forecasts, causal context, confidence, and action policies.

### W13875 Temporal Evidence Graph

Invention: `TemporalEvidenceGraph`.

Track:

- time series;
- timestamp;
- entity;
- feature;
- event;
- forecast;
- anomaly;
- decision;
- outcome.

Primary sources:

- Chronos time-series foundation model: https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.07815
- StatsForecast: https://github.com/Nixtla/statsforecast
- OpenTelemetry: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/
- OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/

### W13876 Time-Series Artifact Compiler

Invention: `TimeSeriesArtifactCompiler`.

Compile:

- forecasting model;
- anomaly detector;
- feature transforms;
- seasonality;
- exogenous variables;
- confidence interval;
- action policy.

The output should be auditable like any other `.kolm` artifact.

### W13877 Forecasting Route Policy

Invention: `ForecastingRoutePolicy`.

Choose:

- statistical model;
- foundation time-series model;
- customer-specific model;
- human review;
- no forecast.

Based on history length, seasonality, drift, and business risk.

### W13878 Anomaly-To-Action Compiler

Invention: `AnomalyToActionCompiler`.

Convert anomalies into:

- explainable alert;
- probable cause;
- impacted service;
- recommended action;
- rollback or escalation.

This links time-series AI to operations.

### W13879 Sensor Stream Distillation

Invention: `SensorStreamDistillation`.

Distill models for:

- IoT telemetry;
- machine vibration;
- device health;
- app traces;
- medical sensors;
- fleet signals.

Use edge runtime and drift monitors.

### W13880 Tabular Foundation Model Gate

Invention: `TabularFoundationGate`.

Do not use a generic LLM for tabular prediction by default. Gate by:

- data volume;
- feature types;
- leakage risk;
- calibration;
- interpretability;
- baseline strength.

Often gradient boosting or statistical models are better.

### W13881 Probabilistic Forecast Passport

Invention: `ForecastPassport`.

For every forecast:

- horizon;
- confidence interval;
- training window;
- covariates;
- backtest;
- drift status;
- action threshold.

### W13882 Operational Anomaly Benchmark

Invention: `OperationalAnomalyBenchmark`.

Benchmark:

- service latency spikes;
- error rate spikes;
- cost anomalies;
- device telemetry failures;
- demand shifts;
- fraud bursts.

### W13883 Temporal Causal Attribution

Invention: `TemporalCausalAttribution`.

For anomalies and metric moves, estimate causes:

- deploy;
- traffic change;
- provider outage;
- customer segment;
- model update;
- data drift.

This prevents alert noise.

### W13884 Alert Fatigue Guard

Invention: `AlertFatigueGuard`.

Control alerts by:

- severity;
- novelty;
- actionability;
- duplicate suppression;
- owner;
- expected value.

AI observability should reduce noise, not generate it.

### W13885 Time-Series Feature Store

Invention: `TemporalFeatureStore`.

Store:

- rolling windows;
- lags;
- event counts;
- seasonality features;
- entity history;
- online/offline parity.

Bind feature versions to artifacts.

### W13886 Real-Time Telemetry Router

Invention: `TelemetryRouter`.

Route streams to:

- anomaly detector;
- forecast model;
- cost monitor;
- safety monitor;
- storage;
- human on-call.

Based on tenant, severity, and SLO.

### W13887 Metric Forecast For Capacity

Invention: `CapacityForecastArtifact`.

Forecast:

- API traffic;
- GPU demand;
- storage;
- proof jobs;
- support tickets;
- enterprise usage.

Feed economics and capacity planning.

### W13888 Temporal Data Quality Monitor

Invention: `TemporalDataQualityMonitor`.

Detect:

- missing time buckets;
- duplicate timestamps;
- clock skew;
- delayed data;
- sensor dropout;
- unit changes.

Bad time-series data should block forecasts.

### W13889 TEMPORAL-K Metric

Invention: `TEMPORAL-K`.

Measure:

- forecast accuracy;
- interval calibration;
- anomaly precision;
- actionability;
- drift handling;
- data quality;
- alert fatigue reduction.

### W13890 Temporal Simulation Harness

Invention: `TemporalHarness`.

Simulate:

- seasonal demand;
- sudden outage;
- sensor drift;
- delayed events;
- cost spike;
- model update regression.

Expected:

- artifact forecasts, flags anomaly, attributes cause, or refuses.

### W13891 Temporal Build Sequence

Invention: `TemporalBuildPlan`.

Implementation order:

1. Temporal evidence graph.
2. Forecast passport.
3. Temporal data quality monitor.
4. Anomaly-to-action compiler.
5. Temporal feature store.
6. Forecasting route policy.
7. Temporal causal attribution.
8. TEMPORAL-K.

### W13892 Temporal Refusal Policy

Invention: `TemporalRefusalPolicy`.

Refuse or downgrade when:

- history insufficient;
- data quality fails;
- drift unhandled;
- interval calibration poor;
- anomaly not actionable;
- causal attribution unsupported.

### W13893 Temporal Product Surface

Invention: `TemporalProductSurface`.

Backend objects:

- time-series artifact;
- forecast passport;
- anomaly event;
- temporal feature view;
- capacity forecast;
- TEMPORAL-K score.

### W13894 Temporal Sources And Evidence

Primary sources:

- Chronos: https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.07815
- StatsForecast: https://github.com/Nixtla/statsforecast
- OpenTelemetry docs: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/
- OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/
